features.name: Swift Response
features.description: Beginning at 1st level, you learn to react with swift and decisive action in combat. You ignore difficult terrain and you gain a rank of Mastery in Initiative. You gain another rank of Mastery at 9th level.

features.name: Lethal Precision
features.description: Also, at 1st level, Select one between Taijutsu & Bukijutsu. You can cast the chosen Jutsu type using Dexterity in place of Strength for all calculations. You cannot switch this choice later.

features.name: Lethal Attack
features.description:
Finally, at 1st level, you know how to exploit a foes distraction, drop in guard, and moment of hesitation. Once per turn, you can deal extra damage to a creature you score a successful attack against. This extra damage is known as a Lethal Attack and is represented as a number of d8's equal to what is seen in the lethal attack column of the Hunter-Nin Class Table. This extra damage can only be triggered if one of the following is true:
• You have advantage on the attack roll.
• One of your allies is within 5 feet of it.
The type of attack that triggers this feature determines the number of dice rolled. If it is a weapon attack, you only roll half the listed amount, rounded down to a minimum of one.

features.name: Cunning Action
features.description:
Beginning, at 2nd level, your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. When you would attempt to Conceal an object, Hide or Sneak you gain a +2 bonus to your Dexterity (Stealth) check made to accomplish those tasks.
Also, while sneaking you can move your full movement speed and you can choose to use your Dexterity (Stealth) check result from your Hide attempt instead of making another check each time you would sneak.
Finally, as a bonus action, you can take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

features.name: Primary Target
features.description:
Finally, at 2nd level, you have experience researching, tracking, and hunting. You can effectively read and track any creature you come across. Once when rolling initiative, you can select any creature you can see within 60 feet and mark it as your Primary Target at no action cost. You can then spend a bonus action to mark another creature you can see within range. You can only have one creature marked as your Primary Target at a time.
Creatures remain marked until they reach 0 Hp, or you mark another creature. While marked, you gain the following benefits;
• You have Advantage on Wisdom checks made to track or search for your Primary Target so long as they are within the same countries borders as you.
• You become aware of one of the following. You cannot gain more information about a creature in this way until you complete a Short rest and reapply;
  o One damage immunity they have.
  o One damage resistance they have.
  o Their current Damage reduction value.

features.name: Hunter Creed
features.description: Beginning at 3rd level you begin to follow a Creed of the Hunt, enabling you to shape your skillset further. Your Creed grants you features at 3rd, 7th, 10th, 14th and 17th levels.

features.name: Hunter's Exploits
features.description:
Also, at 3rd level you learn to exploit your skills to the betterment of your hunt. You learn two exploits, as detailed at the end of this class description. You learn an additional exploit at 10th and 17th level in this class.
You can switch a learned exploit when you would complete a long rest.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Uncanny Dodge
features.description: Starting at 5th level, when a creature that you can see would deal damage to you, you can use your reaction to halve the triggering attack, jutsu or effect, damage against you.

features.name: Mastery
features.description:
Also at 5th level, choose any one skill or tool kit that you have proficiency in. You gain a rank of mastery in the chosen skill or tool kit.
When you would reach 10th and 15th levels, you can select one more proficiency (in skills or tools) to gain a Rank of mastery for as well.

features.name: Hunted Target
features.description:
Beginning at 6th level, a creature marked as your Primary Target, is counted as Hunted against you.
Hunted. A Hunted creature is one who is being stalked by you and is on their last leg. When a hunted creature would cast a Jutsu that requires HS (Hand Signs), or Stand from prone they trigger an attack of opportunity from you.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Swift Response (upgrade)
features.description: You gain another rank of Mastery at 9th level.

features.name: Hunter's Exploits (2)
features.description: You learn an additional exploit at 10th and 17th level in this class.

features.name: Mastery (2)
features.description: When you would reach 10th and 15th levels, you can select one more proficiency (in skills or tools) to gain a Rank of mastery for as well.

features.name: Defensive Tactics
features.description:
Beginning at 11th Level, you learn how to fight defensively against your targets, leading them into a false sense of security in confrontations against you. You gain one of the following features of your choice You gain a second choice at 17th level.
• Escaping Danger: Attacks of opportunity and attacks made as a result of a creature's reaction against you are made at disadvantage.
• Unbroken will: You have advantage on saving throws to resist any Mental or Sensory Condition.
• Evasion: When you are subjected to an effect, that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on a saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

features.name: Hunted Target (2) — Hunter's Strike
features.description:
Beginning at 11th level, you gain a special reaction called Hunters Strike.
Hunters Strike. This special reaction can only be used to make an attack of opportunity.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Elusive
features.description: Beginning at 13th Level, you are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No Attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

features.name: Mastery (3)
features.description: When you would reach 10th and 15th levels, you can select one more proficiency (in skills or tools) to gain a Rank of mastery for as well.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Hunter's Exploits (3)
features.description: You learn an additional exploit at 10th and 17th level in this class.

features.name: Defensive Tactics (2)
features.description: You gain a second choice at 17th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Assassinate
features.description: At 20th level, you have mastered the art of Hunting, tracking, and assassinating targets. You can trigger Lethal attack against up to two creatures per turn, once each.

subclasses.name: Blade Warden
subclasses.description: Hunter-Nin who seek to master weapons and remove foes from the equation. Specialized fighting techniques for use against the direst threats, from an onslaught of enemies to towering brutes.

features.name: Warden's Proficiency
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd Level, you gain proficiency in the Athletics and Intimidation Skills. You can make any checks with the aforementioned skill using Strength or Dexterity. You also gain proficiency in all Weapons from the Blade group.
Also, you have learned to fully invest into a singular weapon designed to best capitalize on your free-flowing form of combat. Select one weapon type that you have proficiency in, that does not have the Two-Handed or Heavy properties. This weapon is hereby treated as your Warden Weapon. A Warden Weapon gains a new weapon property unique to it from the Warden Weapon Property Table. You can switch your Warden Weapon Property when you complete a rest of any type. You can select a second property when you reach 10th level;

features.name: Blade's Prey
features.description:
Also at 3rd Level, your tenacity can wear down the most fortuitous foes. You can trigger Lethal Attack with weapon attacks made with your Warden Weapon twice per turn against the same creature.
You also gain the Wardens Assault, Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit known limit.

features.name: Aggressive Attack
features.description:
Starting at 7th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
Additionally, When you would roll initiative, you can choose to use your Intimidation Bonus as your Initiative bonus.

features.name: Warden's Presence
features.description:
Starting at 7th level, your presence has become passively intimidating to all creatures who you are not allied with, emanating an intimidating aura.
While you are not in combat, whenever you would interact with a creature and make a Deception or Persuasion check, you can choose to instead make an Intimidation check in its place. Creatures who would attempt a Wisdom or Charisma based check against you or a creature allied with you suffer a -2 penalty to their results.

features.name: Blade's Aggression
features.description: Beginning at 10th level, When you hit a creature with a weapon attack using your Warden Weapon, and you would attempt to use a Combat Exploit that forgo's two or more Lethal Attack die, you reduce the number of dice forgone by 1.

features.name: Warden's Retaliation
features.description: Beginning at 14th level, you gain the ability to counterattack when your target tries to sabotage you. If your Primary Target forces you to make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack against them. This attack is made before the resolution of the saving throw. On a successful attack, you gain advantage on the saving throw.

features.name: Superior Offense
features.description: At 17th level, you are so accurate that attacks rarely rely on sight if at all and cannot be diverted. You gain 30 feet of blindsight and attacks you make cannot be made at disadvantage or Jutsu based penalties.

subclasses.name: Necrotic Hand
subclasses.description: Hunters who follow an offensive Medical teaching, using necrosis to fight off enemies with cold-eyed, surgical efficiency.

features.name: Medical Proficiency
features.description:
At 3rd level, you gain proficiency in Medicine and Medicine Kits. You can make any checks using the aforementioned skill and Toolkit with Intelligence or Wisdom.
You take the teachings of your village's medical corps, your direct master or found medical documents and blend it into your techniques. You can learn and add Jutsu with the Medical Keyword to your known jutsu list.
Your unique fighting style has taught you to trigger your Lethal Attack feature against creatures with ranks of Envenomed or Corroded.
Additionally, you gain the Festering Siphonage Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit Limit.

features.name: Decaying Compound
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your skills with medicine, poisons and lethal chemicals are brought with you on every mission you take. You begin with a cartridge of ampoules containing different volatile and lethal chemicals called your Black Medicine Array. Your Black Medicine Array carries 4 ampoules, which are refreshed when you complete a rest of any type.
You can spend an ampoule to either Infuse it with a Jutsu you cast or to Putrefy a weapon you are wielding.
Infuse. When you cast a jutsu that deals Poison or Necrotic damage and forces a saving throw, as part of the same action used to cast the chosen jutsu, you smash the ampoule releasing the chemical, enhancing the jutsu cast with an effect from the Decaying Compound.
Putrefy. As a bonus action, you smash an ampoule on your weapon, covering it in a black liquid that radiates like a mist off of the weapon designed to destabilize a creature's cellular structure. Your weapon/ammunition remains putrefied for 1 minute. While putrefied, your weapon deals either necrotic or poison damage and once per turn when your weapon or Taijutsu attack result is 5 or higher than the targets AC you can inflict one of the effects from the Decaying Compound table.

features.name: Anatomical Studies
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level, your refined eye for anatomy allows you to analyze a targets physiology in real time, striking with uncanny precision, compromising a foes defense with surgical efficiency.
When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may choose to forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack dice.
If you forgo 1 Lethal Attack dice, the damaged creature gains 1 rank of Weakened.
If you forgo 2 Lethal Attack dice, the damaged creature makes their next saving throw against a creature other than yourself at disadvantage, before the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Clinical Appraisal
features.description:
Also, at 7th level, your medical knowledge extends beyond simple anatomy, you can see through the subtleties of body language, stress and chemical influence. You can speak in poisons and toxins and read people like an open scroll. You can use Medicine in place of Insight when using the See Through lies or Sense Motive skill actions.
If you spend at least 1 minute observing or interacting with a creature, you can attempt to make a contested Medicine or Perception check vs the targets Deception. On a success, you becomes aware if they are suffering from a Condition or disease and one of the following;
• If they are under the influence of chemical or drug.
• Determine their Clan or heritage.
•  Determine if the creatures Vulnerability, if any.

features.name: Twisted Medicine
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, you learn to make use of your medical mastery to make your Black medicine Array beneficial for your allies while being even more harmful to your enemies. You learn to refresh 2 Ampoules of your Black Medicine Array by spending 10 minutes working on it and you learn to use both the Rejuvenation and Suppression
Rejuvenation. As a bonus action, you can feed an ampoule to a willing creature you can touch. A creature that consumes the Ampule tastes an extremely bitter concoction but regain 4d10 hit points. A creature can only regain hit points in this way, once per short rest.
Suppression. As an Action, you throw an ampoule as a point you can see within 30 feet shattering and creating a noxious mist. This mist fills a 20-foot radius centering on the chosen point. All creatures in the affected area must make a Constitution saving throw vs a DC 19 or your Ninjutsu save DC, whichever is higher. On a failure affected creatures become Suppressed.

features.name: Mortal Wound
features.description: Also, at 14th level, a creature with a Physical or Mental condition becomes exceedingly obvious to you. They begin to glow a soft red color that only you can see. You gain Blind Sight against such a creature so long as they are within 60 feet of you, making it difficult for them to hide.

features.name: Dr. Death
features.description: Beginning at 17th level, your medical assassination talents are legendary. D-Rank Jutsu with the Medical Keyword that make an attack that you cast, cost 0 Chakra. C-Rank jutsu with the Medical Keyword that make an attack that you cast, cost 5 Chakra.

subclasses.name: Grave Stalker
subclasses.description: Consumed by the thrill of the hunt, these Hunter-Nin become whispers in the dark — hunting via camouflage, infiltration, and shadow-strikes.

features.name: Stalker's Proficiency
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level, you become a whisper in the dark, a shadow between breaths. Trained in unseen warfare. You do not chase prey. You haunt them. This training grants you proficiency in Sleight of Hand and Deception. You can make any checks using these skills with Dexterity or Charisma.
Your training in the tactics used by the Anbu Black-Ops and Corpse Hunters allows you to perceive and manipulate the battlefield in ways others cannot. You gain 20ft of Blindsight, allowing you to detect movement when your eyes fail you. Moreover, while in dim light or darkness, your lack of useless movement renders you imperceptible to those relying on Blindsight or Tremor sense, masking you entirely from such senses.
Also, your training allows you to take your Primary Target and mark them with deeper and more insidious intent, as they become Stalked. A Stalked creature suffers a -2 penalty to Perception or Insight checks made against you. If a Stalked creature is isolated (no allied creatures within 10 feet of them) and you use Lethal Attack against them, you roll +1 Lethal Attack Dice.
The core of this fighting style has taught you to trigger your Lethal Attack against creatures with ranks of Demoralized or Dazzled.
Finally, you gain the Shadow Step Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit Limit.

features.name: Shadow Stalker
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you do not simply just carry weapons. You become them. From the void of shadow and fear, your intent takes form, the shape of your will and intent, cold and merciless.
Through a fusion of misdirection, chakra manipulation and killing instinct, you have learned to shape the intangible into instruments of death. You gain access to a reservoir of specifically chakra weaponry called your Dread Arsenal. You possess a number of Arsenal charges equal to your proficiency bonus, which all replenish when you complete a long rest. You can also replenish up to two charges when you complete a short rest. As a bonus action, you can spend an Arsenal charge to summon your Dread Weapon, a chakra construct weapon of your description that last for 1 minute or until incapacitated. Dread Weapons are valid W(Weapon) components for Shinobi Basics, and Blade weapon group Bukijutsu. Dread Weapons use a d6 damage die, deals psychic or necrotic damage (your choice when summoned) and is treated as a weapon with the Finesse and Light weapon properties.

features.name: Marked for Death
features.description: Beginning, at 7th level, Whenever you cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu that requires a creature to make a Saving throw while you are wielding your Dread Weapon, you may spend 1 Arsenal charge to impose a -2 penalty to the save. If the target is Stalked by you, the target instead makes their save at disadvantage.

features.name: Master Ambusher
features.description:
Starting at 7th level, you excel at leading ambushes, both socially and in combat. You can use Smoke Bomb's as a bonus action. When you and other allied creatures would begin combat while hidden or unseen, you can choose to use your Stealth bonus in place of both; your own and each allied creature's initiative. If you begin combat while out of stealth, you have advantage on initiative rolls.
When you or an allied creature within 30 feet are engaged in a social encounter and would fail a Charisma based check, you can interject, creating a new circumstance, situation or lie throwing the person you are speaking to off. Make a contested check using your Deception vs their Insight. On a success, you shift the conversation, rendering the previous failure as if it never occurred as they are overwhelmed with new information. You can attempt to interject socially in this way, once every 10 minutes.

features.name: One with the Darkness
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, you've become adept at evading creatures that rely on their senses, regardless of what they are. Your blindsight increases to 30 feet. While you are in Dim light or Darkness, your ability to remain undetected increases as you become imperceptible to those that rely on Chakra sight and Dark vision.
When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may choose to forgo 1 Lethal Attack die, inflicting 2 ranks of Dazzled on the damaged creature as you strike at their eyes.

features.name: A Walking Shadow
features.description:
Starting at 14th level, simply being in the darkness is enough to give you an edge above other combatants while you are hidden from a creature Stalked by you, you gain a +2 bonus to your critical threat range.
Additionally, you are able perform either a Fade Step or Silent Execution.
Fade Step. As a bonus action, by spending 1 Arsenal charge you can select one creature you can see within 30 that is producing a shadow of any type. You teleport into the same space as the target. If you end your turn in the same space as them you are forced back into a space within 5 feet that can hold you.
Silent Execution. When you reduce a creature's hit points to 0, you can spend 1 Arsenal charge, gaining 30 feet of movement and can make 1 additional weapon attack this turn using your Dread Weapon. If attacking another creature, you can trigger Lethal Attack again against the new creature. You can use this ability once per turn.

features.name: Shadow Assassination
features.description: Starting at 17th level, if you would make an attack against a creature Stalked by you while they are in Darkness, roll 1 additional d20 as part of your attack roll, taking the higher result. If you have Disadvantage, you instead take the result with neither the higher or lowest value.

subclasses.name: Undertaker
subclasses.description: Brutal, imposing, impossible to ignore — relentless juggernauts trained to break bones, crush morale, and shatter resistance.

features.name: Brutal Proficiency
features.description:
At 3rd level, you are not just a hunter trained for assassination or elegance. You are a warrior bred for brutality and mercilessness. You gain proficiency in Athletics and Demolitions Kits. You can make checks using the aforementioned kits and skills with Strength or Dexterity.
You learn to take the teachings of the Corps Hunters, The Black Root and Golden Horn Forces and apply them to your combat technique. This teaching has enabled you to move faster gaining a +10 bonus to your movement speed and the Great Weapon Stance Bukijutsu Stance. The core of this fighting style has taught you to trigger your Lethal Attack against creatures with ranks of Bruised or Weakened.
Finally, you gain the Savage Attack Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit Lim

features.name: Carnage
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your presence on the battlefield carries with it, the acknowledgement that carnage will follow. When you would roll initiative, you enter a battle state known as Executioners Drive which lasts until the end of an encounter. While in Executioners Drive, you gain access to Carnage.
Carnage. Once per turn, when you deal or take damage from a creature you gain 1 Carnage, with a max of 3 Carnage you can maintain. You can spend Carnage on the following abilities.
• Guillotine Cleave. When you make a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 Carnage. All creatures in your weapons range becomes targets for this attack. If you would trigger Lethal Attack against one of the target creatures, you apply it to all creatures you hit.
• Butchers Momentum. When you score a hit with an attack you make, you can spend 1 Carnage to gain 30 feet of bonus movement. At the end of this movement, you can make 1 Weapon attack against a creature in range.
•  Casket Breaker. When you score a hit with an attack and your deal less damage to a creature's hit points than your rolled result you can spend 1 Carnage to make 1 weapon attack against the same creature. This attack ignores up to 6 DR (Damage Reduction) the target has.

features.name: Savage Inflection
features.description: Beginning at 7th level, You've become frighteningly precise in your brutality. Each wound you carve just tells you where next to strike. Once per turn, when you spend a Carnage, you can reduce the Lethal Damage Dice reduction needed for a Combat Exploit by 1.

features.name: Unspoken Threat
features.description: Also at 7th level, raising your voice is unnecessary. Your presence, scars and eyes do the talking. Your stare makes others shift in their shoes. Others find it difficult to even push you or your allies socially. When a non-hostile creature would attempt to make a Charisma or Wisdom check against you or your allies, you gain 1 Social Carnage (Max 1), which expires after 1 minute. When you or your allies would respond with a Charisma or Wisdom check of your own, you can spend this Social Carnage giving advantage on the roll.

features.name: Weight of Violence
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, you are recognized as a thunderstorm of violence that stifles motion and silences cowardice. You gain the following extra options to spend Carnage on.
• Demonic Aura. At the beginning of your turn, you can spend 2 Carnage and release a demonic pulse manifesting a chakra image of your description. Until the end of your next turn, creatures who would make an attack or cast a jutsu targeting you, suffer the effects of your Lethal Attack if you strike them even if you reached Lethal Attacks use limit for the turn.
• Murderous Aura. At the beginning of your turn, you can spend 2 Carnage and release a murderous pulse manifesting a chakra image of your description. Creatures within 15 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC, on a failed save they gain 2 ranks of Demoralized.

features.name: The Butcher's Promise
features.description: Starting at 14th level, a creature marked as your Primary Target has your intent bound to them, like a predator hunting its prey. When a marked creature would trigger an attack of opportunity, you make the attack at advantage. If you are using your Hunters Strike reaction, on a hit, the target must make a Strength saving throw vs your Taijutsu Save DC. On a failed save, they are knocked prone. If they were already prone and attempting to stand, they remain prone.

features.name: Harvest
features.description:
Beginning at 17th level, when your carnage is at its peak, you embody a walking calamity. With enough savagery you unleash a devastating finisher that turns the tide of battle, for better or for worse. You gain the following option to spend Carnage on.
Red Harvest. When you deal [Weapon Damage], you can spend 3 Carnage as part of the damage dealt. You treat the [Weapon Damage] roll as its maximum possible value, you trigger Lethal Attack ignoring its requirements and the affected creature must make a Constitution saving throw vs the triggering attack/damage types save DC (Ninjutsu, Taijutsu, Genjutsu, if Weapon then Taijutsu). On a success, the affected creature is only knocked prone.
On a failed save, the affected creature is Staggered, has disadvantage on its next Saving throw from a creature other than yourself, and is knocked prone.

subclasses.name: Void Walker
subclasses.description: Hunters who weaponize Time & Space ninjutsu, specializing in hit-and-run tactics — teleporting in and out of combat as easily as blinking.

features.name: Void Proficiency
features.description:
When you choose this Creed beginning at 3rd level, you've learned to hunt prey through invisible lines of space and presence, reading the battlefield as others read a scroll. You gain proficiency in the Ninshou skill and when crafting Ninjutsu Scrolls, you reduce the downtime cost by 2. If this would reduce the downtime cost to 0, you can craft them over the course of a long rest. You can make a check using Ninshou using Dexterity or Intelligence.
Your awareness is not limited to the physical, it bleeds into the spatial. Whenever you or another creature you can see teleports within 10 feet of one another, you can trigger your Lethal Attack feature against them ignoring other requirements. The core of this fighting style has taught you to trigger your Lethal Attack against creatures with ranks of Sealed.
Finally, you gain the Void Step Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit Limit.

features.name: Void Anchor
features.description:
Learning this creed at 3rd level also has enabled you to imprint chakra seals on a space, person or object, Tethering yourself to the marked places. These unique chakra seals are known as a Void Anchor. As a bonus action, you can place a Void Anchor on;
• A creature you can see within 60 feet.
• A surface or unattended object you can see within 60 feet. If the object is moved, the seal dispels itself.
You can maintain up to 4 Anchors at any given time, and each last for 1 minute. If you would place an Anchor while you have the maximum number you can maintain, the oldest Anchor dissipates as the new one manifests.
These Anchors are more than simple seals. They are you specially designed marks of execution. If you would teleport within 10 feet of a creature marked with an Anchor, you can make a Weapon Attack against them as a Bonus Action.
When you trigger Lethal Attack against a creature with one of your marks, you can forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack die to use either Flash Step or Displacement Strike.
Flash Step. When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may choose to forgo 1 Lethal Attack die, teleporting up to 30 Feet to an unoccupied space that you can see.
Displacement Strike. When you deal Lethal Attack damage, you may choose to forgo 2 Lethal Attack die to force the target to make a Strength Saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Taijutsu, your choice) Save DC. On a failed save, they are teleported to an unoccupied space or adjacent to another creature where you have another Void Anchor placed.

features.name: Void Sense
features.description: Beginning at 7th level, your technique with Anchors has taught you to be one for your team. Anchors you have placed while not in combat, last for 10 minutes and you can perceive both visually and auditorily through them. Activities within 30 feet of the Anchors can be perceived in this way by you as if you are there. Additionally, if a chakra or jutsu based effect is within 30 feet of an Anchor the Anchor radiates a small hum that only you can perceive, informing you where the effect originates from.

features.name: Vorpal Strike
features.description:
Also at 7th level, your skill with spatial seals allows you to layer them, creating new modes and methods of assault. When you would mark a creature with an Anchor, you can choose to layer them. A creature with a Layered Anchor counts as having two active Anchors, allowing you to use Vorpal Passthrough or Reverse Summoning.
Vorpal Passthrough. When you cast a jutsu that requires an attack roll, or when you take the Attack action, you may teleport adjacent to the double-anchored creature as part of that action.
Reverse Summoning. As an Action, you can attempt to forcefully summon the creature to a space within 5 feet of you that can hold it. The target must make a Strength saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Taijutsu, your choice) save DC. On a failure, they are summoned to an adjacent space as both seals are dispelled. On a success, one Anchor is broken, leaving one Anchor remaining.

features.name: Sealing Arts
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, your study of spatial fuinjutsu has unlocked forbidden sealing techniques. Through careful anchoring and spatial analysis, you can collapse ongoing chakra-based effects by binding them in place and dispelling them altogether.
When you are within 60 feet of a creature or chakra or jutsu based effect that you can see or are aware of, you can attempt to seal it by placing 3 Anchors on or near the creature or effect as an Action. As part of the action used to place the Anchors, you must make a Ninshou check vs the effects Save DC. If the effect is a non-jutsu based phenomenon, the GM determines its Rank, or its Rank defaults to the Rank of the mission determining its DC (D-Rank: 10, C-Rank: 14, B-Rank: 18, A-Rank: 22, S-Rank: 26). On a success, you seal the effect, effectively dispelling it. Once used, you cannot use this feature again for 10 minutes.

features.name: Vorpal Blade
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, you learn to infuse your Void Anchors into your weapons. By marking one weapon with your Void Anchor, when you would trigger Lethal Attack against your target, your attack bypasses any structure, construct or barrier that would intercept the damage dealt.
Additionally, if you score a critical hit with an Anchored weapon you can use either Flash Step or Displacement strike as part of the chosen attack without forgoing Lethal Attack dice.

features.name: Vorpal Technique
features.description:
At 17th level, you become a master of spatial destruction and a blur on the battlefield. A flash of color before everything in your path suffers a rending strike.
As an Action, you unleash the ultimate vorpal technique, connecting all of your Void Anchors together in a web. You teleport to each anchor at the same time. All creatures within 10 feet of each anchor of your choice becomes a target for this assault. Make a single Melee Taijutsu or Ninjutsu attack, comparing the result against the AC of each creature. On a hit, you deal your [Weapons Damage] (or [Unarmed Damage] if no weapon is equipped) and trigger Lethal Attack against all creatures hit. If you score a critical hit, only apply it to a single creature.
After using this feature, all Void Anchors you currently have active are dispelled and you reappear in a space of your choice within 30 feet of one of the affected creatures.

subclasses.name: Wolves Legacy
subclasses.description: Forged by surviving hardship — scarred and reconstructed, these operatives carry prosthetic gauntlets housing deadly ninja tools and fight by reading their enemies' rhythm.

features.name: Wolf's Proficiency
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level, your training wasn't conventional, it was survival and your master was pain. You gain proficiency in Martial Arts and Survival. You can make checks using the aforementioned skills with Strength or Dexterity.
You learn to take the teachings of the Starving Wolves and Poaching Owls, applying them to your technique for survival. Your technique involves reading your enemies rhythm, anticipating their next move, and striking when they falter. This rhythm is represented within you as Posture. Posture is used to maintain your own defense, but also break through an enemy's guard. Once per turn, whenever you successfully reduce or resist damage from a hostile creature using a Class feature or jutsu you cast as a reaction, you gain 1 Posture (Max 3). You can maintain Posture for up to 1 minute. With or without Posture you can use Deflect, Parry, or Retaliate.
• Deflect. As a reaction to taking damage you can reduce the triggering damage by 4. By spending 1 Posture you instead reduce the triggering damage by 10.
• Parry. As a reaction to a creature scoring a critical hit against you, you can gain resistance to the triggering damage. By spending 1 Posture, you instead gain resistance to all damage until the beginning of your next turn.
• Retaliate. As a reaction to taking damage, you can take the attack action, targeting the creature who dealt the damage to you so long as they are within your weapons range. By spending 1 Posture, this reaction attack does not consume your Reaction.
The core of this fighting style has taught you to trigger your Lethal Attack against Dazed or Confused.
Finally, you gain the Posture Break Hunter Exploit. This does not count against your Exploit Limit.

features.name: Shinobi's Prosthetic
features.description: Also, at 3rd level, you have equipped yourself with a specially crafted prosthetic arm or gauntlet of your design and description. This prosthetic is fitted with hidden compartments that store different trick tools and weapons. This prosthetic begins with 2 Prosthetic Slots. Each prosthetic slot can be fitted with a Prosthetic Tool. You select two tools at 3rd level, and can select one additional tool at 7th, 10th and 14th levels. If your Prosthetic Tool requires an attack or Saving throw, you use your Taijutsu or Ninjutsu Attack/Save DC, your choice. Using your prosthetic tools can over work and strain the prosthetic as a whole, limiting you to being able to use any tool within your prosthetic no more than 4 times every 10 minutes.

features.name: Shinobi's Flow State
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level, your body has adapted to the tempo of conflict. Every moment is a reaction, and every reaction flows into your next movement. Each ability granted by Wolf's Proficiency gains a further enhanced option.
• Deflect. By spending 2 Posture you reduce the triggering and all subsequent damage by 10 until the end of the current turn.
• Parry. By spending 2 Posture, the resistance lasts until the beginning of your next turn and can be applied to a willing allied creature within your weapons range.
• Retaliate. By spending 2 Posture, instead of taking the Attack action you can cast a Bukijutsu you know with a casting time of 1 Action. Casting a jutsu in this way does consume your reaction.

features.name: Eyes of a Shinobi
features.description:
Also at 7th level, you have tapped into a latent skill that has been lost to most shinobi. The ability to sense a creature's presence, even those unseen or unbound by a physical body. You gain a unique special sense known as Shinobi's Awareness.
Shinobi's Awareness. As a bonus action you can make a Survival Check using your honed preternatural senses. For the next minute, you become aware if a creature is observing or targeting you, even if they are invisible, hidden or doing so through extended means (Camera's, special senses, or jutsu based.) Additionally, all creatures within 60 feet of you that are holding weapons, preparing to attack or experiencing combat induced stress or anxiety alert this sense. This does not reveal their location, but gives you awareness of their presence.
While you have this special sense active, you cannot be surprised or suffer penalties to your initiative rolls.

features.name: Wolf Techniques
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, you experience grants you knowledge and wisdom to value patience but also waste no action. Your patience has taught you to act with no hesitation. Once per turn, you can spend 2 Posture to use one of your Prosthetic Tools at no action cost.
Additionally, when you use a Prosthetic Tool that deals damage or requires an attack roll, as part of the Action used to activate it, you can spend 1 Posture to trigger Lethal Attack against all affected creatures so long as you haven't used Lethal Attack in the same turn.

features.name: Mortal Rhythm
features.description:
Starting at 14th level, you have mastered the ebb and flow of your preys' defenses. You don't just read your target, you dismantle them, one rhythmic motion at a time. Your Hunters Strike special reaction can be used as part of any Reaction ability granted by Wolf's Proficiency.
If you would use your Hunters Strike special reaction to use Deflect, Parry or Retaliate, you can spend 2 Posture to deliver a devastating interruption, forcing the triggering creature to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC. On a failure, their remainder of their Action is Interrupted, ending whatever Action or activity they were doing prematurely. If they failed by 5 of more, they also become Dazed and you gain 1 Posture.

features.name: Kage's Die Twice
features.description:
Starting at 17th level, you no longer fear death. You've mastered it. Your battle rhythm hums within you even during your final moments. Once per Full-Rest, when you are reduced to 0 hit points, and would fall unconscious, you can choose to instead remain standing with 1 hit point, gaining 3 Posture and you regain your reaction if you've already used it.
Through this mastery of life and death, you have also mastered the Wolves ultimate technique. You gain the Shadow Execution Exploit.

pickable.editorDescription: Hunter-Nin exploits, learned via the level-up window. Use + Custom for homebrew entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Hunter's Exploits are learned at levels 3 (×2), 10 (+1), and 17 (+1). Free subclass exploits are added automatically and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Aim
options.description: As a bonus action, make a Perception check vs the target's AC. On success, the first attack made against the target is at advantage. If you beat the AC by 10 or more, that attack's critical threat range is increased by +1.

options.name: Analyze
options.description: As a bonus action, make an Investigation check contested by the target's Deception. On success, learn two traits the adversary has; if they're at or below your level, also learn their highest save bonus. A creature can only be Analyzed once per rest.

options.name: Botany
options.description: When you finish a long rest, choose two regions (Land of Fire, Earth, Lightning, Water, Wind, Snow, or three Minor Lands). Until your next full rest, you gain a rank of Mastery to all Nature and Survival checks made within the chosen lands.

options.name: Confuse Beast
options.description: As an action, pick a beast within 30 ft. Make an Animal Handling check contested by its Wisdom save. On success, the beast cannot take actions or reactions until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Diplomacy
options.description: As an action, convince a creature within 30 ft who can hear and understand you. Contested Persuasion vs their Insight (they get advantage if you damaged them in the last hour). On success, increase their disposition toward you — they may help you with a goal against their better judgment.

options.name: Distraction
options.description: As a reaction on an ally's turn, target a creature within 30 ft that can hear you. Make a Deception check contested by the target's Wisdom save. On success, the target becomes Confused.

options.name: Emulate Predator
options.description: While hidden from a creature within 60 ft, as an action make a Nature check contested by its Wisdom save. On success, the target is Demoralized and must Dash away on its next turn via the safest available route, unless nowhere to go.

options.name: False Faces
options.description: As an action, make a Performance check contested by observers' Insight. On success, perfectly imitate a humanoid you've interacted with for at least 10 minutes. Disguise lasts 1 hour. Doesn't duplicate Kekkei Genkai, chakra signature, or fool jutsu-based sensory effects.

options.name: Hinder
options.description: As a reaction to an attack or jutsu cast within 30 ft, make a Sleight of Hand check contested by the target's Constitution save. On success, the target becomes Dazzled.

options.name: I Wouldn't Do That
options.description: As a reaction when a creature would become hostile in a social encounter, make an Intimidation check contested by their Wisdom save. On failure, they're Demoralized and seek another way to resolve things. If others become hostile, the hesitated creature acts last in initiative and doesn't roll.

options.name: Intuit
options.description: As an action, target a creature within 30 ft. Make an Insight check contested by their Deception. On success, gain the benefit of See Through Lies and Sense Motive against that creature.

options.name: Lustful Assumptions
options.description: In a social encounter with a non-negative/non-hostile creature, as an action make a Persuasion check contested by their Insight. On success, the target is Charmed by you for 1 hour.

options.name: Kleptomaniac
options.description: As a bonus action, mark a lock or creature as your Primary Target. While marked this way, the DC to pick the lock or pickpocket the creature is reduced by 4.

options.name: Shadow Step
options.description: While in dim light, darkness, or obscured, as a bonus action teleport to another space granting the same condition. Make a Stealth check vs every hostile creature's Passive Perception; on success, you become undetectable by sensory-keyword jutsu until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Study
options.description: As a reaction when a creature attempts a jutsu or attack against you, make a History check contested by their Charisma save. On success, you have advantage on the first of either a skill check or a saving throw made against that creature before the end of your next turn.

options.name: Tumble
options.description: As a reaction to being attacked, make an Acrobatics check vs the attacker's attack roll result. On success, the attack reduces its damage by 2 damage dice.

options.name: Vanish
options.description: While being observed, as an action make a Stealth check contested by all creatures' Perception. On success, move up to your speed into a space with partial or total concealment and treat your result as a Hide attempt. Observers see you vanish into thin air.

options.name: Deceptive Exchange
options.description: In a social exchange, when a creature attempts to deceive you, as a reaction make a contested Deception check vs their result. On success, you glean one piece of truthful information based on the lie they tried to pass.

options.name: Owls Teachings
options.description: While observing a creature, as an action make a contested Insight vs their Deception. On success, see through them; if they have hostile intentions toward you or anyone near them, you become aware of their malice.

options.name: Void Step
options.description: By spending all your movement, select a Void Anchor you currently have active and teleport to it, landing in a space of your choice within 5 ft of the Anchor. If you teleport within 5 ft of a creature marked with your Anchor, your next attack against them can trigger Lethal Attack.

options.name: Battle Cry
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target and all hostile creatures within 10 ft of the target must make a Wisdom save. On failure, affected creatures gain 1 rank of Demoralized.

options.name: Brute
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target makes a Strength save. On failure, the target is knocked prone and gains 1 rank of Weakened.

options.name: Chakra Recoil
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo up to 2 Lethal Attack dice. Roll the forgone dice — gain temporary Chakra equal to the highest roll. Once every 10 minutes while in combat.

options.name: Feint
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage to a target within 30 ft, forgo 2 Lethal Attack dice. The target makes a Wisdom save. On failure, the target cannot target you until the end of their next turn. Once every 10 minutes per creature.

options.name: Festering Siphonage
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die. The target makes a Strength save. On failure, the target reduces their AC and saving throws by 1 until the end of your next turn, and you gain a +2 bonus to your next attack against them before the end of your next turn.

options.name: Savage Attack
options.description:
When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 or 2 Lethal Attack dice.
  • 1 die: the target makes a Strength save; on failure, they gain 1 rank of Weakened and have disadvantage on their next attack roll before your next turn.
  • 2 dice: the target makes a Strength save; on failure, they're knocked prone and cannot take reactions.

options.name: Instruct
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 3 Lethal Attack dice. Each allied creature's next attack roll against the target is made at advantage.

options.name: Posture Break
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 1 Lethal Attack die (or 0 dice if this exploit is used as part of your Retaliate). The damaged creature cannot cast jutsu that boost their AC or provide bonus DR until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Shadow Execution
options.description: When you score a critical hit and deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 5 Lethal Attack dice. The affected creature makes a Constitution save. On failure, they become Stunned until the end of your next turn.

options.name: Sunder
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage, forgo 2 Lethal Attack dice. The target makes a Dexterity save. On failure, they reduce their AC by 4 until the end of their next turn.

options.name: Wardens Assault
options.description: When you deal Lethal Attack damage using your Warden Weapon, forgo up to 3 Lethal Attack dice. You manifest afterimages, repeating your attack against other creatures of your choice in range — each hit deals the original damage plus the remaining Lethal Attack damage.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Choose any one skill or tool kit you have proficiency in. You gain a rank of Mastery in it.
Select another proficient skill or tool kit and gain a rank of Mastery in it.
Select another proficient skill or tool kit and gain a rank of Mastery in it.

features.name: Refined Focus
features.description:
Starting at 1st level, you begin your path to mastery over Chakra and Ninjutsu. This manifests within you as Focus. You carry a number of Focus equal to 1+ proficiency bonus. You can spend this Focus to accomplish feats of control and finesse with Ninjutsu impossible by others of similar skill called Moldings. Normally, you can use one molding as part of a Ninjutsu you cast. Each molding costs 1 Focus unless otherwise specified. You recover spent Focus on a long rest.
You begin knowing two Moldings of your choice found at the end of the class. You learn additional moldings as you grow in level as seen in the Ninjutsu Specialist class chart. You can only use moldings on Ninjutsu you cast without the Combination keyword.

features.name: Chakra Compression
features.description:
Also at 1st level, you have learned to compress your chakra into a more efficient state when casting Ninjutsu, increasing your efficiency beyond the pale. Once per turn, when you cast a Ninjutsu as an Action, you may compress your chakra into a more efficient state, conserving your stamina. When you do, reduce the cost of the jutsu cast by an amount equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
Beginning at 11th level, your ability to compress your chakra has amplified and become far more prescient. By spending 1 Focus you can cast a Ninjutsu without the Combination keyword, with the casting time of 1 Action, at D-Rank, at 0 Chakra cost. This becomes C-Rank or lower at 17th level.

features.name: Scroll Stance (Chakra Stance)
features.description: Beginning at 1st Level you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the Chakra Stances located in Chapter 13: Customization Options.

features.name: Ninjutsu Tradition
features.description: Starting at 2nd level, you begin to Specialize in a Tradition that enhances the style of ninjutsu that you focus on. The Tradition that you choose grants you features at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th & 18th Levels.

features.name: Scholar
features.description: Also, at 2nd Level your study of Ninshou, chakra and other theoretical and practical applications of ninjutsu gives you a leg up in scholastic use of this knowledge. You gain a rank of Mastery in the Ninshou skill. You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Ninshou at 7th and 13th levels.

features.name: Chakra Surge
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level you have learned a technique allowing you to enter a heightened state of chakra potency, called a chakra surge, but unlike other battle states, this one just like your Ninjutsu is flexible in its purpose and use. You have two different battle states you can enter; Crash and Flow. In order to enter either of these states, you must be in combat and once per turn, spend a bonus action. You cannot benefit from both states at once and if you would attempt to enter either Crash or Flow while benefiting from the other, you exit the state you are currently in first. When you activate this feature to enter either state, you can remain in either state for a combined duration of up to 1 minute and you cannot re-enter either state until you have spent 10 minutes of uninterrupted time resting or meditating.

CRASH (Will Amp)
Crash symbolizes you releasing the limits on your chakra coils, empowering the jutsu you cast.
  • Once per casting, you gain a +1d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast. Increases to +2d10 at 7th, 3d10 at 11th, 4d10 at 15th, and 5d10 at 19th level. Reduce the dice rolled by half (rounded up) if the jutsu affects an area.
  • You can use two or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • You can use the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.
  • You cannot benefit from any source of cost reduction. Casting a Jutsu at 0 Cost does not count as cost reduction.

FLOW (Will Amp)
Flow symbolizes you controlling and refining your chakra, making slight adjustments to it as the battle progresses.
  • You gain a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC. Increases to +2 at 11th level and +3 at 19th level.
  • When you begin your turn in this state, you regain 1 Focus.
  • You make Concentration checks to maintain Ninjutsu at advantage.
  • You cannot benefit from jutsu or features that boost the damage you deal with Ninjutsu.

actions.name: Draw Reserves
actions.description: While in Crash, you can take the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Tenketsu Refinement
features.description: Starting at 5th Level, you learn how to relax your chakra pathway system, allowing you to regain your composure in a swift and more effective manner than most others. When you complete a short rest, if you have less than half of your maximum Focus, you set your Focus to half of its maximum value.

features.name: Meditation
features.description: Also at 5th Level, you take your skill with chakra network refinement and extend it to your meditations and short downtimes. When you and allied creatures would spend at least 10 uninterrupted minutes resting, you can lead a group meditation where you can roll 1d8, regaining the result as chakra for yourself and all participating allied creatures. A creature can only benefit from this feature once per short rest. This becomes 2d8 at 11th and 3d8 at 17th levels.

features.name: Counterseal
features.description:
Starting at 7th Level, you learn to weave a defensive chakra seal, meant to counteract chakra-based interference. When you or an allied creature within 30 feet of you would make a saving throw vs a Jutsu with the Fuinjutsu keyword, you can spend your reaction to grant the affected creature a +2 to their saving throw. If the triggering jutsu would inflict the Sealed condition, the chosen ally makes their saving throw at advantage instead.
When you reach 13th level, when you use this feature, you can instead spend 1 Focus to affect all willing creatures in a 30-foot radius, centered on yourself.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Perfected Surge
features.description:
Starting at 9th Level, you've perfected your Chakra Surge, mastering both your Crash and Flow states. With this mastery, comes a focus. You gain one of the following evolutions to your Crash and Flow.
Select one option each, one Crash Evolution and one Flow Evolution gaining the 9th level effect (You may also choose a Neutral Evolution which counts as either a Crash or Flow, your choice). Starting at 15th level, you may select one additional evolution from the same list but only gaining the 15th level benefit. You do not need to have previously selected the 9th level evolution to select the 15th level evolution and you cannot switch your chosen evolution effects once selected.

OVERDRIVE
Crash Evolution
You push your chakra network to its limits outputting more than humanly possible.
9th Level. While in the Crash state, once per turn, when you cast a Ninjutsu, you roll 1d4 before applying its affects. On a roll of 4, you upcast by 1 rank it at no additional cost.
15th Level. While in the Crash state, when you upcast a Ninjutsu at least 1 rank higher than its normal rank, you can use one Molding at no Focus cost.

BREACH
Crash Evolution
Your thrashing chakra can bypass constructs and structures as if they were formed of less tough stuff.
9th Level. While in the Crash state, structures and constructs that would intercept your Ninjutsu attacks now allows half of your damage to affect its intended target if the attack roll would have hit the original target.
15th Level. While in the Crash state, Ninjutsu you cast that would force a saving throw but have a structure or constructs intercepting it still forces creatures that would be in the affected areas range to make a saving throw. On a failed save the affected creatures take half damage and the structure takes the full damage. On a success, throw only the structure takes damage.

HARMONY
Flow Evolution
Your chakra harmonizes with your allies.
9th Level. While in the Flow state, when an allied creature within 30 feet casts a Jutsu you can spend your reaction to apply your Save DC bonus to their Jutsu.
15th Level. While in the Flow state, when an allied creature within 30 feet makes a saving throw against a jutsu you can spend 1 Focus to apply your Save DC bonus to their saving throw result.

CIRCUIT
Flow Evolution
You maintain a clean chakra flow, reducing waste and internal friction.
9th Level. While in the Flow state, Ninjutsu you cast that require concentration cost 0 chakra to concentrate on the first time you maintain it, costing its normal amount every time you maintain it thereafter.
15th Level. While in the Flow state, you gain immunity to hostile effects that would increase the cost of jutsu you cast.

UNION
Neutral Evolution
You have an even balance of synergy between Crash and Flow.
9th Level. Once per turn, at the start of your turn, you can swap your state without spending a Bonus action.
15th Level. Once per turn, when you swap your state, you regain 1 Focus.

CRASH (Perfected) (Will Amp)
  • Once per casting, you gain a +5d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast.
  • You can use three or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • Your Ninjutsu treats immunity to their damage type like resistance.

FLOW (Perfected) (Will Amp)
  • You gain a +3 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC.
  • You regain 1 Focus at the start and end of each of your turns.
  • You can maintain concentration on up to 3 Ninjutsu at once.

features.name: Ninjutsu Tradition (3)
features.description: The Tradition that you choose grants you features at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th & 18th Levels.

features.name: Chakra Compression (2)
features.description: Beginning at 11th level, your ability to compress your chakra has amplified and become far more prescient. By spending 1 Focus you can cast a Ninjutsu without the Combination keyword, with the casting time of 1 Action, at D-Rank, at 0 Chakra cost. This becomes C-Rank or lower at 17th level.

features.name: Meditation (2)
features.description: This becomes 2d8 at 11th and 3d8 at 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Counterseal (2)
features.description: When you reach 13th level, when you use this feature, you can instead spend 1 Focus to affect all willing creatures in a 30-foot radius, centered on yourself.

features.name: Perfected Surge (2)
features.description: Starting at 15th level, you may select one additional evolution from the same list but only gaining the 15th level benefit. You do not need to have previously selected the 9th level evolution to select the 15th level evolution and you cannot switch your chosen evolution effects once selected.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Chakra Compression (3)
features.description: This becomes C-Rank or lower at 17th level.

features.name: Meditation (3)
features.description: This becomes 2d8 at 11th and 3d8 at 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Ninjutsu Master
features.description: Starting at 20th level, your chakra has become boundless. Your Crash ignites your chakra coils like a solar flare, and your Flow hums like a perfect current. You have become the pinnacle of Ninjutsu refinement, pushing on the very limits of chakra itself. When you would enter either your Crash or Flow state, you can spend 4 Focus, perfecting them for the next minute. Once you do this, you cannot do so again for 10 minutes.

subclasses.name: Blaze Walker
subclasses.description: Primal engine of destruction when using Fire Release Ninjutsu. Burns hotter, faster, and brighter than others.

features.name: Fire Release
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 2nd Level, you gain the ability to learn ninjutsu with the Fire Release Keyword. If you already have access to this nature release, you learn 2 Fire Release Ninjutsu that you qualify for, one of which can be C-Rank or lower. You can also learn and create Ninjutsu with the Fire Release Keyword in half the required time.

features.name: Pyroclasm
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level, your chakra burns hotter, faster and brighter than others. But this flame doesn't simply go out. Instead, it keeps burning until reaching its full potential. Once per turn, when you would cast a Jutsu with the Fire Release keyword, you gain 1 Pyroclasm, represented as a burning ember of flame on your body in a fashion of your choice, with a max of 3 Pyroclasm that can be maintained. You can ignite Pyroclasm on the following abilities (Igniting Pyroclasm spends them).
Detonate. As an Action, you ignite any number of Pyroclasm of your choice, releasing a shockwave of burning fire chakra. All creatures within 10 feet of you must make a Dexterity saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC. On a failed save, affected creatures take 2d6 fire damage and gain 1 rank of Burned for each Pyroclasm spent.
Hellrend. As part of the same action used to cast a Ninjutsu with the fire release keyword, that deal fire damage you can ignite any number of Pyroclasm, reducing affected creature's DR (Damage Reduction) by 2 for each Pyroclasm spent until the end of your next turn.
Devastation. As part of the same action used to cast a Ninjutsu with the Fire Release keyword, that deals fire damage you can ignite 1 Pyroclasm. When you do, roll 1d6, maximizing a number of the triggering damage dice equal to half the result, rounded down, to a minimum of 1.

features.name: Over the Limit
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your battle states are symbolized by flaming red chakra either flickering off of your body while crashing, or a soft ember heat radiating from you while in flow. When you enter your Crash state, by spending 1 Pyroclasm, you begin to Overheat for the duration of the Crash state. While overheating, Ninjutsu you cast with the Fire Release keyword cast gain a +2 bonus to the damage it deals. This increases to +4 at 10th level and +6 at 18th level.
While overheating, you take 1d6 fire damage at the end of each of your turns. This damage cannot be reduced, resisted by any means.

features.name: Crimson Kindling
features.description:
Also at 6th level, you learn a new Molding called Crimson Kindling.
Crimson Kindling. When you cast a Ninjutsu with the fire release keyword that affects an area and forces a saving throw, you can spend 2 Focus. When you do, all creatures in range with ranks of Burned, that fail their saving throw, suffers a -1 penalty to their jutsu save DCs for each rank of Burned they have. This penalty lasts until the beginning of your next turn. If an affected creature's type is a Plant, Beast or Construct (made of flammable material such as wood), they their saving throw at disadvantage.

features.name: Tidebreaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, Ninjutsu you cast with the Fire Release keyword gain advantage on Clash checks vs Jutsu with the Water Release Keyword.

features.name: Ignition
features.description:
Also at 10th level, your control over Pyroclasm has evolved, allowing you to amplify its effects with further refined chakra control. When you use a Pyroclasm ability, by spending 1 Focus, you can further enhance their listed effects, by selecting one of the associated following. By spending an additional 1 Focus you can select one additional enhancement.
Detonate (Burst). Now affects all creatures within 20 feet
Detonate (Heat). Increase the damage per Pyroclasm spent to 3d6.
Hellrend (Sunder). If you spent at least 2 Pyroclasm, then the affected creatures lose their resistances until the beginning of your next turn.
Devastation (Probability). This effect increases the maximum number of dice maximized by +2.
Devastation (Potential). Increase the size of the dice rolled to 1d10.

features.name: Over the Limit — Burnout
features.description: Starting at 14th level, your Crash, Overheat state flares up to white hot levels. When you would enter Overheat, by spending 2 Focus as part of entering the enhanced state, you enter Burnout. While in Burnout you gain the benefits of Overheat, you gain 1 Pyroclasm at the end of each of your turns and you may use the Detonate ability as a bonus action, while also treating it as if it were a Ninjutsu with the Release being cast. But while in Burnout, you take 3d6 fire damage at the end of each of your turns. This damage cannot be reduced, resisted or ignored by any means.

features.name: Blaze Walker (Ultimate Molding)
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, you learn the ultimate molding for fire release ninjutsu called Blaze Walker.
Blaze Walker. When you cast a ninjutsu with the fire release keyword, you can spend 2 Focus and 2 Pyroclasm to embolden the jutsu cast. When you do, increase the damage affected creatures take from creatures other than yourself, the first time each turn by +8. This incoming damage boosting effect lasts until the beginning of your next turn.
Additionally, affected creatures cannot lose or remove ranks of Burned until the beginning of your next turn as their flames burn hotter than any Medical ninja could reasonably treat.

subclasses.name: Lightning Breaker
subclasses.description: Unmatched force of unstoppable power, greater than a lightning storm, when using Lightning Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Lightning Release
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 2nd Level, you gain the ability to learn ninjutsu with the Lightning Release Keyword. If you already have access to this nature release, you learn 2 Lightning Release Ninjutsu that you qualify for, one of which can be C-Rank or lower. You can also learn and create Ninjutsu with the Lightning Release Keyword in half the required time.

features.name: Stormcircuit
features.description:
Also at 2nd Level, your lightning doesn't simply strike-it lingers, arcs and surges. You wield lightning like a painter wielding a brush, imprinting your enemies with volatile currents and galvanizing them into a field of lethality.
Once per turn, when a creature fails a saving throw against a Ninjutsu you cast with the Lightning release keyword, you can mark them with Static. A creature marked in this way remains marked for 1 minute. You can have no more than to two creatures marked with Static at a time. This increases to three creatures at 10th level and four at 18th level. Marking a new creature beyond your limit causes the oldest Static mark to end immediately. While at least two creatures are marked with Static, they become Linked with one another. Creatures remain linked to each other so long as they are within 120 feet of each other.
When you deal lightning damage with a Ninjutsu to a creature marked with static you can forgo a number of your jutsu's damage dice to apply the following effects.
1 Damage Die. By forgoing 1 damage dice, all Linked creatures suffer a surge of electricity through their body, reducing their speed by half.
2 Damage Dice. By forgoing 2 damage dice, all Linked creatures suffer the same condition inflicted by the triggering Jutsu.
3 Damage Dice. By forgoing 3 damage dice, all Linked creatures take the same damage result.

features.name: Voltaic
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your battle states are symbolized by surging sparks of chakra either jolting off of your body while crashing, or gently sparking across the surface of your body while in flow. When you enter your Flow state, by dispelling one creature's Static mark, you become Voltaic for the duration of the Flow state. While voltaic, you gain immunity to the effects of the Shocked condition, but can still gain ranks of it. When you cast a Ninjutsu that affects a creature with ranks of Shocked, you can consume their ranks of Shocked, reducing the number of ranks they have, and gaining an equal number of ranks of Shocked. You can spend ranks of shock you have in the following ways.
Circumvent. When you would forgo damage dice to use an ability granted by Stormcircuit, you can instead spend 1 rank of shock for each damage dice you would forgo.
Speed Jolt. Once per turn, you can spend 1 rank of Shocked to move like a jolt of lightning, teleporting into a space you can see within 10 feet of you.
Supercharge. Once per turn, you can spend 1 rank of Shocked when casting a Ninjutsu with the lightning release keyword to gain the benefit of its Overcharge effect.
At the end of each of your turns, you take 1d6 lightning damage for each rank of Shocked you have. This damage cannot be reduced, resisted or ignored by any means.

features.name: Gigawatt Pulse
features.description:
Also at 6th level, you learn a new molding called Gigawatt Pulse.
Gigawatt Pulse. When you cast a Ninjutsu with the Lightning Release keyword, you can embolden your allies as part of the jutsu's casting by spending 2 Focus. When you do, all allied creatures within 30 feet of you surges with ambient chakra, gaining a +2 bonus to one of the following of their choice until the beginning of your next turn. If their choice requires them to roll a d20 roll to benefit from, the benefit is applied to the next time they roll with the chosen statistic; AC, Attack roll, Critical threat range, Saving throw, Skill check or ability check.
Additionally, if the hostile creature(s) affected by the triggering jutsu's creature type is an Aberration, Celestial or Mutant, then they make all saving throws against the jutsu cast at disadvantage.

features.name: Breeze Breaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, Ninjutsu you cast with the Lightning Release keyword gain advantage on Clash checks vs Jutsu with the Wind Release Keyword.

features.name: Galvanize
features.description:
Also at 10th Level, your technique with battling against Linked enemies has evolved, granting you additional abilities you can use if you forgo the triggering jutsu's damage dice.
1 Damage Die. By forgoing 1 damage die, all linked creatures suffer disadvantage on their next attack roll.
2 Damage Dice. By forgoing 2 damage dice, all linked creatures are pulled 20 feet closer to the damaged creature.
3 Damage Dice. By forgoing 3 damage dice, all linked creatures cannot take a bonus action until the end of their next turns.

features.name: Voltaic — Conduction
features.description: Starting at 14th Level, your Flow, Voltaic state surges with consistent energy and efficiency. When you would enter the Voltaic state, by spending 2 Focus as part of entering the enhanced state, you begin Conduction. While in Conduction, you gain the benefits of Voltaic, you gain 1 rank of Shocked at the beginning of each of your turns and Speed Jolt is no longer once per turn. But while in Conduction, you instead take 1d12 lightning damage per rank of shocked, instead of 1d6.

features.name: Lightning Breaker (Ultimate Molding)
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, you learn the ultimate molding for lightning release ninjutsu called Lightning Breaker.
Lightning Breaker. When you cast a ninjutsu with the lightning release keyword, you can spend 2 Focus and 2 ranks of Shocked to embolden the jutsu cast. When you do, all affected creatures have all jutsu based effects currently affecting them (excluding conditions) dispelled and they must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC, being Stunned until the end of their next turn on a failure.

subclasses.name: Stone Crusher
subclasses.description: Unwavering force of strength, tenacity, and mountain-like toughness when using Earth Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Earth Release
features.description:
When you choose this tradition at 2nd Level, you gain the ability to learn ninjutsu with the Earth Release Keyword. If you already have access to this nature release, you learn 2 Earth Release Ninjutsu that you qualify for, one of which can be C-Rank or lower. You can also learn and create Ninjutsu with the Earth Release Keyword in half the required time.
Also, Earthen Artifacts you conjure can have an AC of 10, and 10 Hit Points if you choose, if neither statistic is established in its method of summoning, being destroyed/dispelled when it reached 0 hit points.

features.name: Seismic Tether
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd Level, you learn to manipulate the battlefield through a unique fusion of geomantic pressure, chakra anchored constructs and reactive reinforcement. Once per turn, as an Action or bonus action or as part of casting a Ninjutsu with the Earth Release keyword, you can mark a space you can see within 20 feet of you with a special chakra seal known as a Geo-Tether. Geo-Tethers can also be treated as Earthen Artifacts. You can maintain up to two tethers at a time. This increases to three at 10th level and 4 at 18th level. While you have at least 1 Tether active, once per turn, when you cast a Ninjutsu with the earth release keyword or take damage you gain 1 Tension (Max 4), which you can spend to accomplish any of the following.
Stone Bind. Once per turn, either as an Action or Bonus Action, by spending 1 Tension, all creatures of your choice within 10 feet of a Geo-Tether must make a Strength saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC. On a failed save, they take 1d6 Earth damage and have their speed reduced by half until the end of their next turn or half as much damage on a success and no further effects. This damage increases to 3d6 at 6th, 5d6 at 10th and 7d6 at 14th levels.
Grit. As a reaction to yourself or an allied creature within 10 feet of a Geo-Tether taking damage, you can spend a number of Tension equal to the number of damaged creatures. Each damaged creature gains +6 Temporary hit points which last until the beginning of your next turn as the Geo-Tether siphons some of the impact damage. This increases to +10 at 6th, +14 at 10th and +18 at 14th levels.
Adamantine Blood. As part of casting a Ninjutsu with the earth release keyword that summons a Construct or structure with hit points within 10 feet of a Ge0-Tether, you can spend 1 Tension. The summoned construct or structure gains DR (Damage Reduction) vs all damage types equal to the rank it was summoned at for the duration of its summoning. (D-Rank/C-Rank: 4, B-Rank/A-Rank: 6, S-Rank: 8).

features.name: Mountain's Aegis
features.description: Starting at 6th level, your battle states are symbolized by materializing fragments of stone either being compressed and atomized while crashing, or by floating harmlessly around you while in flow. When you enter your Flow state, by spending 1 Tension, you manifest your Aegis. While your Aegis is manifested, when you take damage while within 10 feet of an Earthen Artifacts, you may choose to redirect up to half the damage you would take to that construct, as it takes the redirected damage in your place Any excess damage transferred back to you. While wearing your Aegis, once per turn, when you take damage, you reduce your AC by -1 (Max -2), until the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Tectonic Mantle
features.description:
Also, at 6th Level, you learn a new molding technique called Tectonic Mantle that you can use by spending 2 Focus.
All allied creatures within 20 feet of a Geo-Ether gain resistance to the next instance of damage they take before the start of your next turn.
All hostile creatures that fail this jutsu's saving throw has their speed reduced to 0 until the beginning of your next turn and suffer disadvantage on the next Dexterity saving throw they make before the beginning of your next turn.
