Additionally, if an affected creature is a Demon, Monstrosity or Undead, they instead suffer disadvantage on any saving throws before the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Current Breaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, Ninjutsu you cast with the Earth Release keyword gain advantage on Clash checks vs Jutsu with the Lightning Release Keyword.

features.name: Bulwark
features.description:
Also at 10th Level, your mastery over geomantic tethering becomes absolute, no longer anchoring constructs, but shaping the battlefield.
• You can now have a maximum of 6 Tension and can gain Tension when you end your turn within 10 feet of a hostile creature.
• Earthen Constructs you conjure now have 20 Hit Points if it's not established in its method of summoning.
• You can place Geo-Tethers up to 30 feet away.
• Once per turn, if a hostile creature moves within range, you can use Stone Bind at no Action cost.
• When you activate Grit in response to an allied creature other than yourself taking damage, you can redirect up to half the damage they would take to that construct, as it takes the redirected damage in their place. Any excess damage transferred back to them.

features.name: Mountain's Aegis — Bastion
features.description: Starting at 14th Level, your Flow, Aegis state solidifies and hardens like steel as you become a Bastion. By spending 2 Focus as part of manifesting your Aegis, you become a Bastion. While a Bastion, you gain the benefits of Aegis and yourself and allied creatures within 20 feet of you gain immunity to the Bruised, Dazed and Chilled conditions. But while a Bastion, your AC reduction is no longer limited to once per turn and its Maximum value increases to (-3).

features.name: Stone Crusher (Ultimate Molding)
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, you learn the ultimate molding for Earth release ninjutsu called Stone Crusher.
Stone Crusher. When you cast a ninjutsu with the earth release keyword, you can spend 2 Focus and 2 Tension to embolden the jutsu cast. All allied creatures within 30 feet of a Geo-Tether gain a +4 bonus to their AC and immunity to critical hits until the start of your next turn. All hostile creatures within 30 feet of a Geo-Tether suffer the effects of Stone Bind as if they failed their saving throw.

subclasses.name: Storm Terror
subclasses.description: A ruthless natural disaster and unmatched collateral destruction when using Wind Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Wind Release
features.description:
When you choose this tradition at 2nd Level, you gain the ability to learn ninjutsu with the Wind Release Keyword. If you already have access to this nature release, you learn 2 Wind Release Ninjutsu that you qualify for, one of which can be C-Rank or lower. You can also learn and create Ninjutsu with the Wind Release Keyword in half the required time.
Also, you gain proficiency with the Gunbai Fan and Giant Iron Fan if you were not already. The Gunsen, Gunbai Fan, and Giant Iron Fan all gain the Evocation weapon property with a Ninjutsu Echo and Wind Damage with a range of 60 feet. This cannot be changed.

features.name: Tempest Pressure
features.description:
Also at 2nd Level, you have learned to shape and wield the wind itself, building the intensity of each breeze until they become a tempest of pressure. Once per turn, when you would cast a jutsu with the Wind Release keyword or make a Ninjutsu attack using a weapon with the Evocation property, you build Pressure. You can maintain up to 3 Pressure at a time and they last for 1 minute or until spent. For every Pressure you begin your turn with you gain +2 temporary hit points as your swirling wind release chakra protects you. You can spend Pressure in the following ways. If an ability requires a saving throw, you use your Ninjutsu save DC.
Gale Burst. As part of casting a Ninjutsu with the wind release keyword or make a Ninjutsu attack using a weapon with the Evocation property, you can spend 1 Pressure to force all creatures within 10 feet of you to make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they are pushed 10 feet away from you and are knocked prone.
Sharpening. Once per turn, as part of casting a Ninjutsu with the wind release keyword or make a Ninjutsu attack using a weapon with the Evocation property that deals damage, you can spend 1 Pressure to increase the lethality of the jutsu cast, dealing +1 Wind damage for every damage dice rolled, up to a maximum of +10.
Dragwind. As part of the casting a Ninjutsu with the wind release keyword that affects an area, you can spend 1 Pressure to increase the size of the affected area by +5 feet. This increases to +10 feet at 10th and +15 at 18th levels.

features.name: Storm Herald
features.description:
Starting at 6th Level, your battle states are symbolized by powerful torrents of wind either pushing things away crashing, or cycling around you flow. When you enter your Crash state, by spending 1 Pressure, you become a Herald of storms. While acting as a Herald, you gain a 40-foot fly speed and you may halve the damage dice granted by Crash, and instead apply the halved amount to two separate jutsu cast while in this state.
While acting as Herald you become a prime target. Hostile creatures gain a +1 bonus to their attack rolls when attacking you.

features.name: Gale Fracture
features.description:
Also, at 6th Level, you learn a new molding technique called Gale Fracture.
Gale Fracture. When you cast a Ninjutsu with the Wind Release keyword, you can spend 2 Focus to embolden the jutsu cast sundering an enemy's defenses. All affected creatures who fail the jutsu's saving throw (or if the jutsu doesn't force a saving throw affected creatures must make a Dexterity saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC.) on a failed save they suffer a -2 penalty to their AC and a -1 to their saving throws until the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Blaze Breaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, Ninjutsu you cast with the Wind Release keyword gain advantage on Clash checks vs Jutsu with the Fire Release Keyword.

features.name: Eye of the Storm
features.description:
Also at 10th Level, your mastery of wind release intensifies, allowing you to channel greater and more potent winds, generating more pressure. You can now maintain up to 5 Pressure. You also gain enhanced versions of abilities granted by the Tempest Pressure feature which you can activate by spending 1 Focus as part of using them.
Gale Burst. Creatures who fail their saving throw can now be pulled 10 feet closer to you. If they are, they are Dazed instead of being knocked prone.
Sharpening. Increase the bonus damage to +2 for every damage die rolled. (Still a max of +10).
Dragwind. Creatures with ranks of a Physical or Elemental condition that makes a saving throw against the jutsu you cast that affects an area, cannot benefit from jutsu based bonuses to the saving throw.

features.name: Storm Herald — Typhoon
features.description:
Starting at 14th Level, your Crash, Herald state rages upending stone and other unattended objects. When you would act as a Herald, by spending 2 Focus as part of entering the enhanced state, you instead become a Typhoon. While a Typhoon you gain the benefits of being a Herald, you gain 1 Pressure at the end of each of your turns and you may use the base effects of Gale Burst, Sharpening or Dragwind once per turn, at no Pressure cost.
But while a Typhoon, hostile creatures gain a +2 bonus to their attack rolls and +1 critical threat range when attacking you.

features.name: Storm Terror (Ultimate Molding)
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, you learn the ultimate molding for Wind release ninjutsu called Storm Terror.
Storm Terror. When you cast a ninjutsu with the wind release keyword, you can spend 2 Focus and 2 Pressure to embolden the jutsu cast. When emboldened in this way, you generate a zone of terror around no more than one affected creature until the end of your next turn. This zone is called a Storm Zone and radiates outward in a 60-foot radius, centered on the select creature. Hostile creatures in this zone cannot benefit from cover, concealment or Invisibility, their ranged attacks are made at disadvantage, and Jutsu they cast with the Barrier keyword are summoned with half of their listed or rolled hit points.

subclasses.name: Summoner
subclasses.description: Walks the path closest to Sage hood — known for the widest arrangement of jutsu learned directly from sage creatures.

features.name: Bonded Summoning
features.description:
When you choose this tradition at 2nd level, you establish a contract with a Sage tribe, learning the Summoning Technique ninjutsu and changing its casting time to 1 Action. As part of taking this tradition and learning the summoning technique ninjutsu, you also form a bond with the first creature you summon with it, as it becomes your Bonded Sage Beast. A sage creature that is Bonded with you is different from all the rest, as they grow alongside you, benefiting from the following. You can only have one Bonded creature and this cannot be changed, except by GM intervention.
• They are always summoned at the highest rank possible, but at half the chakra cost. They share your level, using it level in place of their own for calculating their AC and Hit points.
• When you complete a rest, your Bonded creature regains half of their maximum hit points and Jutsu slots.
• Your Bonded creature has one of its Tribes Roles, called their Primary Role plus one additional Role of your choice found in Jiraiya's Jutsu, Summoning Jutsu Section.
• At 6th, 10th, 14th and 18th levels, they gain 3 points which they can use to increase their ability scores and can select 1 additional skill for them to gain proficiency in.
• Beginning at 2nd level, they begin with 2 known D-Ranks and C-Ranks, when they reach 6th level, increase the number of jutsu known of D and C-Ranks by +1. Beginning at 10th level, they gain 2 known B-Ranks, at 14th level, they gain 2 known A-Ranks, and at 18th level, they gain +1 known of all currently known jutsu ranks.
• They can use your Focus in place of their Jutsu slots to cast Jutsu they know.
• When they reach 0 hit points, they are dispelled before suffering fatal injury.

features.name: Tag-Team
features.description:
Also, at 2nd Level, you and your Bonded creature are so uniquely acquainted that when you fight, you feel far more like a single entity. When you summon your Bonded creature, your wills bleed into one another, allowing you to pull off feats of teamwork unheard of by other practitioners of Summoning. You and your summon the listed abilities based on their primary roles.
Strikers. Once per turn, your summon score two hits with an attack as part of the attack action or casting a jutsu, you regain 1 Focus.
Caster. Once per turn, while in combat, when your summon casts a jutsu spending a Jutsu Slot, you regain 1 Focus.
Controller. Once per turn, when your summon casts a Jutsu that at least one creature fails their saving throw against, you regain 1 Focus.
Defender. Once per turn, your summon can spend their Reaction to interpose taking the damage from an attack targeting you while they are within 10 feet of you.
Lurker. Once per turn, when your summon deals their Lethal Attack damage, you can teleport into a space up within 20 feet of them that you can see.
Supporter. Once per turn, when your summon restores hit points, chakra points or grants temporary hit points or chakra points to another creature, as part of the action used to apply them you or your summon can reduce the rank of a condition you (or it) has by 1.

features.name: Summoning Duality
features.description:
Starting at 6th Level, your bond with your sage creature deepens, allowing your battle states to echo through one another like mirrored halves of the same soul. When you enter your Crash or Flow state, by spending 1 Focus you and your Bonded creature enter a unique variant of your Chakra Surge states; Crash Duality and Flow Duality. Whichever variant state you enter, your Bonded creature enters the opposite state for the same duration. You and your summon cannot be in the same battle state at the same time. Entering a different battle state causes your summon to switch their states at the same time, always being in the inverse state.
Additionally, your Bonded creature gains additional benefits based on their Primary Role with the battle state they are in and at the end of each of your turns, your Bonded creatures loses 2 Jutsu Slots.
STRIKER
Crash. Your summon gains a +2 bonus to the damage it deals with attacks.
Flow. When your summon scores a critical hit, it regains 2 jutsu slots.
CASTER
Crash. Once per turn, your summon can reroll the damage it deals with jutsu it casts.
Flow. It can main concentration on jutsu for its full duration instead of 2 turns.
CONTROLLER
Crash. As part of casting their jutsu your summon can use one of your Moldings by spending 1 Focus.
Flow. Your summon can use its role ability to apply a d4 penalty twice per turn, instead of once.
DEFENDER
Crash. Your summon gains Temporary hit points equal to its level at the beginning of each of your turns.
Flow. Your summon turns the first crit they take each turn into a normal hit.
LURKER
Crash. Your summon can move at their normal speed and Dash while Sneaking.
Flow. Your summon gains +10 movement speed and advantage on stealth checks.
SUPPORTER
Crash. Add the damage bonus to the amount of hit points restored.
Flow. As part of its Action for its turn, Your summon can spend 2 Jutsu slots causing you to regain Chakra equal to your Chakra Die plus your Constitution modifier.

CRASH DUALITY (Will Amp)
  • Once per casting, you gain a +1d10 bonus to damage you deal with Ninjutsu you cast. Increases to +2d10 at 11th and +3d10 at 19th level.
  • You can use two or more Moldings at once as part of the same casting.
  • You can use the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.
  • You do not benefit from cost reduction from any source. Casting a Jutsu at 0 Cost does not count as cost reduction.

FLOW DUALITY (Will Amp)
  • You gain a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu Save DC. Increases to +2 at 15th level.
  • When you begin your turn in this state, you regain 1 Focus.
  • If you are a Bonded creature, you instead regain 1 Jutsu Slot.
  • You make Concentration checks to maintain Ninjutsu at advantage.
  • You cannot benefit from jutsu, features or traits that boost the damage you deal with Ninjutsu.

actions.name: Draw Reserves
actions.description: While in Crash Duality, you can take the Draw Reserves skill action as an Action.

features.name: Combination Technique
features.description:
Also, at 6th Level, you learn a new molding called Combination Technique.
Combination Technique. You spend 2 Focus and command your Bonded creature to spend their reaction to embolden the jutsu cast. The Jutsu is upcast by 2 ranks at no additional cost and your jutsu can gain the benefit of any Role effect or Special feature your summon has that would qualify if they had cast it themselves. If your Bonded creature has no such Role effect or Special feature that qualifies, then they can cast a D-Rank jutsu they know as part of this molding at no Jutsu slot cost. This becomes C-Rank at 14th level.

features.name: Rule Breaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, you can cast the Summoning Technique ninjutsu as a Bonus Action on your turn.

features.name: Summoning Expert
features.description: Also at 10th Level, your bond with your Sage Tribe has become unbreakable. You are their found family, and they are yours. You can gain a second Bonded sage creature, most likely one that is related to your first bonded creature but may also just be another member of the tribe you've grown close to, but its level is treated as half of yours (Rounded up). You can have both of your Bonded creatures summoned at once, and can now spend either your Action or Reaction to command the Bonded creature that has not been commanded this turn. Additionally, only one bonded creature can benefit from your Summoning Duality class feature at a time

features.name: Summoning Master
features.description:
Starting at 14th Level, your bond with your sage tribe and particularly your Bonded summons, deepens into perfect synchronicity. In battle you can momentarily channel a surge into your Bonded allies, awakening their full potential.
As part of summoning a Bonded creature, you can spend 4 Focus. When you, the Bonded creature summoned is suffused with your chakra radiating through them. But unlike normal summoning, they are automatically dispelled/unsummoned after 1 minute due to the burden. While suffused with your chakra for the duration, they gain a new role of your choice called the Bonded Role. This role only grants them the benefits of the role for the purposes of the Tag-Team and Summoning Duality class features. (A total of 3 roles, 1 Primary, 1 Secondary, 1 Bonded).
You can only have one Bonded creature with a Bonded Role summoned at a time.

features.name: Synchronization Technique
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, you learn the ultimate molding for working with your Bonded summons called Synchronization Technique.
Synchronization Technique. You spend 2 Focus and command each Bonded creature to spend their reaction and 4 Jutsu Slots to embolden the jutsu cast. When you do, yourself and all Bonded creatures unite around this single jutsu. Each Bonded creature must cast a Jutsu it knows of the lowest rank it can cast, relying on your initial jutsu. If the triggering jutsu scores a hit or at least one of the affected creatures to fail their saving throws your Bonded creatures jutsu to apply their effects as if they scored a hit or the creature also failed their saving throws vs their jutsu.
Once you use this molding, you cannot do so again for 10 minutes

subclasses.name: Tsunami
subclasses.description: Embodies the force of a tsunami — unphased by the sea's power and all-encompassing like an ocean when using Water Release Ninjutsu.

features.name: Water Release
features.description: When you choose this tradition at 2nd Level, you gain the ability to learn ninjutsu with the Water Release Keyword. If you already have access to this nature release, you learn 2 Water Release Ninjutsu that you qualify for, one of which can be C-Rank or lower. You can also learn and create Ninjutsu with the Water Release Keyword in half the required time.

features.name: Shifting Tides
features.description:
Also, at 2nd Level, you learn to manipulate water as a force of control and pressure, exerting a crushing momentum through your water release forcing enemies to bend to this flow. When you cast a Ninjutsu with the Water Release keyword or make an attack with a weapon with the Evocation property, you can Shift Tides entering one of four different unique combat stances until the end of your next turn, applying its effects to the triggering jutsu and subsequent jutsu you cast for the duration of the stance. You can shift stances once per turn.
Shark Maw. Once per turn, while in this stance, Cold damage you deal, also deals Chakra damage to a creature equal to half the Cold damage result.
Piranha Fang. Once per turn, while in this stance, when you inflict the Chilled condition on a creature, you gain 1 Focus.
Surge. While in this stance, when you deal damage to a creature you can move them up to 10 feet away from you or closer to you (your choice).
Undertow. While in this stance, Ninjutsu you cast with the Water Release keyword bypasses interception effects of jutsu with the Barrier Keyword, directly affecting at least one of its original targets

features.name: Surging Maelstrom
features.description:
Starting at 6th Level, your battle states are symbolized by swirling vortexes of water—thrashing violently in Crash or spiraling calmly around your limbs in Flow. When you enter your Crash state, by spending 1 Focus, you become a Maelstrom, devouring control and territory with each motion. While acting as a Maelstrom you can benefit from two stances from Shift Tides at once, and once per turn, when you deal Cold damage to a creature with a Ninjutsu with the Water Release keyword, you can inflict one of the affected creatures with Tidal Draw until the beginning of your next turn.
While a Maelstrom, you increase each instance of damage you take by +2.

features.name: Pressure Stagnation
features.description:
Also, at 6th Level, you learn a new Molding called Pressure Stagnation.
Pressure Stagnation. When you cast a Ninjutsu with the water release keyword you can spend 2 Focus to embolden the jutsu cast, creating a zone of Stagnation around yourself, radiating outwards in a 40-foot radius sphere until the end of your next turn. All hostile creatures that would cast a jutsu that requires HS (Hand Signs) while within the Stagnation radius take 4d6 Cold and Chakra Damage as spikes of Water Release Chakra forms spikes and spears of drilling water that siphons chakra from triggering foes. This increases to 6d6 at 14th level.

features.name: Terra Breaker
features.description: Starting at 10th level, Ninjutsu you cast with the Water Release keyword gain advantage on Clash checks vs Jutsu with the Earth Release Keyword.

features.name: Tidal Crescendo
features.description:
Also at 10th Level, your mastery over shifting chakra stances allows you to manipulate your stances with greater force and fluidity. The stances you can swap between have been enhanced greatly.
Shark Maw. The Chakra damage instead equals the total Cold damage dealt.
Piranha Fang. Can trigger twice per turn.
Surge. You can instead move a creature 15 feet.
Undertow. You can affect all creatures in an affected area when bypassing a barrier.

features.name: Surging Maelstrom — Abyss
features.description: Starting at 14th Level, your Crash-Maelstrom state rages grinding away stone and sheering other solid structures with droplets of water alone. When you would become a Maelstrom, by spending 2 Focus as part of entering the enhanced state, you instead become an Abyss. While an Abyss, you gain the benefits of being a Maelstrom, you can inflict Tidal Draw on all creatures affected by a Ninjutsu you cast with the Water release keyword and each time a you deal Chakra damage to a creature, you regain 1 Focus. But while an Abyss, you instead increase each instance of damage you take by +4.

features.name: Tsunami (Ultimate Molding)
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, earn the ultimate molding technique for Water release ninjutsu called Tsunami.
Tsunami. When you cast a ninjutsu with the Water Release keyword, you can spend 3 Focus you to embolden the jutsu cast.
All affected creatures in your jutsu's range that fails their saving throw (or if the jutsu doesn't force a save, the affected creatures must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC) increases the cost of jutsu they cast by an amount equal to your level, until the end of your next turn.

pickable.editorDescription: Refined Focus moldings, learned via the level-up window. Use + Custom for homebrew entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Refined Focus Moldings are learned at levels 1 (×2), 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, and 19 (+1 each). Tradition-granted moldings are added automatically and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Careful Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that forces other creatures to make a saving throw, select up to 4 creatures. Chosen creatures suffer no effect from the cast Ninjutsu.

options.name: Distant Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu, increase its range by +30 feet. If the Ninjutsu has a range of touch, the range instead becomes 30 feet.

options.name: Doubled Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that targets only one creature and doesn't have a range of self, you can target a second creature in range with the same Ninjutsu.

options.name: Focused Ninjutsu
options.description: When you're forced to make a concentration check to maintain a Ninjutsu you've cast, you automatically pass. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Heightened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that forces a saving throw, give one target disadvantage on its first saving throw against the Ninjutsu. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Piercing Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that deals damage, it ignores resistance and half of a creature's DR. Creatures immune to the damage type instead treat their immunity as resistance.

options.name: Potent Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that deals damage, after damage is rolled you can select up to 3 damage dice and treat each selected die as the maximum possible result.

options.name: Precise Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that requires an attack roll, you can gain advantage on one of the attacks.

options.name: Quickened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu that has a casting time of 1 action, you can change the casting time to 1 bonus action.

options.name: Reactive Ninjutsu
options.description: As a reaction, on your turn, you can cast a Ninjutsu with a casting time of 1 action.

options.name: Redirected Ninjutsu
options.description: If you make an attack roll for a Ninjutsu and would miss, you can remake the attack roll targeting another creature within range. You must use the second result. Can be used with other moldings.

options.name: Subtle Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu, you can cast it without any hand signs. Can be used in addition to other moldings.

options.name: Tenacious Ninjutsu
options.description: When a creature attempts to Clash, dispel, or interrupt a Ninjutsu you're casting or have cast, use your reaction to increase your clash check by 1d10 and the DC of Countering, dispelling, or interrupting this jutsu by +2.

options.name: Widened Ninjutsu
options.description: When you cast a Ninjutsu with an area of effect, increase its size (line +5/+10, cube/sphere +5 ft radius, cone +10 ft length, cylinder +10 ft radius or height).

options.name: Jutsu Breaker
options.description: When a creature within 90 feet casts a Ninjutsu that would deal damage, as a reaction cast a 1-action Ninjutsu you know. It gains the clash keyword if it doesn't have it and automatically initiates a clash.

options.name: Crimson Kindling
options.description: Area Fire Release jutsu: creatures with Burned ranks failing saves suffer -1 to their jutsu save DCs per rank of Burned. Plant/Beast/flammable Construct makes the save at disadvantage.

options.name: Gigawatt Pulse
options.description: On Lightning Release cast, all allies within 30 ft gain +2 to a chosen statistic until your next turn. Aberration/Celestial/Mutant enemies affected make all saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Tectonic Mantle
options.description: Allies within 20 ft of a Geo-Tether gain resistance to the next damage. Hostile creatures failing their save have speed 0 and disadvantage on next Dex save. Demon/Monstrosity/Undead → disadvantage on all saves.

options.name: Gale Fracture
options.description: On Wind Release cast, affected creatures failing the save suffer -2 AC and -1 saves until your next turn.

options.name: Combination Technique
options.description: Command your Bonded to spend their reaction to embolden the jutsu cast — upcast by 2 ranks, gains any Role/Special feature your summon qualifies for. Becomes C-Rank at 14th.

options.name: Pressure Stagnation
options.description: On Water Release cast, create a 40-ft Stagnation sphere. Hostile creatures casting Hand-Sign jutsu in it take 4d6 Cold+Chakra (6d6 at 14th).

options.name: Blaze Walker (Ultimate)
options.description: On Fire Release cast, affected creatures take +8 damage from other sources the first time each turn. Cannot lose/remove Burned ranks until your next turn.

options.name: Lightning Breaker (Ultimate)
options.description: On Lightning Release cast, affected creatures have all non-condition jutsu effects dispelled, then CON save or Stunned until end of their next turn.

options.name: Stone Crusher (Ultimate)
options.description: On Earth Release cast, allies within 30 ft of a Geo-Tether gain +4 AC and crit immunity. Hostile creatures within 30 ft of a Tether suffer Stone Bind's effects as if they failed.

options.name: Storm Terror (Ultimate)
options.description: On Wind Release cast, generate a 60-ft Storm Zone around one creature. Hostiles inside lose cover/concealment/Invisibility, have disadvantage on ranged attacks, and Barrier jutsu summon with half HP.

options.name: Synchronization Technique
options.description: Command each Bonded to spend reaction + 4 slots to ride your initial jutsu — each must cast a low-rank jutsu; if yours hits, theirs apply too. 10-min cooldown.

options.name: Tsunami (Ultimate)
options.description: On Water Release cast, affected creatures failing (or CON save if no save) increase jutsu cost by your level until end of your next turn.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in Ninshou.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Ninshou.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Ninshou.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

features.name: Shinobi's Path
features.description:
Beginning at 1st level you realize that a shinobi's strength doesn't lie in raw power or hyper specialization. It lies in flexibility, adaptation, and awareness in the world around them. This evolving awareness sets you apart from more specialized shinobi, for when others rely on a single discipline, you walk the winding path of versatility.
Choose a Shinobi Path below. You gain an additional choice at 11th level.

PATH OF AWARENESS
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of Awareness, mastering the unseen is your art, and your perceptive capabilities are your brush to the canvas that is the world. You trained yourself to not only notice what others miss, but to exploit it. You gain the following benefits;
Overly Perceptive. You can use the Search or Identify Genjutsu skill-based actions as a Bonus action.
Sensory Type. As an Action, you can broaden your perceptive abilities allowing you to sense chakra. When you do, make a Wisdom (Perception) check vs the Chakra Control DC of all creatures within 60 feet of you. On a success, you gain the benefit of the Chakra Sensing special sense for the next minute against them for the duration until they either have 0 chakra or they make and succeed their Suppress Chakra skill action.

PATH OF THE MENDER
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of the Mender, you understand that even the sharpest blade dulls in isolation. You walk a path of stabilization, bringing focus, healing and calm during and after a heated battle gaining the following benefits;
Field Medic. You can use the Administer Aid skill action as a bonus action.
Battle Medicine. You can use the Treat Wounds skill action as an Action.

PATH OF THE SWIFT
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of the Swift, movement is life. You've trained to move like wind through the forests, across rooftops and along sheer canyon walls, not just to escape Danger, but to control the battlefield, gaining the following benefits;
Mastering the Basics. When Wall Running or Water Walking, you do not spend extra movement.
Boosted Athleticism. Increase your movement speed by +1 feet and the height of your High jump and distance of your long jump by 5 feet.

PATH OF THE COMMANDANT
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of the Commandant, a plan wins fights before the first-hand sign is ever made. You've studied battlefield tactics, choke points and ambush tactics. Your presence on a team isn't just help, it's a force multiplier. You gain the following benefits;
Field Strategist. You can take the Help Action as a Bonus action even with skills you do not have proficiency in.
Tactical Callouts. Once per turn, when an allied creature is the target of an attack or jutsu's effect, you can use your reaction to move up to half your movement closer to them and if you end your movement within 15 feet of them, you command them to take cover, granting them a +2 bonus to their AC or saving throw against the triggering attack or saving throw.

PATH OF THE ARCHIVIST
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of the Archivist, knowledge is the ultimate weapon. You devour information faster and process that information faster than anyone else. You gain the following benefits;
Encyclopedic Knowledge. When you take the Identify Ninjutsu/Genjutsu/Taijutsu/Bukijutsu action when you make your check, you compare your result to the targets Jutsu save DC (you do not add the +5 to the save DC)
Lore Extraction. You can attempt to Study as a Full-Turn Action, instead of spending 1 minute completing the activity. Additionally, whenever you Research a topic, can choose to hyper fixate for 1 hour gaining a 1d8 bonus instead of spending multiple hours on the topic.

PATH OF THE ROGUE
In the eyes of a Scout-Nin following the Path of the Rogue, you become a whisper in the dark, the unseen hand slipping through the cracks of an enemies defenses, making silence, shadow and sleight of hand your main weapons gaining the following benefits;
Veiled Hand. You can use the Steal Action as a Bonus Action and you can use the Pick a Lock action without a Security Kit once per lock as if you have 1 Charge.
Shinobi Step. You can move your full movement when attempting to Sneak.

features.name: General Technique
features.description:
Also at 1st Level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the Fighting Stances located in Chapter 13: Customization Options, between Taijutsu Stance, Weapon Stance and Chakra Mode(s). You select an additional Fighting stance of a type you have not selected from at 7th and 11th levels. You can't take a Stance more than once.
Also, when fighting with basic Shinobi implements (Kunai, Shuriken, Senbon's), when you take the attack action you can attack twice, instead of once, with these weapons. If you do, you cannot make an attack with them as a Bonus Action the same turn.
Finally, you can use the following tools in place of an attack made with your Attack Action; Chili Pepper Bombs, Paper Bombs, Flash Tags or Smoke Bombs.

features.name: Adaptive Initiative
features.description:
Finally, at 1st Level, your training emphasizes adaptability over raw reflexes. Whether it's gauging enemy intent, reacting with raw physicality, or sensing fluctuations in chakra, you can leverage specialized knowledge to seize the initiative. When you would roll Initiative, you can choose to substitute your Initiative bonus with one of the following skills, based on the circumstances of the encounter. If the GM determines that your declared substitution is not relevant to the encounter's opening situation, they may require you to roll Initiative instead
Athletics. You can substitute your initiative for an Athletics check if prior to combat breaking out you were doing something physically demanding or athletic in nature.
Ninshou. You can substitute your initiative for a Ninshou check if prior to combat, you could sense the chakra of a hostile creature or are concentrating on a Ninjutsu at the time of combat being initiated.
Illusion. You can substitute your initiative for an Illusion check if prior to combat you were investigating a Genjutsu, concentrating on a Genjutsu or are under the effects of a Genjutsu at the time of combat being initiated.
Insight. You can substitute your initiative for an insight check if you are aware of the emotional or intents of the creatures you are initiating combat with or if you are aware their current attitude towards you is Hostile prior to combat being initiated.

features.name: Superior Technique
features.description:
Starting at 2nd level, you have learned to weave together the arts of battle into a single flowing form. While others specialize, you adapt. Switching from a flurry of weapon or unarmed strikes to weaving a Ninjutsu to conjuring an illusion in a mixed assault, pressing every advantage.
You gain a pool of 3 Superiority Dice represented as d8's which embody your refined technique and your ability to act decisively in any situation. You gain more as you gain levels in the class as defined in the Superiority Dice column in the Scout-Nin class table. You can spend this superiority die when a class feature calls for it. You recover spent Superiority Dice when you spend at least 10 minutes on uninterrupted rest or meditation.

features.name: Switch Hitter
features.description:
Also, at 2nd level your style thrives on switching approaches. You blend casting disciplines with such skill that your transitions become strengths, not weaknesses.
First, select your Primary casting discipline keyword (Ninjutsu, Genjutsu, Taijutsu and Bukijutsu). The chosen casting discipline becomes the lynchpin of your form of fighting. When you cast your Primary jutsu, you set up a strong follow up for the next jutsu. You cannot select a keyword as your Primary if your keywords casting ability modifier is lower than another casting ability modifier. (Ex. You cannot select Ninjutsu as your primary, if your Taijutsu ability modifier is higher than your Ninjutsu ability score.)
When you cast your Primary casting discipline, you can choose to either Follow Up, or Set-Up.
Follow Up. One affected creature grants you a +1 bonus to hit or to your Jutsu save DC against them if the next jutsu cast affecting them is not your Primary casting discipline. This becomes a +2 a 11th and +3 at 19th level. This bonus to hit and save DC lasts until the end of your next turn.
Set-Up. All affected creatures suffers a -1 penalty to their AC or Saving throw (your choice) against the next jutsu cast from one of your allies (excluding you) before the beginning of your next turn. This becomes a -2 at 11th and -3 at 19th level.

features.name: Jack of All
features.description:
Finally at 2nd level, you exemplify the ideal of generalized combat. Select one of the following Generalizations between Warfare, Seize and Tactics. When you do, you gain its benefits. You can switch which generalization you have by spending a Bonus Action.

WARFARE
When using the Multiattack trait with a Kunai, Shuriken, or Senbon, you can add your ability modifier to the damage rolled. Also, once per turn, while you are gaining the benefits of this generalization you can spend one Superiority Die as part of casting a Jutsu. When you do, you gain a bonus to the damage dealt equal to 1d8. This bonus increases to 2d8 at 5th, 3d8 at 11th, 4d8 at 15th and 5d8 at 20th level. If used as part of a jutsu that affects an area, roll half the listed number of dice, rounded up, minimum 1.
If you use a Maneuver granted by your Scouting Technique (Subclass) as part of an attack or jutsu's casting, you can use this feature as part of that maneuver, reduce the number of bonus damage by 1 dice when you do.

SEIZE
While you are gaining the benefits of this generalization, when you would force a creature to make a saving throw with a Maneuver, item, or feature you use, you can spend 1 Bonus Action. When you do, the affected creature cannot gain bonuses to their saving throw as a result of jutsu they cast.
Also, when making a contested check with a hostile creature, you can spend 1 Superiority Die to add the result to your check.

TACTICS
While you are gaining the benefits of this generalization, when you use a skill-based action that require an Action, it can be performed as a Bonus action. Once per turn, If a skill-based action costs a reaction, you can use it at no action cost.
Also, you can spend one superiority die as part of a Skill-based action, adding a +2 bonus to the check. This bonus increases to+3 at 11th, +4 at 20th level.

features.name: Scouting Technique
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, choose a technique that you strive to emulate in combat and style. The Scouting Technique you choose grants you features at 3rd, 6th, 10th, 14th and 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
If you take the Attack action using a Kunai, Shuriken or Senbon, you can attack three times, instead of twice, whenever you attack with those weapons.
Finally, the following explosive tools gain a d4 ammunition die instead of a set number of charges/uses, so long as they are of base quality; Chili Pepper Bombs, Paper Bombs, Flash Tags, and Smoke Bombs.

features.name: Superior Momentum
features.description: Also at 5th level, once per turn you can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. By spending 2 Superiority Die you can take one additional action on your turn, which can only be used to Cast a Jutsu. This action cannot be used to cast a jutsu of the same casting discipline you previously cast on this turn. If you would use this feature again before you complete a rest, increase the number of Superiority Die you spend to activate this feature by +1 for each time used beyond the first.

features.name: Signature Jutsu
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level, you have found a jutsu that you specialize in better than anyone else who may have come before. Select one Jutsu you know of B-Rank or Lower. The chosen jutsu becomes known as your Signature Jutsu, gaining one of the following Signature Modification of your choice when casting the chosen Jutsu and when cast, it can count as your Primary jutsu regardless of its casting discipline keyword. You may switch which jutsu this feature affects whenever you would complete a Full-Rest. You can select a second Signature effect, which the chosen Jutsu gains the benefits of as well, at 15th level.

SIGNATURE MODIFICATION - POWER
When you use your chosen jutsu, your Jutsu ignores resistance to its damage and treats immunity to its damage as resistance.
If you are gaining the benefit of the Warfare generalization, you add your Jutsu's ability modifier to the damage dealt.
If you are gaining the benefit of the Seize generalization, you can cast your chosen jutsu as a Bonus Action, in place of an Action. You cannot cast your Chosen jutsu as an Action if you do. If you already cast your chosen jutsu as an Action, you cannot cast it as a Bonus action using this ability.

SIGNATURE MODIFICATION - RAMPING
When you would upcast your chosen jutsu, increase the number of damage or healing dice it rolls by +1.
If you are gaining the benefit of the Seize generalization, and a creature casts a Jutsu as a reaction to something you do, they increase the cost of that jutsu by an amount equal to your level.
If you are gaining the benefit of the Tactics generalization, if your jutsu requires an attack, you can use one skill-based action that requires an Action or Bonus Action, as part of the attack.

SIGNATURE MODIFICATION - REFINEMENT
When you would cast your chosen jutsu, reduce the cost of the chosen jutsu by an amount equal to its rank. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
If you are gaining the benefit of the Tactics generalization, if the jutsu you cast requires you to make a check, you gain a +1d4 bonus to the result.
If you are gaining the benefit of the Warfare generalization, you can use two Maneuvers per turn, even using them both as part of your chosen jutsu's casting.

features.name: General Technique (2)
features.description: You select an additional Fighting stance of a type you have not selected from at 7th and 11th levels. You can't take a Stance more than once.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Signature Path
features.description:
Beginning at 9th level, you commit to a path originally chosen when you became a Scout. The original path you chose gains a new enhanced benefit as listed below. Beginning at 18th level, you can select the enhanced benefits of the second path chosen.

PATH OF AWARENESS – PERCEPTION
Creatures making attacks or opportunities against you or allied creatures within 10 feet of you are made at disadvantage and you gain 20 feet of Blind sight.

PATH OF THE MENDER – MEDIC
Blood Pills and Chakra Pills you use increase the amount of hit points or chakra you gain from them by +5 and all Medicine Kit actions that cost 2 or more charges, reduce the number of charges they cost by 1.

PATH OF THE SWIFT – MOBILE
Yourself and allies who begin their turns within 10 feet of you can take the Dash and Disengage actions as Bonus Actions. You can also ignore difficult terrain and environmental effects that cost extra movement.

PATH OF THE COMMANDANT - LEADER
You and your team are able to fight for longer periods of time by forcing your allies to go beyond their normal limits.
As an Action, you can release a battle cry inspiring your allies to keep going. Each allied creature within 60 feet of you, that can see or hear you, that is inspired by your cry can spend 2 Hit die & Chakra die. Regaining hit points and chakra points equal to their respective dice results plus their constitution modifier.

PATH OF THE ARCHIVIST - GENIUS
Your ability to consume information and utilize it for any situation. You gain a bonus to all checks you make when using a Skill or Toolkit you have proficiency in by +2.
Additionally, when you roll a 1 on a Skill or ability check, you can reroll it taking the second result.

PATH OF THE ROGUE - HIDDEN TECHNIQUE
You can remain perfectly still for long periods of time to set up ambushes. As an Action while you are at least lightly obscured, you can attain perfect stillness. When you do, you and all allies within 5 feet of you become Invisible to creatures attempting to Search for you for the next minute.
If in combat. This invisibility lasts until the beginning of your next turn or one of the other Invisible creatures moves, castings a jutsu, attacks, or performs any action that reasonably requires physical movement of some type.
While a creature is gaining this benefit, they are also undetectable by the following special senses; Tremor sense and Chakra sight.

features.name: General Technique (3)
features.description: You select an additional Fighting stance of a type you have not selected from at 7th and 11th levels. You can't take a Stance more than once.

features.name: Shinobi's Path (2)
features.description: Choose a Shinobi Path below. You gain an additional choice at 11th level.

features.name: Multifaceted
features.description:
Beginning at 11th level, When you cast a Jutsu as an Action, you can spend your Bonus Action, making 2 Unarmed or Weapon Attacks using a Kunai, Shuriken or Senbon.
Additionally, if you take the Attack Action and make an Unarmed or Weapon attack using a Kunai, Shuriken or Senbon, you can apply Follow Up to one creature damaged as part of your attacks made.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Signature Switch
features.description:
Starting at 13th level, when you deal damage to a hostile creature using your Primary casting discipline, you can spend 1 Superiority Die to apply both Follow Up and Set-Up to the same hostile creature.
Additionally, once per casting, when you deal damage to a creature with a Jutsu you cast, you can switch both your current Fighting Stance and your Generalization after the damage calculation.

features.name: Signature Jutsu (2)
features.description: You can select a second Signature effect, which the chosen Jutsu gains the benefits of as well, at 15th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Signature Path (2)
features.description: Beginning at 18th level, you can select the enhanced benefits of the second path chosen.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Superior Scout

subclasses.name: Arbiter Scout
subclasses.description: Focused on commanding and enabling allies — battlefield coordinators who turn a team into a force multiplier.

features.name: Superior Arbitration
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to command and order your allies to victory on the battlefield, which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Arbitration Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
Also, you learn to enforce your presence into any discussion or squabble. You gain proficiency in Deception, Intimidation and Persuasion if you were not already. If you are proficient in any of the above skills already, you instead gain a rank of Mastery in the associated skill you have proficiency in.

features.name: Battlefield Judication
features.description:
Also, at 3rd level, you become the leader your team needs in times of strife and conflict. Someone who can lead the team to victory no matter the odds. When you complete a rest, you select a number of creatures other than yourself, equal to your proficiency bonus that you designate as your Squad-Mate(s), sometimes simply referred to as Squad.
You and your Squad gain the following benefits when in combat:
• When initiative is rolled, you and your Squad gain a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma Modifier.
• Once per turn, when you would take the Attack Action, in place of one of your attacks, you can select one member of your Squad who can see or hear you and Command them. They can do one of the following of their choice;
  o Make a weapon attack.
  o Cast a Jutsu of D-Rank or lower that requires an attack roll. This increases to C-Rank at 10th level and B-Rank at 17th.
  o Perform a Skill-based Action. You regain 1 superiority die.
  o Take the Dodge action
  o Take the Disengage action and move up to half their speed. You regain 1 superiority die.
• When a Squad-Mate makes an attack of any type, you can spend a Superiority die and your Reaction, to make a weapon attack against the same target if you can. On a hit, you add your Superiority dies result to both your own and the triggering allies damage rolls.

features.name: Absolute Authority
features.description:
Beginning at 6th level, your will becomes law on the battlefield, and your presence shapes the flow of combat like a shinobi general. You gain a rank of Mastery in one of the following skills; Deception, Intimidation or Persuasion.
When yourself or a Squad-Mate would attempt to perform a Skill-Based Action, using Strength, Dexterity, or Charisma. As a reaction, you can use any Charisma based skill you have ranks of Mastery in, in its place as you declare with confidence alone what and how it should be done.

features.name: Adaptive Orders
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your commands become second nature to your squad, honed by experience and mutual trust. Your Squad no longer needs to see or hear you to follow your commands so long as they are within initiative with you.
Additionally, you can Command your Squad-Mates twice per turn now, instead of once. By forgoing attacks made as part of your attack action, you can command a different member of your squad, once each, for each attack forgone.

features.name: Commanding Presence
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, your mastery of battlefield tempo and presence allows you to shift the course of battle with a single order or rallying cry. Your Squad moves as an extension of your will.
As a Bonus Action on your turn, you can spend 2 Superiority Die to command all members of your squad to Focus!. All Squad-Mates that can see or hear you in initiative, that are under the effects of a hostile creatures jutsu, feature or trait immediately remake their saving throw to end its effects adding the skill bonus granted by your Jack of All: Tactics class feature to their results.
As a Reaction on a Squad-Mates turn, you can use your Superior Momentum feature by spending 3 Superiority Die. When you do, the Squad-Mate whose turn it is gains an additional Action following the rules of the Superior Momentum class feature. A creature can only benefit from Superior Momentum once per turn.

features.name: Unbreakable Formation
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, through training, perseverance and trusting in each other, yourself and your Squad gain immunity to the Confused condition so long as you are not incapacitated.
Additionally, when you or any member of your Squad begins their turn within 10 feet of at least one other Squad-Mate they gain 10 temporary hit points, which last until the beginning of their next turn. While a member of your Squad has temporary hit points granted by this feature, they gain advantage on saving throws to resist effects that would move or teleport them against their will.

features.name: Who Decided That!?
features.description: Beginning at 17th level, your authority on the battlefield becomes absolute, unyielding and instinctive. Your mere presence shatters doubt and rewrites the fates of your allies as your word is law. When a member of your Squad, other than yourself, fails a saving throw of any kind, you can spend 2 Superiority Die and your Reaction. When you do, they instead automatically succeed, suffering no additional effects. A creature can gain the benefit of this feature once per initiative.

subclasses.name: Assault Scout
subclasses.description: Raw physical power honed to deadly perfection — brutal combatants who overwhelm enemies through aggression and focus.

features.name: Superior Assault
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd Level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to lay down aggressive tactics and strategies snatching victory from your foes through sheer skill alone which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Assault Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
As part of your superior skill to lay down oppressive force, you gain a +1 bonus to your critical threat range with attacks made using your Primary casting discipline.

features.name: Brutish Momentum
features.description:
Starting, at 3rd level, your brutish nature in combat has compelled you to learn to overcome any and every one you consider your rival. This drive has pushed you to become a brutal combatant capable of overwhelming whoever is your enemy with simple but effective tactics.
When initiative is rolled, you may select one creature you can see. This creature is marked as your Combat Rival. Creatures marked as your Combat Rival cannot be changed and remain so until combat ends or either you or they reach 0 hit points, whichever occurs first. When a Combat Rival reached 0 hit points, you may immediately select a new Combat Rival so long as you are still in initiative. While you have a Combat Rival you can use the following abilities.
• Relentless Durability. Once per turn, when you take damage you can spend 1 Superiority Die, gaining temporary hit points equal to the result. This increases to twice the result at 10th level and three times the result at 17th level. These Temporary hit points last until the end of the current turn.
• Relentless Aggression. Once per turn, when score a hit against your Combat Rival on your turn with an attack as a result of the Attack action, you can perform one of the following Skill Based Actions; Trip, Grapple, Shove or Disarm using your attack result in place of your skill check.
• Relentless Pursuit. When your Combat Rival would move further than 5 feet away from you at the conclusion of their movement, you can spend your reaction to move up to your full speed closer to them. If you end your movement in range, you can make an unarmed or weapon attack, adding your Superiority Die to the damage result.
• Relentless Progress. The first time each turn you score a hit against your Combat Rival with an attack whose result is 5 or higher than their AC, or they fail a saving throw by 5 or more as a result of a jutsu you cast, you regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Untapped Potential
features.description:
Beginning, at 6th level, when you would use your Warfare generalization bonus damage effect, you can do so, once per turn, without spending a Superiority Die.
Additionally, when you use a maneuver that requires you to rolls your Superiority Die to determine its effectiveness, you can roll it twice, taking the higher result.

features.name: Brutish Aggression
features.description: Also at 6th level, you've learned to weaponize pressure itself. Every shove, every feint, every lock of your grip is calculated to break your rival's focus and rhythm. When you perform a Skill based action, targeting your Combat Rival, if you succeed the check, they suffer disadvantage on their next attack roll and you regain 1 Superiority Die. You can regain a Superiority Die in this way, once per turn.

features.name: Remarkable Aggression
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, your will to dominate the battlefield manifests in every strike and stance. Where others tire, you press on, undaunted, unbroken, and focused beyond reason. Increase your critical threat range with your Primary casting discipline to +2 and you gain the following abilities to use while you have a Combat Rival.
Relentless Stamina. Once per turn, when you would gain ranks of a Physical condition; you can spend a superiority die to reduce the number of ranks you gain by 1.
Relentless Focus. While you are concentrating on a Jutsu you cast, when you would be forced to make a chakra control check, you add your Tactics Generalization skill bonus to the result.

features.name: Devastating Assault
features.description: Starting at 14th level, your strikes are no longer just attacks, but instead declarations of supremacy. With a single, perfectly timed blow, you bring the full weight of your skill, power, and fury to bear, cutting through defenses with ruthless finality. And should the battle demand it, you'll do it again and again. When you score a critical hit, you can choose to spend 1 Superiority Die to maximize your attacks damage without doubling the dice pool, instead of rolling twice the damage dice pool. If you do, you also double your ability modifier as well (Ex. 4d10 +5, becomes 40+10, instead of 8d10+5) If you would use this feature again before you complete a rest, increase the number of Superiority Die you spend to activate this feature by +1 for each time used beyond the first.

features.name: Relentless
features.description: Starting at 17th level, when you use the Superior Momentum class feature, you can gain 2 Actions, instead of one. But you also increase the superiority die cost by +2. These extra Actions still cannot be used to cast a jutsu of the same casting discipline you previously cast on this turn.

subclasses.name: Cloning Scout
subclasses.description: Masters of duplicative combat — scouts who employ superior numbers via Clones and Bunshin to overwhelm enemies.

features.name: Superior Clones
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to divide and conquer with your ability to multiply yourself which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Cloning Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
You add the Shadow Clone Technique Ninjutsu to your known jutsu list and can cast it as a Bonus Action. You can also learn any Ninjutsu with the Clone keyword, ignoring any nature release keyword requirements. When you summon a clone, you can choose to instead summon a Bunshin, using the Bunshin stat block instead of the cast jutsu's clone. You can have up to 4 Bunshins active at once, and so long as you have Bunshins active, you cannot summon or have clones summoned as a result of jutsu with the Clone keyword active. Bunshins last for 1 minute in combat or 1 hour outside of combat. Bunshins can exist up to 1 mile away from you, but if they would enter combat in any way, they are immediately dispelled. When a Bunshin is dispelled, you gain all the information that Bunshin had prior to being dispelled. You can command Bunshins in the following ways;
Where I say. Once per turn, at no action cost, you can command all active Bunshin to move into a space of your choice within their movement speeds range.
Clone Strike. Once per turn, when you would take the Attack Action, in place of one of your attacks, you can command one or more of your Bunshin to perform a Bunshin Strike or Bunshin Cast.
Split Focus. Once per turn, as a Bonus action, you can command one of your Bunshin to perform a Skill based action or the Help action for one of your allies.

features.name: Tajū Kage Bunshin
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, your control of clones has placed you into an echelon all your own. Unlike others who dip into clone-based tactics, your level of control is otherworldly. By spending 1 Superiority die and a Bonus action, you can summon 1 Bunshin. This increases to 2 Bunshin at 10th and 3 Bunshin at 17th level. Your clones can use the following Class features following their normal limitations and using your use limits; Cloning Maneuvers, Switch Hitter and Jack of All (Using whatever Generalization you are currently benefiting from)
Also, whenever you summon 2 or more Clones or Bunshins at once as part of casting a Ninjutsu with the clone keyword, you regain 1 Superiority die.

features.name: Clones Everywhere
features.description:
At 6th level, once per turn, while you have at least one or more clones or Bunshin active, and you, a clone or a Bunshin scores a hit with an Unarmed/Weapon attack or a Bunshin Strike in the case of your Bunshin, then the damaged creature is momentarily dizzy and must make a Wisdom Saving throw vs your Stealth DC. On a failed save, they become Disoriented.
Additionally, your clones can aid you in retaining and learning information. When you would attempt to learn, modify or create jutsu, your clones help reduce the time needed. Reduce the downtime cost to learn, create and modify jutsu by an amount equal to the rank of the jutsu. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)

features.name: Never Alone
features.description:
Also at 6th level, people begin to realize that even when standing by yourself, you are never truly alone. You always have yourself to back you up. When you make an attack or cast a jutsu, you can command any active Bunshin to assist you. Bunshin that assist you in this way cannot make an attack or cast a jutsu this turn. All assisting Bunshins first move into a space as close to you as possible ignoring their movement speeds doing whatever you describe to assist you in your endeavor. For every Bunshin that assists you, you gain access to different benefits;
Army of One. For every Bunshin that assists you, increase the damage your attack or jutsu would deal by +2, up to a maximum of +10.
Pivot!. Once per turn, you can command one or more of your Bunshin to perform a Bunshin Strike or Bunshin Cast.
Bunshin Throw. If two or more Bunshin assist you, they can throw you to close the distance between you and your target. Select a space you can see no further than 10 times the number of Bunshin assisting you, as they throw you into that space as you also regain 1 Superiority Die. This movement does not trigger attacks of opportunities.
Bunshin Amp. If three or more Bunshin assist you, and you are casting a jutsu that can be upcast, they can sacrifice themselves to amplify your jutsu. Dispel up to four of your assisting Bunshin. For every 2 Bunshin Dispelled, you upcast your chosen jutsu at no additional cost as you also regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Specialized Clones
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, your Bunshin evolve from distractions to elemental instruments of warfare, tuned to react, retaliate, and reshape the flow of battle at your command. First, increase the number of Bunshin you can have active to a maximum of 6.
Second, If a Ninjutsu you have that has the clone keyword also has a nature release keyword, when you summon a Bunshin, they can gain a specialized bonus based on the keyword you have access to. A Bunshin can only benefit from one keyword bonus at a time and you can only have up to two different keywords active at once, regardless of the number of Bunshin you have.
Earth Release. Your Bunshins hit points become 10, instead of 1. Additionally, they gain 10 DR and 10 temporary hit points for the duration of their life. At the beginning of each of your turns, they regain 10 temporary hit points.
Fire Release. Your Bunshins are walking bombs. When they fall to 0 hit points, they detonate forcing all creatures within 10 feet of them must make a Dexterity saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC, taking 6d6 fire damage on a failed save. A creature can only make a saving throw vs this effect twice per turn..
Water Release. Your Bunshins take no damage if they pass a saving throw. Additionally, they can use your Ninjutsu attack bonus in place of their saving throw bonus.
Lightning Release. Your Bunshins are extremely potent. When they deal damage, they use d8's in instead of d6's.

features.name: Clone Tactics
features.description: Starting at 14th level, you know when to get out of dodge and make yourself scarce. When you take damage or fail a saving throw, you may spend your reaction. When you do, first you summon a Bunshin to occupy the space you are currently in, and then teleport into the space of another Bunshin within 60 feet of you. The Bunshin you teleport to is dispelled as part of this teleportation. If this teleport would place you into a space that is at least lightly obscured, you can attempt a Hide skill action.

features.name: Clone Master
features.description:
Beginning at 17th level, you don't just create clones, you command a phantom army. Increase the number of Bunshin you can have active to a maximum of 8. When you would cast a Jutsu with the Clone keyword in order to summon Bunshin, each Bunshin after the first costs 2 Chakra to summon.
Additionally, you can now use the Clone Strike command a number of times equal to the number of attacks you can make as part of your Attack Action.

resources.name: Bunshin (active)
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L3, 6 at L10, 8 at L17

subclasses.name: Elemental Scout
subclasses.description: Nature-release savants who let their chosen element flow constantly through their chakra, amplifying jutsu and their own body alike.

features.name: Superior Elements
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd Level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to manipulate your nature release in such a way as to magnify all form of combat you dabble in which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Elemental Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
You have focused your efforts on harnessing your innate Nature Release. You may select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning Release) gaining the ability to learn and cast Jutsu with the corresponding keyword.
When you cast a jutsu with a nature release keyword, you can retain some of that elemental energy, augmenting you and your weapons, known as Elemental Infusion. Also, once per turn when you cast a Jutsu that doesn't have a nature release keyword, you can give it the nature release keyword you gained to as a result of this class feature.
Elemental Infusion (Strike). Until the end of your next turn, your unarmed and weapon attacks damage type becomes the damage type associated with the nature release of the initiating nature release jutsu.

features.name: Elemental Flux
features.description:
Also, at 3rd level, as part of learning and mastering your nature release, you also become able to unleash this stored power, known as your Elemental Flux. While in combat, when you cast a jutsu with a nature release keyword, you can enter your Elemental Flux state until the end of your next turn. Your Elemental Flux state takes on properties of the casted nature release (Ex. Floating gravel for earth, burning embers for fire and sparks of electricity for lightning). If you cast another jutsu with the same nature release before it ends, the duration is extended by 1 round and its effects persist. If you cast a jutsu with a different nature release, your Flux type immediately changes to match the nature release and the duration resets.
Once per turn, when you cast a jutsu that would apply or benefit from the Follow Up or Set-Up statuses from the Switch Hitter class feature while in Flux, you regain 1 Superiority Die.

features.name: Perceived Weakness
Elemental Aura
features.description:
Also at 6th level, you may select a second nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning Release) gaining the ability to learn and cast Jutsu with the corresponding keyword that you did not learn from the Superior Elements class feature.
You passively radiate a passive aura of elemental energy affecting all creatures of your choice within a 10-foot radius. Affected creatures in your aura gain a bonus on saving throws vs jutsu with a nature release keyword equal to half of your Constitution modifier.
Additionally, while you are in Elemental Flux, you gain resistance to the damage of your current Flux type.

features.name: Resonance
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, through intense training and battlefield intuition, you've learned to ride the wave of your allies power, slipping into Elemental Flux through the echoes of their chakra. When an allied creature you can see within your Elemental Aura of you casts a jutsu with a nature release keyword, you can spend 1 Superiority Die to resonate with their chakra, entering your Elemental Flux state based on the nature release of the triggering jutsu.
Additionally, the triggering ally adds 1d8 to their jutsu's damage or reduces their jutsu's chakra cost by an amount equal to half the result (their choice).

features.name: Primordial Flux
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, you've tapped into a raw well of elemental chakra from deep within you, unleashing the limits of your abilities. Increase the size of your Elemental Aura to a 20-foot radius.
Additionally, once per turn all allied creatures within your Elemental Aura when you enter your Elemental Flux type gains 1d8 temporary chakra.
Finally, your Elemental Infusion now adds 1d8 bonus damage to unarmed or weapon attacks made as part of your attack action or bonus action.

features.name: Primal Storm
features.description:
Beginning at 17th level, your body is constantly producing massive quantities of nature release chakra.
At the beginning of your turn while you are in Elemental Flux, you may spend one superiority die to gain immunity to the damage type of your current Elemental Flux type until the beginning of your next turn.
Additionally, while you are in Elemental Flux you can use one additional maneuver on your turn.

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning) to gain the ability to learn and cast jutsu with that keyword.
Select a second nature release (from the five elements). Your picker excludes any release you already possess.

subclasses.name: Phantom Scout
subclasses.description: Ghostly infiltrators and assassins — masters of stealth, deception, and illusory combat who fade in and out of sight.

features.name: Superior Phantasm
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to trick, confuse and confound your enemies by becoming a phantom on the battlefield, which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Phantom Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
Also, you begin to become familiar with your ghastly jutsu and how it interacts with the world around you. You gain proficiency in Deception and Illusions if you are not already. If you are proficient in any of the above skills already, you instead gain a rank of Mastery in the associated skill you have proficiency in.
Finally in place of an attack made as part of your attack action or as a Bonus Action, you may make a Ghastly Deception check. This is treated as a Skill based action.
Ghastly Deception. Your body fills with phantasmic chakra, enhancing your deceptive capabilities. Select a creature you can see within 20 feet of you and make a Deception check vs the targets AC. On a success, you can select a space you can see within 10 feet of the target that can hold you and teleport into that space as your body dematerializes and rematerializes in the new space in a fashion of your description. This applies the Follow-Up status from the Switch Hitter class feature.

features.name: Phantom's Flame
features.description:
Also at 3rd Level, you learn to compress your phantasmic chakra for bursts of ghastly power known as your Phantasmic Flame. In order to fuel your Phantasmic Flame, you must first build up and spend Ghostfire.
Ghostfire. Once per turn, when you benefit from the Follow Up status or perform a Skill based Action, you gain 1 Ghostfire (Max 3). Ghostfire is represented as soft blue embers or motes of illusory flame that manifest themselves in any way of your description. You can spend Ghostfire when a feature requires it. You begin with the following Phantasmic Flame abilities.
Phantasm's Grip. When you take the attack action or cast a jutsu that requires an attack, you can spend 1 Ghostfire, giving the attack/jutsu the Genjutsu keyword for the purposes of interacting with class features. The triggering attack, shifts it into one that strikes the mind or chakra coils. The attacks damage type becomes psychic or chakra damage (your choice). If the jutsu or maneuver used would inflict a condition, all ranks of the condition that would be inflicted is replaced with Concussed. If the attack is made as part of the attack action, then the affected creature must make a Wisdom saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Genjutsu) save DC, gaining 1 rank of Concussed on a failed save. Regardless, If the attack result is 5 or higher than the targets AC, you regain 1 Superiority die.
Ghost Walk. When you would interact with or walk into a solid structure, you gain an incorporeal form, capable of phasing through solid matter like a ghost. As part of movement on your turn, you can spend 1 Ghostfire to become incorporeal until the end of your current turn. While incorporeal you can move through objects and walls that are not made by jutsu or reinforced with chakra. If you end your movement inside an object or structure, your body is shunted out of it, into the closest space that can hold you, taking 10 necrotic damage for every 5 feet you are forced through while being pushed out. Damage taken in this way cannot be resisted, reduced or ignored by any means.
Gravesight. As a Bonus action, you briefly see through the veil between life and death by spending 1 Ghostfire. Until the end of your next turn, you gain 30-feet of Blindsight, can see Invisible creatures and can discern if a creature is a clone, construct or undead.

features.name: Haunting Veil
features.description:
Like mist rolling through trees or whispers echoing in an empty hall, your presence cloaks your allies. Beginning at 6th level, whenever you are not in combat and would attempt to Hide, all allied creatures within 10 feet of you gain Spectral Concealment and you regain 1 Superiority Die. You can regain 1 Superiority die in this way, once every 10 minutes.
Spectral Concealment. Creatures with spectral concealment uses the Stealth check result of the Scout that applied this status, in place of their own for Hide and Sneak attempts.
