They also become undetectable to Chakra sight and Dark vision. This status lasts for 1 minute or until they become observed by another creature.

features.name: Hungry Phantom Flame
features.description:
Also at 6th level, the Ghostfire you manifest becomes a bit more real and a lot hungrier than they should be. As a reaction to a creature dealing damage to you with an attack, you can spend 1 Superiority Die and any number of Ghostfire you have. When you do, reduce the damage you would take by 2d6 for each one spent. This instead becomes 2d8 for each one spent if the triggering attack is a Genjutsu attack, or the damage dealt is Psychic damage. If this reaction reduces the damage to 0 all hostile creatures within 10 feet of you, must make a Wisdom saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Genjutsu) save DC. On a failed save they take chakra damage equal to the damage reduced as your Ghostfire consumes all the chakra around you and no damage on a success.
Regardless, you gain 1d6 temporary chakra for every Ghostfire spent this way that lasts for 1 minute.

features.name: Phantasm's Inferno
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, your phantasmal chakra grows within you, bleeding into everything you do. Increase the maximum number of Ghostfire you can maintain to 5.
You've also learned how to more easily generate Ghostfire. When an allied creature would benefit from the Set-Up status, you gain 1 Ghostfire.
While learning of more ways to generate Ghostfire, you've also learned of some creative ways to spend it as well. You've learned the following advanced Phantasmic Flame abilities.
Spectral Brand. When you deal psychic or chakra damage to a creature, you can spend 1 Superiority Die and 1 Ghostfire to mark that creature with a special Fuinjutsu made from illusory fire known as a Spectral Brand. A creature is branded in this way for 1 minute or until the brand is detonated. While branded, the affected creature is always under the effect of the Set-Up status, but it only provides its benefits once per turn. The brand can be detonated by you as an Action. When Detonated, all branded creatures must make a Wisdom saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Genjutsu) save DC. On a failed save the brands detonate inflicting 2 ranks of Sealed and Concussed.
Hollow Reflection. When you would gain a Mental or Sensory condition as a result of a hostile jutsu from a creature you can see, you may spend your reaction, 1 Superiority Die and 1 Ghostfire to manifest a ghastly image of the target creature in their own minds. The triggering creature must make an Intelligence saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Genjutsu) save DC. On a failed save you can reduce the ranks of all Mental and Sensory conditions you have to 0. Then the triggering creature gains 2 ranks of each condition you reduced to 0.

features.name: Between the Veil
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, your phantasmic chakra allows you to become physically unbound by normal rules imposed on you the material plane. When you place yourself and at least one other creature under the effects of Spectral Concealment, you can choose one of the following enhancements and gain its benefits for Spectral Concealments duration. Once you select an enhancement, you cannot pick the other one until you've spent 10 minutes of uninterrupted meditation.
Dimensional Walk. You've created a veiled space in-between dimensions using fuinjutsu. While Sneaking, all creatures under the effects of Spectral Concealment is also benefiting from your Ghost Walk ability, at no Ghostfire cost.
Extended Veil. You extend the duration of your Spectral Concealment to 10 minutes. Additionally, you can cast jutsu that is not harmful to another and cannot become harmful to another creature without breaking stealth for the duration.

features.name: Phantasmic Eruption
features.description:
Beginning at 17th level, you have mastered the art of becoming ethereal and have learned to even make it a part of your fighting style gaining an additional ability for your Phantasmic Flame called Phantasmic Eruption.
Phantasmic Eruption. While you are gaining the benefit of your Ghost Walk feature if you deal damage with a melee attack, you can spend 3 Ghostfire to reach inside your enemies body and release a torrent of phantasmic chakra within them. The affected creature must make a Charisma saving throw vs your Ninjutsu (or Genjutsu) save DC. On a failed save, they have their chakra network burned and overloaded by you as they become Ghostburned.

subclasses.name: Tactical Scout
subclasses.description: Battlefield academics — tactical specialists who turn combat into an exercise in planning, preparation, and overwhelming knowledge.

features.name: Superior Tactics
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level you learn maneuvers that capitalize on your ability to see the why behind the how. The tactics behind the plan. The Big picture beyond this small battle, which are fueled by your Superiority Die.
You learn two maneuvers of your choice, which are detailed in the Tactical Maneuvers Section at the end of this class. You earn more at higher levels as shown in the Maneuvers Known Column of the Scout-Nin Class table. Maneuvers normally enhance your unarmed or Weapon attacks or jutsu you cast in some way. You can use one maneuver per turn as part of an unarmed or weapon attack made with the Attack Action or as part of a Bonus Action attack or as part of a jutsu's casting. Otherwise, a maneuver will detail its Action economy cost.
Additionally, you may select one additional Shinobi Path from the Shinobi's Path class feature.
Finally, once per turn, when an allied creature would make an attack, or cast a Jutsu, you can spend a superiority die. When you do, select one maneuver you know. The chosen attacking or casting creature can apply the effects of the maneuver to their attack or jutsu casting so long as its applicable.

features.name: Master of Tactics
features.description:
Starting at 3rd Level, unlike other Shinobi, your ability to control a combat comes from your tactical acumen.
At the beginning of combat when you roll initiative, you can choose any of the listed skills regardless of circumstance from the Adaptive Initiative class feature, rolling those or your normal initiative at advantage.
Your focus in combat allows you to build up information as a combat continues. At the beginning of each of your turns or once per turn, when you perform a skill-based Action you gain 1 Tactical Insight (Max 4), which is represented as bits and pieces of information that allows you to make better tactical decisions. You can spend this Tactical Insight on the following;
Always Prepared. When you would spend Superiority Die on a class feature that isn't a maneuver, you can spend an equal number of Tactical Insight in their place.
Updated Tactics. On your turn you can spend 1 Tactical Insight to switch which Generalization from Jack of All, you are benefiting from at no action cost.
Follow Through. When you would apply either Follow Up or Set Up from Switch Hitter, you can choose to instead spend 1 Tactical Insight to instead use both.
Tactical Placement. On your turn by spending 1 Tactical Insight, you can reroll whatever you rolled for initiative. At the end of your turn, your position in initiative moves to match the new result. You do not take a second turn in the same round if your result would cause your turn to come up again in the same round.

features.name: Better Than the Best
features.description: Beginning at 6th level, your training has led you down a path unexplored by many. You have made it your job to become the most well-prepared Shinobi in any room you stand in. Select any two maneuvers from the following Scout-Nin subclasses, adding them to your Maneuvers known, not counting it against your known maneuvers limit. You cannot take a maneuver with the Specialized keyword from another subclass. You can select an additional +2 at 14th level; Arbiter Scout, Assault Scout, Cloning Scout, Elemental Scout, Phantom Scout.

features.name: Role Reassignment
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your mastery of the fundamentals allows you to fit into whatever space or role the team needs at moment's notice, you only need a bit of time to prepare. When you complete a rest, you can select one of the following roles gaining its benefits until your next rest.
Infiltrator. You can move through occupied spaces of enemies of the same size or larger and while moving you cannot be heard or detected by traps or alarm systems.
Field Analyst. As a bonus action on your turn, you can select one creature you can see and study them, by making an Insight or Investigation check vs the targets Charisma Save DC. On a success you become aware of the following: Whether its Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution is highest and whether its Wisdom, Intelligence, or Charisma is lowest.
Tactical Swap. On your turn, by spending 1 Superiority Die you can select one willing creature in initiative and swap places with them. At the end of their or your turn (whichever occurs the latest in the turn order), your positions in initiative switches with them. You do not take a second turn in the same round if your result would cause your turn to come up again in the same round.

features.name: Exceeding Expectations
features.description:
Starting at 10th Level, your knowledge and experience has provided you with amplified knowledge on how to handle difficult, if not impossible situations. You can use one additional maneuver per turn And you gain a unique Generalization called Tactician.
Tactician. While you are benefiting from this Generalization you can use the following abilities by spending Tactical Insights.
Lasting Affliction. By spending 2 Tactical Insight; Physical, Mental or Sensory conditions you would inflict gain the [Persists] tag for 1 minute after which they automatically end. All conditions that would normally end at the end of the affected creatures next turns must now be ended by making a Constitution saving throw vs your highest Jutsu save DC at the end of the affected creatures turn.
Always Prepared. Once per turn, when you would inflict a condition you can spend 2 Tactical Insight. When you do, select one Physical, Mental or Sensory condition (Excl. Staggered, Restrained, Lacerated, Berserk, Charmed, and Blinded). You inflict the chosen condition instead of the condition your jutsu or ability would inflict.
Never Caught Off Guard. By spending 2 Tactical Insight, you can use the Superior Momentum class feature to give yourself an additional reaction. This reaction is lost if it is not used before the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Mastered Path
features.description:
At 14th level, others wish they were of your caliber. You may select a third Shinobi Path from the Shinobi's Path class feature.
You may select the Signature Path for the Shinobi Path you selected as a result of the Superior Tactics class feature.

features.name: The Elite
features.description:
At the peak of your training, you have become an elite shinobi. Unpredictable and unmatched in adaptability. You flow between disciplines with ease and very few even see you as normal now, simply calling you The Elite.
Beginning at 17th level, your stamina and ability to push beyond your limits allows you to reduce the Superiority Die cost to use the Superior Momentum class feature by -1
Additionally, when you cast two or more different jutsu with different casting disciplines during a single turn or make a Weapon or Unarmed attack and Cast your Primary casting discipline during the same turn, you regain 1 Superiority Die.
Finally, you can use two additional maneuvers per turn, instead of one.

pickable.editorDescription: Scout-Nin maneuvers, learned via the level-up window. Non-specialized maneuvers are available to any Scout-Nin; specialized ones require the matching Scouting Technique. Use + Custom for homebrew or Tactical's Better-Than-The-Best picks.
pickable.pickerDescription: Scout-Nin Maneuvers scale with level (2 at 3, +1 each at 7/11/15/19). Maneuvers marked as Specialized require your specific Scouting Technique.

options.name: Back Off!
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: all Squad members move up to half speed away from hostiles without provoking; gain DR equal to the Superiority Die result until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Pincer Attack
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die when 2+ Squad members engage the same creature: each engaged Squad-Mate makes an Unarmed/Weapon attack against that creature, adding the Superiority Die to damage. Each successful strike imposes -1 on the target's next save vs a jutsu you cast before end of turn.

options.name: Guards Up!
options.description: Reaction when a visible creature within 30 ft would take damage from a failed save or an attack: spend 1 Superiority Die. They gain bonus to AC or save vs the triggering effect — AC: full result; Save: half result.

options.name: Go All Out
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: select a Squad-Mate and command them to spend their reaction to cast a B-Rank or lower 1-Action jutsu. If cast, add your Warfare bonus to damage; area effects roll half dice (min 1).

options.name: I Know What I'm Doing
options.description: When you attempt a skill-based action, spend a Superiority Die to remove its action cost. Once per turn if in combat.

options.name: Follow My Lead
options.description: Melee attack + 1 Superiority Die: gain your Follow-Up bonus to hit. On hit, apply Set-Up, then select a Squad-Mate to spend their reaction to make the same attack type (or cast same-rank jutsu) against the same target.

options.name: Authoritative Strike
options.description: On hit or on a jutsu save failed: spend 1 Superiority Die. All affected hostile creatures gain 1 rank of Demoralized or Weakened until the start of your next turn.

options.name: Leader's Charge
options.description: Spend 2 Superiority Die as movement begins: +20 ft speed. On a hit at end of movement against an adjacent creature, all Squad-Mates can spend reactions to move full speed and attack/cast C-Rank or lower against the same target, adding the Superiority Die to their damage.

options.name: Shared Willpower
options.description: On hit / jutsu save failed: spend 1 Superiority Die. If you are under a jutsu-based effect requiring repeat saves, a Squad-Mate may make a save using their stats in place of yours; on success the effect ends on you.

options.name: Leader's Arbitration
options.description: Action, 1 Superiority Die: creatures of your choice within 30 ft save WIS or INT (your choice) vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC. On fail, for 1 minute they can't Disengage or lie to you or your allies.

options.name: Amplified Force
options.description: When casting a jutsu forcing your Combat Rival to save vs a condition, spend 1 Superiority Die. On fail, they gain +1 rank of that condition.

options.name: Arresting Strike
options.description: On hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Target saves STR vs your attack DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed). On fail, speed halved, knocked prone, and can't stand without using Bonus Action until end of their next turn.

options.name: Rending Critical
options.description: On a critical hit vs your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. Reduce target's AC and DR by half the Superiority Die result. Rival can't gain jutsu-based bonuses to AC/DR until your next turn.

options.name: Knock Back
options.description: On hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Push target 10 × result feet away, ending prone. If stopped by a structure ≤5 ft thick, destroy it and continue; target takes 2d6 bludgeoning per structure.

options.name: Whirlwind Strike
options.description: On damage to your Combat Rival with an attack: spend 1 Superiority Die. Select up to (result) creatures within 10 ft of target; compare your attack to their AC. Each hit takes half the original damage.

options.name: Menacing Assault
options.description: On damage to your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. Creatures of your choice within 20 ft of you or the target save WIS vs your Attack/Jutsu DC or gain ranks of Demoralized = half Superiority Die result.

options.name: Just You
options.description: On an area jutsu that includes your Combat Rival: spend 1 Superiority Die. The jutsu loses its area and only affects your Rival; they make their save at disadvantage.

options.name: Limit Breaker
options.description: No action cost, spend 1 Superiority Die: until your next turn, can't be reduced below 1 HP by attacks/jutsu; add Superiority Die to first attack each turn; first incoming attack each turn has advantage. Reuse before rest increases cost by +1 each time.

options.name: Relentless Retaliation
options.description: On damage taken by a creature in range: spend 2 Superiority Dice + Reaction. Make an attack or cast a known jutsu at the triggering creature. On hit or failed save, repeat at -4/+4 each subsequent time (cumulative by ±2).

options.name: Staggering Will
options.description: On a failed save that deals damage and inflicts a condition: spend 1 Superiority Die, add result to the save. If this flips to success, hostile creatures within 20 ft save WIS vs your Jutsu DC or Staggered until your next turn.

options.name: Clones Stealth
options.description: When you summon 2+ Bunshin and are within 60 ft of obscuring terrain that can hold them: spend 1 Superiority Die, make a Stealth check vs highest passive Perception, adding Superiority Die result. On success, Bunshin are undetected.

options.name: Clone Swap
options.description: Reaction on damage to yourself or an ally: spend a Superiority Die. A clone/Bunshin within 15 ft swaps places with the target and takes the damage and effects instead.

options.name: Clone Combo
options.description: On attack with at least one Bunshin active: spend 1 Superiority Die. Dismiss any number of Bunshin — each -1 to target AC (max -3) and -2 DR (max -6) for the triggering attack.

options.name: Clone Flank
options.description: When you summon 2+ clones/Bunshin with a hostile within 60 ft: spend 1 Superiority Die. For each Bunshin adjacent to target, -1 to their next STR/DEX/CON save (max -3) until end of your next turn.

options.name: Clone Raid
options.description: When a hostile within 10 ft of a clone/Bunshin casts a jutsu granting HP or temp HP: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Roll 1d8 per clone within 10 ft of them; reduce HP/temp HP gained by result.

options.name: Clone Swarm
options.description: When casting an attack-roll jutsu: each miss may be remade by spending 1 Superiority Die + dismissing a Bunshin. Remade attacks lose advantage.

options.name: Durable Clone
options.description: On damage taken by you or a clone/Bunshin you control: spend a Superiority Die. The affected creature gains DR equal to the result.

options.name: Elite Clone
options.description: When summoning 2+ Bunshin: spend 1 Superiority Die during casting. Summon only one Bunshin instead, with HP = 5 × result; +1 jutsu cast; +1d8 damage once/turn. Max one Elite Bunshin active.

options.name: Cloned Technique
options.description: Select a Bunshin, spend 1 Superiority Die: until end of turn, that Bunshin deals the jutsu's listed damage on hit.

options.name: Clones Support
options.description: Reaction on a save: spend 1 Superiority Die. Dismiss any number of clones/Bunshin; +1 save per dismissed (max +4).

options.name: Elemental Disruption
options.description: On damage to a creature from an attack while in Elemental Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Until start of your next turn, the target's jutsu cost increases by twice the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Elemental Being
options.description: Reaction on damage taken by an attack while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. +AC = result until end of turn. If triggering attacks are melee, deal damage (type = Flux) to attackers until end of turn, equal to twice the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Elemental Malady
options.description: When casting a nature-release jutsu that can inflict an Elemental condition: spend 1 Superiority Die. It inflicts additional ranks of that condition equal to half the Superiority Die result (on fail/hit as usual).

options.name: Heightened Jutsu
options.description: When casting a nature-release jutsu: spend 1 Superiority Die. The jutsu upcasts by +1 rank at no extra cost, ignoring normal limits.

options.name: Natured Jutsu
options.description: When an ally within 30 ft casts a jutsu while you're in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Their jutsu gains your Flux's nature-release keyword and benefits from your Flux's shared bonuses.

options.name: Elemental Chakra-Strike
options.description: Bonus Action, 1 Superiority Die: select a known Ninjutsu/Genjutsu requiring an attack roll. It gains your nature release and is infused until end of your next turn. Next hit deals Weapon/Unarmed damage + jutsu damage; if the jutsu requires a save, target saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Primal Recovery
options.description: Bonus Action while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain temp chakra = 2 × result; regain ability to mold chakra if lost. Flux ends. Requires 10 min to recharge; reuse before recharge +1 cost each time.

options.name: Primal Power
options.description: Spend 2 Superiority Dice. Your next attack is made at disadvantage and can't gain advantage or jutsu-based bonuses. On a hit, treat as a critical hit.

options.name: Primal Enhancement
options.description: When casting a jutsu that reduces damage or grants temp HP while in Flux: spend 1 Superiority Die. Increase the effect by the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Shared Flux
options.description: Action, 1 Superiority Die: transfer Flux to a creature within 30 ft. Your Flux ends; their Flux lasts until your next turn. You can still spend Superiority Dice to activate maneuvers on their behalf during that time.

options.name: Spirit Siphon
options.description: On chakra damage to a creature: spend 1 Superiority Die. Target saves WIS vs attack DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed) or gains 1 rank of Slow. If already suffering a Mental condition, the save is at disadvantage.

options.name: Poltergeist Visage
options.description: In place of an attack from Attack Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. Hostiles within 20 ft save WIS vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or drop weapons, move full speed away; can't cast hand-sign jutsu until their next turn.

options.name: Ghastly Wail
options.description: In place of an attack from Attack Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. 30-ft cone: creatures save CHA vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or gain 2 ranks of Dazzled.

options.name: Phantom Avoidance
options.description: Reaction when hit by an attack or failing a damage save: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain DR = 5 × your current Ghostfire count.

options.name: Ghostfire Claw
options.description: When using Phantom's Grip: spend 1 Superiority Die. If the target would gain Concussed, they also gain 1 rank of Sealed. From Hidden/Unaware, the save is at disadvantage, and on fail: +1 Concussed + 2 Sealed.

options.name: Haunting
options.description: When a creature within 5 ft moves away: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Become invisible, move up to speed ignoring terrain/other creatures' spaces, end as close as possible to triggering creature, invisibility ends, make one weapon attack adding Superiority Die to damage.

options.name: Geist Walk
options.description: When making a save or skill check to pass through a barrier (e.g., Adamantine Barrier, Forcecage): spend 1 Superiority Die, add result to check.

options.name: Shadow Snatch
options.description: When a visible creature within 30 ft would hit an ally with an attack: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Reduce the attack roll by the Superiority Die result.

options.name: Shadow Drain
options.description: When a visible creature within 30 ft gains a bonus to hit or damage: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. They save WIS vs your Ninjutsu/Genjutsu DC or lose the bonus, and you gain it until your next turn.

options.name: Superior Disarming Trip
options.description: On a hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Infuse Disarm, Trip, and Shove into the attack; use your attack roll result in place of each skill check, compared to target's AC.

options.name: Superior Feint
options.description: On a hit: spend 1 Superiority Die. Infuse Demoralize and Feint into the attack; use your attack roll result in place of each skill check, compared to target's WIS Save DC.

options.name: All A Part Of The Plan
options.description: Bonus Action, spend 1 Superiority Die: roll 1d20 and record the result (max 3 stored for 1 min). On a save, substitute your roll with any recorded result.

options.name: Superior Riposte
options.description: When a creature misses you with an attack: Reaction + 1 Superiority Die. Make a melee/ranged weapon attack; on hit, target gains 1 rank of Weakened or Demoralized.

options.name: Superior Suppression
options.description: When a creature under your cast jutsu's effect saves to resist: spend 1 Superiority Die. Reroll the save, taking the lower result. On fail, they gain 1 rank of Demoralized or Sealed.

options.name: Tactical Breakthrough
options.description: On damage: spend 1 Superiority Die. If damage would be reduced by DR or temp HP, target saves CON vs attack/jutsu DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed). On fail, reduce their DR or temp HP by 2 × Superiority Die result until end of turn.

options.name: Tactical Escape
options.description: Reaction on a hostile's Action: spend 1 Superiority Die. Gain Disengage + Dash, move full speed. If you make a STR (Athletics) or DEX (Acrobatics) check before end of turn, add Superiority Die to result.

options.name: Tactical Overwrite
options.description: On an attack targeting a creature: spend 1 Superiority Die. If target would gain reaction-based bonuses to AC, they save Chakra Control vs your attack save DC (Taijutsu if weapon/unarmed) or lose the bonus until end of turn.

options.name: Tactical Parry
options.description: When taking damage and using your reaction to cast a damage-reducing jutsu: spend 1 Superiority Die. Add twice the result to that jutsu's reduction.

options.name: Tactical Strike
options.description: During Attack Action: forgo one attack + spend 1 Superiority Die to mix in a jutsu casting. Cast a C-Rank or lower jutsu that requires at most one attack; then finish your remaining attacks. Reuse on same turn increases cost by +1 per prior use (resets at end of turn).

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

features.name: Martial Technique
features.description:
Beginning at 1st level, your practice of martial arts gives you master of your body in both an offensive and defensive way. While you are wearing no armor, your AC equals 10 + Proficiency + Dexterity Modifier.
Also, choose one Taijutsu stance located in Chapter 13: Customization Options and you gain the following benefits while you are in a Taijutsu Stance or wielding a weapon you are proficient in:
Martial Strength. Your unarmed damage die is a d6. This increases to a d8 at 7th, d10 at 13th level and d12 at 19th levels.
Agile Combat. You can use Strength or Dexterity for the attack and damage rolls and Save DC calculation of your unarmed strikes, Taijutsu and the Martial Arts skill. You can also make one unarmed attack as a Bonus action.
Unwasted Movements. When you use Taijutsu, you waste little energy. Taijutsu you cast have a predetermined base chakra cost based on its rank unless its cost is already Special in which case you use the prewritten Special cost. The following values are treated as Special and cannot be combined with other sources of cost reduction. (D-Rank: 3, C-Rank: 5, B-Rank: 9, A-Rank: 14, S-Rank: 20)

features.name: Enhanced Movement
features.description: Also, at 1st level, you can take the Dash action as a bonus action and your speed is increased by +10. This bonus increases to +15 and your jump distance is doubled when you take the Dash action at 5th level. The bonus to speed increases to +20 at 11th level, +25 at 15 level, and +30 at 20th level.

features.name: Martial Adept
features.description:
Also at 1st level, your training with unarmed techniques have granted you a technique that others of your skill level could only ever pray to emulate. This manifests in the form of Martial die, which are d6. Your martial die grows in size, matching the size of your unarmed damage die based on the Martial Strength ability from the Martial Strength class feature.
At the beginning of each of your turns you manifest a pool of martial die, equal to the number shown in the Martial die column of the Taijutsu specialist class table. You can spend your martial die to complete any number of Martial Techniques or Taijutsu specialist class features. Unspent martial die are lost at the beginning of each of your turns.
You begin with two known martial techniques, learning more as noted in the Martial techniques column of the Taijutsu Specialist class table. Martial Techniques are detailed at the end of this class description and uses your Taijutsu save DC if they require a save of any type.

features.name: Chakra Valves
features.description:
Starting at 2nd Level, your attacks become imbued with your chakra, enhancing each strike into becoming more and more lethal as more chakra is packed behind each blow.
The first time you deal damage on a turn as a result of the Attack action or a Taijutsu you cast, you can use either the Sustained Pressure or Valve Break abilities.
Sustained Pressure. By spending 1 Martial Die, the triggering unarmed attack and every subsequent unarmed attack for the remainder of the turn adds your Martial Die to the damage dealt.
Valve Break. When you cast a Taijutsu that deals damage, you can spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, you add a number of Martial Die to its damage equal to the rank of the jutsu cast. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)

features.name: Physical Genius
features.description:
Starting at 2nd level, your drive, ambition and need to push yourself to your physical limits has bled into how you train, learn and work. You can learn and create Taijutsu in half the time and without spending Ryo to support your lifestyle.
Additionally, you can reduce the final chakra cost of a newly created Taijutsu by an amount equal to its rank. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)
Finally, whenever you would make a saving throw or check to resist gaining ranks of Exhaustion, you make that saving throw or check with advantage.

features.name: Student of Perfection
features.description:
Also at 2nd level, while they were partying, you hardened your fists. While they were making hand signs, you learned to punch holes in steel. While they played mind games with Genjutsu, you kicked the tree 1000 times. Now that they are in battle and they can't take a punch, they have the audacity to come to you. So be it. Select one of the following Paths of Perfection. These paths represent your hard work. Your tenacity and your technique. Some paths interact with Taijutsu stances in unique ways.

PATH OF THE TYRANT
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a tyrant. Someone who takes away an opponent's options for hope. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Brutal Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Brutal keyword. Taijutsu with the Brutal keyword increases the damage they deal by +2. This increases to +4 at 11th level.
Stolen Hope. When a creature attempts to cast a jutsu that has a Hand signs (HS) component while adjacent to you, allows you to take the Attack Action as a reaction targeting them.
Victimizer. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Brutal keyword that inflicts a Physical condition cannot have their saving throw made at advantage.

PATH OF THE PREDATOR
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a predator who is hunting for its prey. Someone who stalks and strikes at their victim until they can no longer run. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Furious Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Furious keyword. Taijutsu with the Furious keyword that deal damage to a target reduces its DR by -2 for each instance of damage dealt. This reduction lasts until end of your next turn.
Can't Run. Each time you deal unarmed damage to a creature, you reduce their speed by 5 feet until the end of your next turn.
Never Escaping. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Furious keyword that targets or affects a creature whose movement speed is 0, can have their first attack per turn made at advantage, or the affected creature makes their first saving throw per turn against them at disadvantage.

PATH OF THE TECHNICIAN
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a perfectionist. Someone who doesn't know the meaning of good enough. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Technical Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Technical keyword. Taijutsu with the Technical keyword that requires concentration allows you to make Martial Arts checks in place of Chakra control checks to maintain concentration.
Unbreakable Focus. Taijutsu you cast cannot have their cost increased by hostile effects.
Perfectionists Musing. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Technical keyword that does not affect hostile creatures, cannot be reacted to or dispelled by jutsu of equal rank to them.

PATH OF THE SAVAGE
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a barbarian. Someone who values inflicting as much harm and collateral damage as possible. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Devastating Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Devastating keyword. Taijutsu with the Devastating keyword gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range.
Unstoppable Force. When a creature you targeted with a Taijutsu spends their reaction to cast a jutsu to summon a construct or structure that would intercept the damage you deal, you can spend your reaction to gain an additional Action, which you can only use to recast the same jutsu, targeting the same creature.
Fueled By Ruin. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Devastating keyword that scores a critical hit has half of its chakra cost refunded, as you regain temporary chakra equal to half the chakra cost spent on the jutsu cast.

PATH OF THE HERO
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a protector or guardian. Someone who values protecting the weak over displaying strength. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Protective Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Protective keyword. Taijutsu with the Protective keyword grants an allied creature within a 10-foot radius, other than its caster, a +1 bonus to their AC for each instance of damage it deals (Max +2)
Guardian Force. When you cast a Taijutsu as a reaction to taking damage or making a saving throw, all allied creatures within a 10-foot radius, other than yourself. They gain the benefits of your Taijutsu for its duration, so long as they remain within 10 feet of you.
Champion. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Protective keyword grants all affected allied creatures 10 temporary hit points for each instance of damage dealt (Max 20)

PATH OF THE GENIUS
You adjust your Taijutsu stance to embody the fighting style of a prodigy. Someone whose skills and technique comes along once in a generation to usher in a new form of fighting. While you are in a Taijutsu stance you can achieve the following;
Elite Taijutsu. Once per turn, a Taijutsu you cast gain the Elite keyword. Taijutsu with the Elite keyword gain a +1 bonus to attack rolls.
The New Era. Increase the Taijutsu save DC of Taijutsu you cast that forces a creature to make a saving throw, by +1.
Next Generation. Beginning at 9th level, Taijutsu you cast with the Elite keyword that forces a creature to make a Chakra control check to maintain concentration, can instead force the target to make an Athletics check.

features.name: Taijutsu Style
features.description: At 3rd level you learn a Combat style that fits in with how you view combat and encompasses your overall approach towards Taijutsu. Your Style grants you feature at 3rd Level and again at 6th, 10th, 14th and 17th Level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
Additionally at 11th level, when you take the attack action, you may as a bonus action make 2 unarmed attacks.

features.name: Moment of Stillness
features.description:
Also at 5th level, you've learned a secret technique capable to halting even the most physically dominant opponent in their tracks. The technique you learn is one that is informed by your form of combat. Select one of three different Martial Techniques that you learn that enable your style of fighting. This cannot be changed later. These techniques can all be triggered when you score an unarmed or Taijutsu attack that deals unarmed damage and spending 2 Martial Dice.
If triggered as a result of a Taijutsu, if the Taijutsu you cast would already force a saving throw, you replace it with this Martial Techniques saving throw, applying both the Martial technique and the jutsu's effects on a failed save.
You may select a second Martial Technique from this list when you reach 11th level.

BONE BREAKER
All it takes is a single strike to rattle an enemies skeletal structure, breaking them from the inside out, making it difficult to take physical actions. When you use this technique When you do, they must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, they are Bone Broken. Once you use this Martial Technique you cannot do so again until the end of your next turn.

BALANCE BREAKER
All it takes is a single strike to shatter an enemies balance, knocking them off guard, as they find it difficult to even stand up straight. When you would score an unarmed or Taijutsu attack that deals unarmed damage, you can spend 2 Martial dice. When you do, they must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they are Balance Broken. Once you use this Martial Technique you cannot do so again until the end of your next turn.

FLOW BREAKER
All it takes is a single strike to completely break an enemies flow, stopping them in their tracks and bringing them to their knees. When you would score an unarmed or Taijutsu attack that deals unarmed damage, you can spend 2 Martial dice. When you do, they must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they are Flow Broken. Once you use this Martial Technique you cannot do so again until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Your unarmed damage die is a d6. This increases to a d8 at 7th, d10 at 13th level and d12 at 19th levels.

features.name: Iron Body
features.description:
Also at 7th level, you learn how to blend your martial prowess with your bodies physical resilience, granting you a strong physique. You gain a rank of Mastery with Constitution saving throws. This increases to two ranks at 13th level.
Additionally, your physical resilience is further represented as Guard Slots, of which you have 2. When you complete a Full-Rest, you can select any 2 minor armor seals found in Chapter 7: Adventuring & Missions, Armor Enhancement Seals. While you are unarmored, you gain the benefits of the chosen armor seals until your next Full-Rest. You can select +1 additional minor armor seal at 9th level. When you reach 13th level, you can select 1 greater or refined armor seal of your choice.

features.name: Agile
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level, your instinctive agility lets you move on instinct alone. You gain a bonus to your initiative equal to the highest modifier between your Intelligence, Wisdom or Charisma.
Also, when you make a Dexterity save to take only half damage, you instead take no damage and suffer no effects on a success and only half damage if you fail.
Additionally, you can also spend a bonus action to take the Disengage Action. This becomes Disengage and the Dodge Action at 13th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Iron Will
features.description:
Beginning at 9th level, most shinobi can train their muscles, but they cannot train their mind or will like you. You gain a rank of Mastery with Wisdom saving throws. This increases to two ranks at 15th level.
Additionally, once per turn, you would make a saving throw to resist a jutsu with the Fuinjutsu keyword, you can spend 1 Martial Die, adding it to the result.

features.name: Expert of the Fist
features.description:
Beginning at 11th level, you have mastered the art of weaving your special techniques into your flow of combat. You can use up to two Martial Techniques per Trigger.
Additionally, you become an Expert in one Taijutsu stance you know. Select one Taijutsu Stance Expertise based on a Taijutsu stance you know found in Chapter 13: Customization Options

features.name: Extra Attack (upgrade)
features.description: Additionally at 11th level, when you take the attack action, you may as a bonus action make 2 unarmed attacks.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Your unarmed damage die is a d6. This increases to a d8 at 7th, d10 at 13th level and d12 at 19th levels.

features.name: Persistence
features.description:
Starting at 13th level, you don't know how to or when to give up, and it only makes it harder to stop you. When you would make a saving throw against an effect that would inflict ranks of a Physical or Sensory condition, you can spend 1 Martial Die adding it to your result.
And if you would begin your turn with a Physical or Sensory condition, you can spend 3 Martial Die to end one of them.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Martial Strength (upgrade)
features.description: Your unarmed damage die is a d6. This increases to a d8 at 7th, d10 at 13th level and d12 at 19th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Mastery of Self
features.description:
Beginning at 20th level, your intense training has brought on a mastery of self that cannot be emulated or replicated.
• You can use each ability granted in your Chakra Valves class feature at no Martial Die cost.
• You gain immunity to the Envenomed, Exhaustion and Dazed conditions.
• When you make a Constitution or Wisdom save to take only half damage, you instead take no damage and suffer no effects on a success and only half damage if you fail.
• You gain a Rank of Mastery in Constitution and Wisdom saving throws.

subclasses.name: Disturbance
subclasses.description: Trickster brawlers who overwhelm with multi-angle flurries and combo-breaking strikes — capable of halting even the most focused opponents mid-cast.

features.name: Disturbance Flurry
features.description:
When you choose this style, starting at 3rd level, you learn to break your opponent's combat flow using your mastery over combos and repeated strikes but almost exclusively when they act. Once per turn, when you deal unarmed damage to a hostile creature as a result of an attack or a Taijutsu you cast, you gain 1 Momentum (Max 4) and last for 1 minute. Momentum is represented as your ability to exchange your own combat momentum in order to interrupt your nemesis. You can spend momentum you have on the following abilities.
Blitzing Momentum. By spending 1 Momentum on your turn, you increase your movement speed by +10 feet and until the beginning of your next turn, when you take a Reaction, you can move 10 feet as part of the reaction. If your reaction would already grant you movement as part of it, you increase the distance of that movement by +10 feet.
Surging Momentum. Once per turn, by spending 2 Momentum on your turn, you gain an additional Bonus action, which can only be used to cast a Taijutsu of D-Rank or lower. This becomes C-Rank at 10th level and B-Rank at 17th level. This additional bonus action lasts until the end of current turn. If it is not used, it is lost.
Disturbing Momentum. Once per turn, by spending 2 Momentum on a hostile creatures turn, you gain an additional Reaction, which can only be used to cast a Taijutsu or use a Martial Technique. This additional reaction lasts until the end of the current turn. If it is not used, it is lost.

features.name: Blinding Speed
features.description:
When you choose this Style, starting at 3rd level, you focus on overwhelming your enemies with more than they could conceivably handle at one time. You learn to accomplish this through a series of Martial Techniques, unique to you and your style of combat. You learn the following Martial Techniques.
Shatter. When a hostile creature casts a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu, you may, as a reaction, spend 1 Martial Die to move up to half your speed, ending your movement as close as possible to the triggering creature. If you end this movement in range of the triggering creature, you can make two unarmed attacks. If at least one of those attacks are successful, the affected creature must make a Chakra control check vs your Taijutsu save DC. On a failed save you Interrupt their jutsu's casting. On a success, the jutsu is cast normally.
Unstable Drive. When a hostile creature triggers an attack of opportunity from you, in place of the attack made as part of the attack of opportunity, you may spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, you can instead cast a D-Rank Taijutsu targeting them. This becomes C-Rank at 10th and B-Rank at 17th level.

features.name: Unpredictable
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, why block a strike when you can simply be somewhere else? You fight not just with speed, but with the mind of your foe, bending their expectations until they strike shadows.
Creatures cannot target you with attacks of opportunity or attacks made as a result of a reaction they take.
Additionally, creatures that cast a Ninjutsu or Genjutsu while adjacent to you, they trigger an attack of opportunity from you.

features.name: Conversational Feint
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your words are weapons. You can mask an attack in banter, disguise a truth in jest, and strike at an enemy's certainty with nothing more than a sentence. .
As a Reaction to a creature passing a Charisma check when in a social encounter, you can attempt a Social Feint.
Social Feint. Make a Martial Arts or Deception check vs the targets Wisdom Save DC. On a success, you gain the benefit both; a Lie and misdirecting the enemy, causing them to lose whatever benefit they were attempting to gain from the triggering social action.

features.name: Unmatched Reflexes
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, the maximum Momentum you can maintain increases to 6. You also learn to utilize your momentum in the following ways.
Overwhelming Momentum. Once per turn, when a creature makes their saving throw against a Taijutsu you cast, you can spend 1 Momentum and 1 Martial Die. When you do, the affected creature cannot take reactions against the Taijutsu's castings or damage unless they take on 2 ranks of Dazzled.
Imperceivable Momentum. When a hostile creature, that you can reach with your movement, casts a Ninjutsu or Taijutsu, you can spend your reaction and 1 Momentum and 1 Martial Die. When you do, you move with an imperceptible speed, teleporting into a space that can hold you, within 5 feet of them. As part of this reaction, you can also use another class feature that costs a reaction to use.
Debilitating Momentum. When you score two or more attacks as part of the same action, against the same creature you can spend 2 Momentum and 2 Martial Die. When you do, you force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC. On a failed save, the creature becomes Vulnerable to the next instance of damage they take from a creature, other than yourself, until the beginning of your next turn

features.name: Close the Distance
features.description: At 14th Level when you take damage from a ranged attack, you can spend any number of Momentum you have, reducing the damage taken by 1d10 and moving 10 feet for every momentum spent. When you move this way, you must move closer to the source of the ranged damage.

features.name: Disturbing Technique
features.description: Beginning at 17th Level, even the boldest shinobi whisper of you, saying to never face you without backup. But even backup won't save them. When you would cast a Taijutsu that requires you to make 2 or more attacks and there are at least two or more hostile creatures within the jutsu's range, you may spend 3 Martial Die, when you do, all hostile creatures in range become targets of your Taijutsu attacks, applying the results of the jutsu's attacks against each creatures AC.

subclasses.name: Nin-Tai
subclasses.description: Elemental-infused martial artists — they coat their body in nature-release chakra and blend Taijutsu with Ninjutsu finishers.

features.name: Nature Enhanced Combat
features.description:
When you choose this style starting at 3rd level, you have focused your effort on harnessing your innate element. If you have access to one or more nature releases already, select one. It becomes your chosen nature release that this and other feature from this subclass references.
If you do not have access to one, you may select one (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning Release). You gain the ability to learn and cast jutsu with the corresponding keyword.
Taijutsu you cast gains the chosen nature release keyword, if they don't have one already.
By spending 1 Martial Dice, as a Bonus Action, you enhance your body with the Chakra of your chosen Nature release putting you into your Elemental Cloak state for the next minute or until you spend a bonus action to end it early. Once you enter this state, you cannot do so again until you spend at least 10 minutes of uninterrupted meditating.

ELEMENTAL CLOAK (Fortitude Amp)
Taijutsu you cast deals its nature release's associated damage type, and your chosen nature release grants your cloak different benefits:
  • Earth Release. You gain +4 DR. This DR cannot be reduced by any means. This becomes +6 at 10th and +8 at 17th level.
  • Wind Release. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, and damage you deal with Taijutsu penetrates 2 of a creature's DR. When you reach 10th level, you gain immunity to the Grappled and Restrained conditions. Finally, when you reach 17th level, your bonuses become +2 to AC and penetrate 4 DR.
  • Fire Release. You gain a rank of Mastery to [Unarmed Damage]. This becomes two ranks of Mastery at 10th and three ranks at 17th level.
  • Water Release. Your unarmed attacks gain the Reach 1 and Trip weapon properties. Beginning at 10th level, the first time each turn you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature you can move them up to 10 feet in any direction of your choice. Finally, at 17th level, creatures cannot gain jutsu-based bonuses to their saving throws vs Taijutsu you cast.
  • Lightning Release. Your unarmed and Taijutsu attacks gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range and a +10 to speed. Beginning at 10th level, when you score a critical with an unarmed attack, you may add 1 unarmed damage die to the result. Finally, at 17th level, [Unarmed Damage] you deal cannot have its damage intercepted, always affecting its original target.

features.name: Burst Techniques
features.description:
Also, at 3rd level, you learn to harness your elemental chakra to enhance your combat potential. You learn to accomplish this through a series of Martial Techniques, unique to you and your style of combat. You learn the following Martial Techniques.
Elemental Rush. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee Taijutsu attack you can spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, you can spend your bonus action to cast 1 D-Rank Ninjutsu with the same nature release whose casting time is 1 Action, using your Taijutsu ability modifier in place of your Ninjutsu ability modifier. The Ninjutsu ranks you can cast as part of this feature increases to C-Rank at 10th level and B-Rank at 17th level.
Elemental Crush. When you cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword and are able to utilize its Finisher effects, you can spend 1 Martial Die to instead use your Elemental Finisher in its place. You still spend the chakra cost as if casting the Jutsu, but you apply your Elemental Finishers effects in its place. This is still treated as a Finisher. You can use this Martial Technique once per turn.

features.name: Elemental Defenses
features.description: Starting at 6th level, you have learned to utilize your chakra cloak to recharge itself from impacts dealing with your chosen nature release. While you are in your Elemental Cloak state, you gain resistance to your nature releases associated damage type.

features.name: Primal Glare
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your voice carries like a thunderclap, but your eyes radiate a primal force like that of an erupting volcano, or a raging storm. As an Action while in a social encounter, you can attempt a Primal Glare targeting a single creature.
Primal Glare. Make a Chakra Control check vs the targets Wisdom Save DC. On a success, they gain 2 ranks of Demoralized and are unable to lie or withhold important information when asked their next question. Once used, a creature cannot be affected by this feature for 1 hour.

features.name: Unchecked Power
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, at this stage in your training, you have deepened your connection to the elemental chakra that cloaks your body, allowing you to channel its power with greater precision and variety. You gain the following additional Martial Techniques.
Elemental Core. When you are forced to make a saving throw against a Ninjutsu, you may spend 1 Martial Die adding it to the result of your saving throw. If the jutsu cast has the same nature release as your Elemental Cloak you regain the spend Martial Die at the end of the current turn.
Ninjutsu Combo. When you cast a Ninjutsu, you may spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, you may cast it using your Taijutsu ability modifier and it gains the Combo keyword and the following effect language; Until the end of your turn, you can target one affected creature with a Taijutsu Finisher regardless of range, once per turn, using either an Action or Bonus action, ignoring its listed casting time.
Flashguard Counter. When you cast a jutsu in reaction to damage you take, you may spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, you collapse your elemental energy around yourself, manifesting a brief shield of energy, gaining temporary hit points equal to 5 times the result of the martial die. If the jutsu cast would grant you temporary hit points, you add the result of this technique to the jutsu's temporary hit points gained, but you lose all temporary hit points at the end of the current turn.

features.name: Elemental Recharge
features.description: Beginning at 14th level, you have learned to convert your Chakra into the energy needed to keep going. As a bonus action on your turn, by spending 4 Martial Die you gain an additional Action this turn, which can only be used to cast a Taijutsu. Once you use this feature you cannot do so again until you spend a Full-Turn Action to stabilize your body.

features.name: Nin-Tai Technique
features.description:
Starting at 17th level, your technique is so overwhelmingly powerful that your enemies speak of you as if you are a force of nature. Something that is unavoidable and if encountered, can only be weathered.
• You enter your Elemental Cloak when initiative is rolled, at no Martial Die cost.
• Difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.
• While you have your reaction, the first time each turn when a creature within 10 feet of you hits you with an attack, it takes damage equal to 4 Martial Die, of a type equivalent to your Elemental Cloak.
• When you score a hit with a melee Taijutsu or Unarmed attack, you can select a space you can see within your speeds range, teleporting to that space. You can do this once per successful attack.

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning). If you already have multiple releases, the picker excludes ones you already own.

subclasses.name: Passionate
subclasses.description: Relentless combo masters with unbreakable rhythm — green jumpsuits, weighted clothing, and a style built around tempo and Finisher crescendos.

features.name: Never Ending Combo
features.description:
When you choose this Style starting at 3rd level, your body moves with unstoppable rhythm, every strike flowing into the next like crashing waves, with each blow fueling the next, building an unrelenting tempo that drives you toward a single, devastating finish.
When you cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword, you enter a state of combat rhythm you call your DYNAMIC FORM!. Which lasts until you cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword or initiative ends, whichever happens first. While in your DYNAMIC FORM! state, each time you cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword, you gain 1 Tempo (Max 3). You lose 1 Tempo at the end of each of your turns, where you didn't cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword. While you have Tempo you gain passive bonuses based on the number of Tempo you have. When you cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword, you immediately spend all accumulated Tempo, gaining cumulative bonuses based on the amount of Tempo spent.
Passive Tempo Benefits. While you have tempo, you gain the following benefits.
• 1 Tempo. While you have 1 tempo, you gain a rank of Mastery with Athletics and Acrobatics based skill actions and you can ignore difficult terrain.
• 2 Tempo. While you have 2 tempo, you can move through enemy spaces, and attacks of opportunities against you are made at disadvantage.
• 3 Tempo. While you have 3 tempo, you gain a +1 bonus to Taijutsu attacks critical threat range and your Taijutsu save DC.
Finisher Tempo Benefits. When you cast a Taijutsu finisher, you spend all tempo you have, gaining cumulative benefits for each tempo spent.
• 1 Tempo. When you spend 1 tempo, you can use one Martial Technique at no Martial Die cost.
• 2 Tempo. When you spend 2 tempo, if the jutsu requires an attack, you can now compare the attack results to the AC of all creatures in a 5-foot radius around the original target, affecting them with the jutsu as well on a successful hit. This increases to 10-feet at 10th level and 15 feet at 17th level.
• 3 Tempo. When you spend 3 tempo, if you score a critical hit or the affected creature fails their saving throw by 5 or more, you regain the action spent casting the jutsu.

features.name: Passionate Flurry
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, a master of relentless engagement, you have refined your fighting style into an unbroken chain of calculated strikes and precision movements. In the hands of a true martial artist, even the simplest movement becomes part of an unstoppable assault.
Once per turn, in place of an unarmed attack made with the attack action, you can use either a Shove, Trip or Grapple skill action.
You also learn to use a series of Martial Techniques, unique to you and your style of combat. You learn the following Martial Techniques.
Chakra Enhanced Blows. You channel Chakra into your hands enhancing your blows even further. When you make an unarmed attack or cast a Taijutsu, you can spend 1 Martial Die, selecting either Sustained Pressure or Valve Break following their effect requirements and applying its effect at no additional Martial Die cost. Additionally, Creatures who fail their saving throw against the Taijutsu cast or are hit two or more times by your unarmed attacks, gain a rank of Bruised.
Tempo Boost. While you have Tempo, when you cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword, you can spend 1 Martial Die. When you do, the jutsu can still be casts ignoring its listed casting time, but it loses the Finisher keyword, instead gaining the Combo Extender keyword. You do not benefit from the jutsu's finisher effect.

features.name: Unreasonable Training
features.description:
Starting at 6th Level, through relentless discipline and a lifetime of unconventional conditioning, you have forged your body into a weapon. But what others don't know, is that everything you've done until now, has been you restrained by self-imposed limiters. You now have the confidence to unleash those. You have been using the following training implements and can utilize them in combat. Select one of the following training implements, gaining it. You may select a second one at 14th level.
Binding Hand Wraps. Special weighted chakra enhanced wraps that you use to band your hands, arms, legs or feet during training. These wraps are treated as a martial weapon, that you are proficient in with the following traits; Light, Disarm, Reach 1, Grapple, Unarmed, Hidden. If you would perform a Taijutsu Finisher against a creature grappled by these hand wraps, you can select a number of damage die rolled, up to half of your proficiency bonus, and treat them as the maximum value.
Training Weights. You have been training under the strain of 500lb weighted gear, forcing your body to move with explosive potential when freed from its duress. As a bonus action you may drop your weights, removing them for 1 minute. While unweighted, your movement speed increases by +20 feet and the first attack you make each turn creates an afterimage from the sheer speed at which you strike. When you make a melee unarmed or Taijutsu attack, you may immediately make an unarmed attack. This attack does not add your ability modifier to damage. After one minute, your muscle cramp up and tire, requiring you to re-weight them for at least 10 minute before unweighting them again.
Specialized Training Kit. You carry a unique set of training tools that you've learned to use via muscle memory. You have proficiency in this kit. You may select two toolkits found in Chapter 5: Equipment. Your specialized Training kit counts as both of the selected toolkits and has 5 charges to utilize per rest. It grows in usefulness as you grow in level, being treated as Greater kits at 10th level, and Superior kits at 14th level.

features.name: Dramatic Pose!
features.description:
Also at 6th Level, you've mastered the art of making an impression so bold that it shifts the flow of conversation or even battle. Whether it's a heartfelt compliment, an exaggerated bow, or an impossibly cool stance, your sheer presence draws focus and changes hearts.
While in a social encounter, as an Action, you may choose a creature whom can see and hear you as you strike a pose of your description while attempting one of the following, of which, you can use once per creature, per social encounter. If you are in combat, you may use these abilities as a Bonus Action, but only once per initiative.
• Encourage. The target adds your Martial Die to their next Charisma or Wisdom based check.
• Flatter. The target is overwhelmed by your flattery. They suffer a penalty to their next Wisdom check equal to your Martial Die.
• Callout. You challenge your target, calling them out on their most recent statement or bluff. The target must make a Charisma Saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC. On a failed save, they lose the benefit of their most recently successful Charisma or Wisdom check within the last minute.

features.name: Can't Stop, Won't Stop
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, at this stage in your training, your DYNAMIC FORM! has become second nature, your tempo shifts and surges like a tide under your command. While in your DYNAMIC FORM! state, your maximum tempo increases to 5 and you no longer need to spend all your Tempo when you cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword; instead, you can choose to spend any amount of Tempo up to your current total.
Passive Tempo Benefits. While you have tempo, you gain the following benefits as well as the previous tempo benefits.
• 5 Tempo. You have advantage on all Dexterity Saving throws.
Finisher Tempo Benefits. When you cast a Taijutsu finisher and spend all tempo you have, gaining all previous and the following tempo benefits.
• 5 Tempo. At the end of the current turn, you regain Chakra points equal to the total amount of chakra you spent since the beginning of the current turn.

features.name: Sheer Will
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, your determination is as unshakable as the mountains, and your body responds with explosive precision even under the heaviest pressure.
When you fail a saving throw, you can spend 2 Tempo to remake the saving throw and when a creature would make a melee attack against you, you can choose to spend 2 Martial Die, to cast a Taijutsu with the casting time of 1 Action, as a reaction.

features.name: Passionate Flame Technique
features.description: Beginning at 17th level, your passion and drive is so potent that you have weaponized this passion into a flurry of strikes that cripple foes and help build your legend, expanding your techniques alure. When you cast a Taijutsu that has the Finisher keyword, you may spend 3 Martial die. When you do, you may gain the benefits of your Tempo Boost martial technique while still applying the jutsu's Finisher effects.

subclasses.name: Stancer
subclasses.description: Style collectors and stance-blenders — weaving multiple Taijutsu stances so quickly opponents can't predict the rhythm.

features.name: Stance Blending
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level, select one Taijutsu Stance found in Chapter 13: Customization Options that you do not know as you learn it as well as the Expert feat associated with the Taijutsu stance. The feat gained in this way does not increase your ability score.
When you make an Unarmed or Melee Taijutsu attack, at the conclusion of the attack, you may switch stances instead of spending a bonus action.
Once per turn, when you swap to any stance, you gain a Stance Shift benefit of your choice from the following list. These benefits last until the beginning of your next turn;
• Impact Switch. Each time you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature you can push them 5 feet into any direction of your choice (Excluding vertically).
• Defensive Switch. Each time you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature, roll 1 Martial Die, gaining the result as temporary hit points.
• Retaliatory Switch. Hostile creatures that cast a Jutsu with the Hand Sign (HS) component while adjacent to you, triggers an attack of opportunity at no reaction cost, once per turn. If you score a hit with an unarmed attack of opportunity the affected creature must make a Strength saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC, on a failed save, they are knocked prone.
• Deflective Switch. The first time each turn you take damage from an attack, roll 1 Martial Die, reducing the damage you take by the result.

features.name: Fluid Stance
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, your technique is weaponized to drift between a myriad of fighting styles to defeat your enemies.
You learn to accomplish this through a series of Martial Techniques, unique to you and your style of combat. You learn the following Martial Techniques.
Style Shift. When a creature makes an attack targeting you, you may as a reaction spend 1 Martial die. When you do, you may switch your Taijutsu stance and make an unarmed attack against the triggering creature. On a hit, you force the target creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, they become Dazed.
Dodge Cancel. When you hit a creature with a melee Taijutsu or Unarmed attack, you can spend 1 Martial die. When you do, you can switch your Taijutsu stance and take a defensive stance. The next attack the damaged creature makes against you until the beginning of your next turn is made at disadvantage.

features.name: Secret Stances
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your study of martial arts and their respective stances have become all encompassing, allowing you to make use of the knowledge mid combat. You may select one of the following Clans, learning a modified version of their specific Taijutsu Stance. This is treated as a Taijutsu stance.
Hanami Clan: Medical Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal becomes Necrotic and once per turn when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature as a result of an Unarmed attack while in this stance, you can force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC gaining a rank of Envenomed on a failed save.
Hyuga Clan: Gentle Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal becomes Force and once per turn when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature as a result of an Unarmed attack while in this stance, you can force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC gaining a rank of Sealed on a failed save.
Uchiha Clan: Agile Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal becomes Piercing and once per turn when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature as a result of an Unarmed attack while in this stance, you can force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC gaining a rank of Confused on a failed save.
Ryu Clan: Dragon Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal becomes Chakra and once per turn when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature as a result of an Unarmed attack while in this stance, you can force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC gaining a rank of Weakened on a failed save.
Kuru Clan: Dark Devotion Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal becomes Psychic and once per turn when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature as a result of an Unarmed attack while in this stance, you can force them to make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu save DC gaining a rank of Slow. Ranks of slow gained in this way are treated as a Physical conditions.

features.name: Mimicry of Motion
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your obsession with watching, studying and internalizing martial stances extends beyond combat. You instinctively mirror posture, tone and body language in conversation.
While in a social encounter, as a Reaction when a creature succeeds on their social check, you can quickly imitate a creature you can see and hear. Make a Martial Arts check vs the creatures Charisma Save DC. On a success, for the remainder of this social encounter, when making Charisma based checks, you can use their bonuses in place of your own. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until at least 1 hour have passed.

features.name: Stance Mastery
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, through relentless training, your mastery of martial stances has reached a new height. Where others must reset their footing, you weave one stance into the next with an ease that borders on artistry.
You may select one Taijutsu Stance found in Chapter 13: Customization Options that you do not know as you learn it. Additionally, you gain the following additional Stance Shift options to use.
• Momentum Switch. You gain the benefit of the Dash action.
• Anchored Switch. Each time you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature, reduce their speed by 10 feet until the end of their next turn.
• Distracting Switch. Hostile creatures you deal damage to for the remainder of the turn grant your allies advantage to their next attack targeting them until the beginning of your next turn.
• Resilient Switch. Select one damage type of your choice, gaining resistance to it until the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Ancient Scrolls
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, your relentless pursuit of martial mastery has unlocked the hidden depths of ancient clan traditions. What were once glimpses into their signature techniques have become living extensions of your own style. First, select a clan from the Secret Stances feature, as you learn their Taijutsu Stance as well.
Second, the original Taijutsu stance you selected for Secret Stances, has becomes interwoven into your technique in a far more natural way, going from simple mimicry, to actual innovation. The original stance evolves into a Prestige stance gaining their listed effects.
Hanami Clan Prestige: Medical Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal to creatures with ranks of Envenomed also reduces the next instance of damage they deal by 1 Martial Die up to a max penalty of 2 Martial Die.
Hyuga Clan Prestige: Gentle Fist. [Unarmed Damage] you deal to creatures with ranks of Sealed increases the cost of their next jutsu cast by 1 Martial Die up to a max penalty of 2 Martial Die.
Uchiha Clan: Agile Fist. Once per turn, when you make an unarmed attack targeting a creature with ranks of Confused roll 1 Martial Die. On a roll of 6 or higher, they cannot react to your attacks or unarmed damage dealt by the attacks until the end of the current turn.
Ryu Clan: Dragon Fist. As a reaction to a creature with ranks of Weakened dealing damage to you, you can make an Unarmed attack against a hostile creature in range. On a hit, you gain temporary chakra equal to 1 Martial Die
Kuru Clan: Dark Devotion Fist. Once per turn, when you deal [Unarmed Damage] to a creature with ranks of Slow you can spend 1 Martial Die. If you do, you consume 1 rank of slow, reducing their current ranks by 1 as you gain 1 additional Bonus Action, which can only be used to take the Dash, Disengage or Dodge actions.

features.name: Stancer Technique
features.description: Beginning at 17th Level, your style is so unique and graceful that your enemies speak of you as if you were the originator of the different stances and styles you've blended. When you would cast a Taijutsu that requires a Taijutsu stance, you may spend 2 martial die, until the end of the current turn, attacks you make or saving throws you force a creature to make ignores bonuses to AC as a result of jutsu if the Taijutsu you cast required an attack, and forces a hostile creature to remake a successful save if the Taijutsu cast forced a saving throw.

subclasses.name: Talented Genius
subclasses.description: Prodigies with instinctive self-weaponization — Perfection-point spenders whose mastery borders on effortless perfection.

features.name: Genius Talent
features.description:
When you choose this Style starting at 3rd level, unlike other Taijutsu specialist, you focus on magnifying your already spectacular skills and talents. Unlike others, your genius is natural and it becomes more pronounced as you grow in your role.
When you roll initiative, your body naturally drifts into a state of instinctual self-weaponization known as becoming Flawless. You remain in this state for the duration of combat, exiting it when combat ends. While in your Flawless state you gain access to Perfection. Once per turn, when you spend at least 1 Martial Die, you gain a Perfection, of which, you can maintain up to 4. You can spend Perfection on the following abilities. You can only use each
Flawless Disturbance. Once per turn, by spending 2 Perfection on a hostile creatures turn, you gain an additional reaction, which can only be used to cast a Taijutsu. This additional reaction lasts until the end of the current turn. You can gain 1 reaction in this way until the beginning of your next turn.
Flawless Focus. Once per turn, you can spend 1 Perfection in place of any 1 Martial Die expenditure. When you do, you still roll your Martial Die as required, but it is not spent from your pool.
Flawless Stances. Once per turn, by spending 1 Perfection, you can switch your current Taijutsu Stance, in place of spending your Bonus action. When you do, the next attack that targets you before the beginning of your next turn is made at disadvantage.
Flawless Technique. When you would roll a Martial Die you can spend 1 Perfection. Roll your Martial Die at advantage, taking the higher result, and applying it to the triggering effect.
Flawless Tempo. Once per turn, when you cast a Taijutsu with the Combo keyword, you can spend 2 Perfection. When you do, the next Taijutsu you cast gains the Combo Extender keyword from the Passionate Taijutsu specialist subclass and you can cast a Taijutsu with the Finisher keyword as a Reaction until the end of your current turn.

features.name: Focused Genius
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, your talent and focus is weaponized to completely drive home the point that it doesn't matter how much your enemies train to overcome you, they are out of their league and they need to come to grips with this new reality they find themselves in.
You learn to accomplish this through a series of Martial Techniques, unique to you and your style of combat. You learn the following Martial Techniques.
Redirected Aggression. When a hostile creature makes a melee or ranged attack, you may as a reaction, spend one Martial die reducing their attack by the result. If their attack would miss, you can redirect it back towards them by making a melee or ranged unarmed attack, using the missed attacks original range as your attacks range. On a hit, the creature suffers the effects of their original attack plus 1 Martial Die in additional damage.
Shatterpoint. When you deal [Unarmed Damage], you can spend 1 Martial die. When you do, the affected creature must make a Strength Saving throw. On a failed save, they become Shattered.

features.name: Perfected Stillness
features.description:
Beginning at 6th level, your combat prowess far surpasses everyone else who thinks they are your equal. When you would use a Martial Technique granted by the Moment of Stillness class feature, you can do so by only spending 1 Martial Die instead of 2.
