Additionally, the condition inflicted no longer ends of the end of the affected creatures turn, instead requiring a successful resave at the end of each of the affected creatures turns to end its effects. This resave cannot be made at disadvantage.

features.name: Pressure of the Talented
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your brilliance radiates in ways that cannot be ignored. Conversations bend around you, as if every word spoken must first pass through the weight of your presence.
When in a social encounter, your presence is an aura of magnificence that puts pressures on everyone involved that isn't on your side. Roll 4 Martial Die, dividing each result in half (min 1) and recording each. You can choose a recorded result and penalize a creatures Charisma or Wisdom based check with it. You do not add additional dice results to your recorded results when you exhaust your current results. You can only record the Martial Die results in this way once per encounter.

features.name: Expanded Talent
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, your mastery of instinct and insight elevates your genius talent into something beyond mortal precision. Your maximum Perfection increases by 2. You unlock new ways to spend Perfection, pushing your combat to impossible heights.
Flawless Power. By spending 2 Perfection and a Bonus action, you coat yourself in a surge of chakra magnifying your physical capabilities. Until the beginning of your next turn, you gain a +2 to your AC and the first creature to make a saving throw against your Martial Techniques are suffer a penalty to their saving throw equal to half the result of 1 Martial Die.
Flawless Execution. When you score a critical hit with an unarmed or Taijutsu attack, you can spend 1 Perfection to make an additional unarmed attack as apart of the same action.
Flawless Balance. Once per turn, when you would be moved against your will or knocked prone, you can spend 2 Perfection to instead not be knocked prone or be moved against your will. This does not prevent teleportation.
Flawless Guard. When an allied creature, other than yourself, within 15 feet of you would be hit by an attack, you may spend 1 Perfection and a Reaction. If you do, you can move up to 10 feet, ending that movement adjacent to the allied creature and reducing the damage they take by 2 Martial Die.
Flawless Clash. Once per turn, when you would make a Clash check with a Taijutsu you cast, you can spend 1 Perfection as part of the check. When you do you can add 1 Martial Die to the result.

features.name: Perfect Physique
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level your dense chakra infused body allows you to assault and harm, far more than others in the fray of battle. You gain resistance to one of the following damage types; bludgeoning, piercing, slashing, earth, wind, fire, water, lightning, force, poison or necrotic.
You can replace this resistance with another from the same list by spending 1 bonus action and 2 Perfection and 2 Martial Die.

features.name: Genius Technique
features.description: Starting, at 17th level all it takes is a single strike to completely immobilize and bring an enemy to their knees. When you would score an unarmed or Taijutsu attack that deals unarmed damage on a creature, you can spend 3 Martial dice and 3 Perfection. When you do, they must succeed a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they are Stunned until the beginning of your next turn.

pickable.editorDescription: Taijutsu Specialist Martial Techniques. Learned via the level-up window. Your subclass also grants additional techniques automatically at level 3.
pickable.pickerDescription: Martial Techniques are learned at levels 1 (×2), 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, and 19 (+1 each). Techniques granted automatically by your Taijutsu Style are added separately and don't count against this pick.

options.name: Arresting Technique
options.description: On a melee unarmed/Taijutsu hit: 1 Martial Die, DEX save — fail: speed 0 and prone until your next turn.

options.name: Delayed Fall
options.description: On falling damage: spend up to 3 Martial Die to reduce the number of damage dice rolled by twice the result.

options.name: Drowsy Strike
options.description: In place of an Attack-action attack: 1 Martial Die. Strike a pressure point — roll Martial Die × 10; if ≥ target's current HP, sleep 10 min (ends on damage / ally wake / being moved). Elite/Solo/higher-level foes immune. Target immune for 8 hours after.

options.name: Forceful Strike
options.description: On unarmed damage: 1 Martial Die, STR save — fail: pushed 5×result ft. 2 dice at 7th, 3 dice at 13th. Colliding with a solid structure deals 1d6 bludgeoning per unmoved 5 ft.

options.name: Martial Guard
options.description: Reaction vs melee attack: 1 Martial Die to reduce the triggering damage by the result. 2 dice at 7th, 3 dice at 13th.

options.name: Parry the Shot
options.description: On hit by a ranged Weapon/Ninjutsu/Taijutsu attack: reaction + up to 3 Martial Die to reduce damage by result + your level.

options.name: Precise Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: up to 2 Martial Die — +1 to hit per die spent.

options.name: Raw Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: up to 2 Martial Die — +1 to save DC per die spent.

options.name: Regenerative Surge
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Martial Die: regain HP = result + Taijutsu ability mod. Once per rest.

options.name: Shockwave Strike
options.description: On Taijutsu cast requiring an attack: 1 Martial Die. Creatures of your choice within 5 ft of the target make a DEX save; fail = half damage dealt. Once per casting.

options.name: Quivering Strike
options.description: On unarmed hit: 5 Martial Die — target CON at end of each of their turns; fail: 5 Martial Die bludgeoning (unreducible); success ends. Once per rest per target.

options.name: Step of Savagery
options.description: Action + up to 4 Martial Die + full movement in a straight line, passing through creatures. They save DEX; fail = unarmed damage + spent dice, prone, pulled along to your end space. Doesn't affect creatures 2+ sizes larger.

options.name: Step of Silence
options.description: 1 Martial Die: creatures can't react to your movement; no sound or tracks.

options.name: Step of the Stars
options.description: On a triggered attack of opportunity: 1 Martial Die to make two unarmed attacks instead of one.

options.name: Technical Technique
options.description: On Taijutsu cast: 1 Martial Die — gains Technical keyword. Concentration cost is reduced by half the result.

options.name: Hurricane Strikes
options.description: Action, 4 Martial Die: vanish; select up to 4 creatures in movement range, make a single unarmed attack vs each AC, teleport adjacent to one. Hits deal unarmed damage + 4 Martial Die and CON save — fail = Staggered.

options.name: Step of the Wind
options.description: On your turn, 2 Martial Die + all your movement: teleport to a space you can see within your movement range.

options.name: Shatter
options.description: Reaction to a Ninjutsu/Genjutsu casting: 1 Martial Die, move half speed toward caster, make 2 unarmed attacks. On ≥1 hit, Chakra Control check vs your Taijutsu DC — fail Interrupts the casting.

options.name: Unstable Drive
options.description: In place of an AoO attack: 1 Martial Die to cast a D-rank Taijutsu (→ C at 10, B at 17) targeting the trigger.

options.name: Elemental Rush
options.description: On a melee Taijutsu hit: 1 Martial Die + bonus action to cast a D-rank Ninjutsu of your nature release (1-Action cast time) using Taijutsu ability mod. → C at 10, B at 17.

options.name: Elemental Crush
options.description: On a Finisher Taijutsu using its finisher effect: 1 Martial Die to swap to your Elemental Finisher (still consumes chakra). Once/turn.

options.name: Chakra Enhanced Blows
options.description: On an unarmed attack or Taijutsu: 1 Martial Die for Sustained Pressure OR Valve Break at no additional Martial Die cost. 2+ unarmed hits or Taijutsu save fail inflicts Bruised.

options.name: Tempo Boost
options.description: On Finisher while in Tempo: 1 Martial Die to cast ignoring casting time — loses Finisher keyword, gains Combo Extender. No finisher effect applies.

options.name: Style Shift
options.description: Reaction to an attack targeting you: 1 Martial Die, switch Taijutsu stance, make an unarmed attack. On hit, STR save — fail = Dazed.

options.name: Dodge Cancel
options.description: On a melee Taijutsu/unarmed hit: 1 Martial Die to switch to a defensive stance. Next attack by the damaged creature vs you (until your next turn) is at disadvantage.

options.name: Redirected Aggression
options.description: Reaction to a hostile attack: 1 Martial Die — reduce their attack by the result. If the attack misses, redirect: make a melee/ranged unarmed attack using their range; on hit, target suffers their original attack's effects + 1 Martial Die additional damage.

options.name: Shatterpoint
options.description: On unarmed damage: 1 Martial Die — STR save, fail = Shattered.

options.name: Elemental Core
options.description: Add 1 Martial Die to a Ninjutsu save. If the casting jutsu matches your Cloak's nature release, regain the Martial Die at end of turn.

options.name: Ninjutsu Combo
options.description: On Ninjutsu cast: 1 Martial Die to cast using Taijutsu ability mod; gains Combo keyword + Finisher-targeting rider (once/turn, target one affected creature with a Finisher regardless of range, Action or Bonus Action).

options.name: Flashguard Counter
options.description: On a reaction jutsu cast in response to damage: 1 Martial Die — gain temp HP = 5 × result (stacks with jutsu temp HP, all lost at end of turn).

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

secondarySubclasses.name: Hallucinatory Instrument
secondarySubclasses.description: Your illusions manifest as an instrument of your choice that everyone can see and hear. Great for crowd-control Auditory Genjutsu.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument
features.description:
Your Illusions become manifest as an instrument you have an affinity for that you can conjure as a hallucination that everyone can see and hear called your Hallucinatory Instrument that you hold in a free hand.
As a bonus action, you can tune your Hallucinatory Instrument for the next minute. While tuned, when you cast a Genjutsu with the Auditory keyword that does not require an attack roll, your instrument can change the jutsu's range to either of the following; Once you tune your Hallucinatory Instrument you cannot retune it for 10 minutes.
• Self (20- Foot Cone)
• Self (10-Foot Radius Sphere).
All creatures of your choice in the affected area are affected by the Genjutsu cast so long as they are not deafened.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument (2)
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level, you gain the Words of Affirmation or the Words of Detriment ability, pick one. You can use the chosen ability as an Action.
Words of Affirmation. All creatures of your choice in a 10-foot radius centered on you gain 2d4 + your level in Temporary Hit points. A creature other than you cannot gain temporary hit points from this feature more than twice per initiative.
Words of Detriment. All creatures of your choice in a 20-foot cone originating from yourself that would remake a saving throw to end an effect they are suffering from at the end of their next turn, they remake that save at disadvantage.

features.name: Hallucinatory Instrument (3)
features.description: Starting at 11th level when a hostile creature succeeds a saving throw that they remade, you can spend 1 Actualization Die and your Reaction to force them to remake that saving throw with no bonuses to the result taking the new result.

secondarySubclasses.name: Illusory Weapon
secondarySubclasses.description: You shape your illusions into a psychic weapon — d8 melee / d6 ranged (30 ft) — counting as a Weapon component for Bukijutsu.

features.name: Illusory Weapon
features.description:
You shape your illusions into a weapon of pure psychosis called an Illusory Weapon.
As a Bonus action, you summon an Illusory Weapon in your empty hand, choosing its design each time you summon it. Your Illusory Weapon deals psychic damage and uses a d8 for melee and a d6 for ranged attacks. Ranged attacks made with your Illusory Weapon has a range of 30 feet. You are always proficient with your Illusory Weapon and attacks you make with it use your Genjutsu attack bonus. Your Illusory Weapon is dismissed by you at no action or if you are ever incapacitated. If a class feature would ever interact with a weapon, your Illusory Weapon is always a valid option for it. Your Illusory Weapon is a valid W (Weapon) component for any Bukijutsu of D-Rank. This increases to C-Rank at 7th level and B-Rank at 15th level.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (2)
features.description: Beginning at 7th level, when you take the Attack action with your Illusory Weapon you can attack twice instead of once.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (3)
features.description: Starting at 11th level, when a creature would make a saving throw against a Jutsu you or an allied creature cast if they succeed, you can spend 1 Actualization Die and your Reaction to take the Attack Action. If you score a hit at least once, they must remake their saving throw taking the new result.

features.name: Illusory Weapon (4)
features.description: Your Illusory Weapon is a valid W (Weapon) component for any Bukijutsu of D-Rank. This increases to C-Rank at 7th level and B-Rank at 15th level.

secondarySubclasses.name: Reality Marble
secondarySubclasses.description: An object of significance you imbue as a focus — store Reality Motes from casts and spend them to relay Genjutsu across multiple targets.

features.name: Reality Marble
features.description:
Your illusions suffuse themselves with an object of importance or insignificance of your choice that can fit in the palm of your hand that you call your Reality Marble. This object is usually a glass marble or it can be a children's toy, a compass, or even a piece of jewelry
As a bonus action, you can awaken your Reality Marble as it begins to glow with an ethereal light only and creatures you allow can see. Your Reality Marble remains awoken for 1 minute. While awakened, each time you cast a Genjutsu it stores some of your chakra inside itself as a Reality Mote. When you would cast a Genjutsu you can spend your Reality Mote to mark your target as a reality relay. While marked, each time you cast a Genjutsu targeting the marked creature, you may select up to 2 additional creatures of your choice within 30 feet of the target creature. Each selected creature makes their saving throw as if they were the original target after which, the mark ends. A creature can only be marked once every 10 minutes. If any of the selected creatures fail, they all share the worst result made between each other. Once you awaken your Reality Marble you cannot reawaken it for 10 minutes.

features.name: Reality Marble (2)
features.description: Beginning at 7th level, you Genjutsu you cast that requires concentration, reduces their concentration costs by 2 (Minimum of 1).

features.name: Reality Marble (3)
features.description: Starting at 11th level, if a creature marked as your relay would pass a saving throw they make, you can spend 1 Actualization Die to force them to remake their saving throw taking the new result.

secondarySubclasses.name: Temporal Stopwatch
secondarySubclasses.description: A clock-like focus that bends initiative and reaction economies around your Genjutsu.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch
features.description:
Your Illusions originate from a stopwatch you have made a distinct bond with, called a Temporal Stopwatch. A mundane and trivial clock that can be in any form you choose like a wrist watch or pocket watch.
When you would roll initiative, you can spend and roll 1 Actualization Die targeting one creature you can see. You can choose to either increase or reduce their initiative by the result. You can do this once per initiative.
As a bonus action, you can wind or unwind your Temporal Stopwatch causing its hands to slowly tick both forward and backwards for the next minute.
For the duration, once per turn, when you cast a Genjutsu, as part of the same action you can momentarily delay hostile creatures' perception of you, allowing you to gain the benefits of either the Dash or Disengage actions after the jutsu's effect is applied.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch (2)
features.description:
Beginning at 7th level you gain either the Rewind or Fast Forward ability, pick one.
Rewind. Once per turn, when you would fail a Strength or Dexterity saving throw or be hit by an attack, you can spend your Reaction to remake the saving throw or make the attacking creature re roll their attack taking the second result.
Fast Forward. As a bonus action, you can select one allied creature other than yourself, speeding up their ability to think and react. They gain the benefit of an additional reaction. Once a creature gains the benefit of this ability, they cannot benefit from it again until they complete a rest of any type.

features.name: Temporal Stopwatch (3)
features.description: Starting at 11th when a hostile creature currently under the effects of a Jutsu you or an allied creature cast makes an attack or cast a Jutsu, you can spend 1 Actualization Die and your Reaction they lose their ability to remake saving throws at the end of their turn, until the beginning of their next turn.

features.name: Actualization
features.description:
Also, at 1st level, Genjutsu you cast and Illusion checks you make may use Charisma instead of Wisdom as their Ability modifier, for Attack, Damage and skills, as well as Save DC calculation.
Additionally, your talent and skill in the art of Genjutsu has granted you the ability to tap into a deep reserve of creativity called Actualization Die, represented as a D6. You have a number of Actualization Die equal to 1 + Proficiency Bonus. You recover spent die on a long rest.
When you cast a Genjutsu that would affect hostile creature(s), you may spend your Actualization die to do one of the following;
• Spend 1 Actualization Die. Roll a number of d6's, equal to rank of the jutsu cast (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5), adding the result to the damage dealt.
• Spend 1 Actualization Die, add the result to any Deception, Insight, Perception or Persuasion skill check.
• Spend any number of Actualization Die, reducing the target(s) save by -1 for each die spent.
Finally, you gain a rank of Mastery with the Illusion skill. You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Illusions at levels 7 and 13.

features.name: Chakra Disruption
features.description:
Starting at 1st level, you have learned to restrain some of your enemies Chakra when they are affected by your Genjutsu. Once per turn, when you cast a Genjutsu that affects one or more creatures where they must make a saving throw or you score a hit with a Genjutsu attack, you can disrupt all affected creature's chakra network until the end of their next turn. When you do, the next jutsu they cast has its cost increased by +2. This cost penalty increases in potency (+2) at 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th levels becoming +4, +6, +8 and +10 respectively.
Beginning at 9th level, your ability to disrupt an enemy's chakra control has amplified. By spending 1 Actualization Die when a creature fails their saving throw vs a Genjutsu you cast, they must make a Concentration check to maintain concentration on jutsu they are concentrating on. If they would already be forced to make a concentration check due to damage dealt or the effect of the jutsu cast, then they make their concentration check at disadvantage.

features.name: Genjutsu Pledge
features.description: Starting at 2nd level, the Genjutsu Specialist makes a pledge towards the illusions & Genjutsu they intend to craft. The pledge that you choose grants you features at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th & 18th levels.

features.name: Malleable Mirages
features.description: Starting at 2nd level, you have learned how to manipulate fragments of reality itself imbuing you with unique capabilities that some might even consider unnatural. At 2nd level you learn two Mirages of your choice, found at the end of this Class. You learn one additional Mirage at 4th, 8th, 12th 16th, & 20th levels. When you would gain a Mirage you can switch any Mirages you currently know with a different one.

features.name: Genjutsu Inception
features.description: Starting at 3rd level, you have learned to create a conceptual basis for how to manipulate the perception of reality for anyone who stands in your way. You follow one of these concepts detailed, at the end of the class.

features.name: Keen Awareness
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, for a Genjutsu Specialist, details are the foundations of constructing proper Genjutsu. You gain the ability to accurately recall anything you've seen or heard with great detail, granting you advantage on History Checks.
Additionally, your awareness has taught you the ins and outs of how to quickly learn your preferred art. Reduce the downtime needed to create or learn Genjutsu by half.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Mind Over Matter
features.description: Beginning at 5th level, whenever you would use an effect granted by Actualization on a Genjutsu you cast, that Genjutsu deals additional psychic damage equal to you your Genjutsu ability modifier, unless it already adds your Genjutsu ability modifier to its damage rolls.

features.name: Mental Refocus
features.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you have learned to regain focus in the heat of battle, fueling your creativity. Once before the beginning of each of your turns (Once per round), when you would succeed on a Saving throw against a hostile creature, you regain 1 Actualization Die.
Additionally, when you complete a Short rest, you regain 2 Actualization Die. This increases to 3 at 11th level and 4 at 17th.

features.name: Actualization (2)
features.description: You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Illusions at levels 7 and 13.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Master of Illusion
features.description:
You have mastered some forms of Genjutsu. Beginning at 9th level, you immediately gain one of the following features listed. You gain an additional choice at 15th level.
9th Level
• Higher Understanding: Creature must beat your save DC by 8 or more to be treated as if they Critically succeeded on a Genjutsu you cast.
• Genjutsu Flow: The Genjutsu Specialist increases the range of Genjutsu they cast by 30 feet. Genjutsu with a range of 5 feet or "Touch" can now have a range of 30 feet.
15th Level
• New Reality: Genjutsu the Genjutsu Specialist cast that requires a saving throw can no longer be made with Advantage.
• Greater Mastery: Creatures who's saving throw result is 4 or more, lower than your Save DC, is treated as if they Critically Failed.

features.name: Mental Refocus (2)
features.description: This increases to 3 at 11th level and 4 at 17th.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Actualization (3)
features.description: You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Illusions at levels 7 and 13.

features.name: Master of Illusion (2)
features.description: You gain an additional choice at 15th level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Mental Refocus (3)
features.description: This increases to 3 at 11th level and 4 at 17th.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: The Prestige
features.description:
Starting at 20th level, you have achieved the pinnacle of illusionary skill and potential. Genjutsu you cast cannot be dispelled or Interrupted.
Additionally, Once per turn, when you cast a Genjutsu that would force a creature to make a saving throw, you can spend 3 Actualization Die. When you do, you reduce all affected creatures' level of success by 1 stage. (Critical Success > Success) or (Failure > Critical Failure)

subclasses.name: Beguiler
subclasses.description: Confusing adversaries who misdirect foes with illusory duplicates of themselves — up to three Duplicities act as decoys, distractions, and disguised strikers.

features.name: Beguiling Duplicity
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level when you take this Pledge, you can create an illusory duplicate of yourself as a Bonus Action, this illusion is known as a Duplicity. This Duplicity is a perfect visual illusion of yourself and when summoned, is placed into an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of yourself. It lasts for 1 minute but ends early if you dismiss it on your turn (no action requires) or are ever incapacitated. You can have up to 3 Duplicities active at the same time, but only one can be commanded at a time.
The Duplicity is intangible and doesn't occupy its space and is treated as a Genjutsu you cast that is always of the highest rank you can cast based on your class level. The Duplicity is animated and moves and acts as you describe. While the Duplicity is active, you can gain the following benefits with it.
Displaced Genjutsu. You can cast Genjutsu as if you were in the Duplicities space, but you must use your own senses.
Illusory Body. While your Duplicity doesn't occupy space it can be attacked. It has an AC equal to your Genjutsu Save DC and 1 hit point. If its hit points are reduced to 0, it is dispelled. Creatures cannot discern which of you are real unless they take the Identify Genjutsu skill-Action. If a creature has Chakra Sight, Chakra Sensing, True Sight or Tremor Sense they can always tell which of you are real, becoming immune to Illusory Distraction.
Illusory Distraction. While you and your Duplicity are within 5 feet of the same hostile creature, melee attacks made by that creature always target your Duplicity first.
Moving Illusion. As part of the action used to summon it and as a Bonus Action, you can move your Duplicity up to your movement speed to a space you can see within 120 feet of yourself. It cannot fly unless you have a fly speed.

features.name: Beguiling Presence
features.description: Also at 2nd level, when a creature falls for your Genjutsu, you vanish from its sight. Until the end of your next turn, you become Invisible to any creature of your choice that failed a saving throw as a result of a Genjutsu you cast.

features.name: Who Are You Fighting?
features.description: Starting at 6th level, your Genjutsu is woven into your movements making people question if you were ever there to begin with. When you would take damage as a result of an attack and your Duplicity is active, you can spend your Reaction and 1 Actualization Die to swap places with it. The attack automatically hits your Duplicity instead of you.

features.name: Question Everything
features.description: Also at 6th level, when you cast a Genjutsu of D-Rank or lower, that Genjutsu gains the Unaware keyword. This becomes C-Rank at 10th level and B-Rank at 14th level.

features.name: Triplicity
features.description:
Beginning at 10th level, when you have 2 or more active Duplicities, as an Action and by spending 1 Actualization Die, you vanish and seemingly occupy all of their spaces simultaneously, controlling them all at once. You gain the following abilities until the end of the current turn. At the end of your current turn, select one active Duplicity and dismiss it and teleport to their space.
Move As One. By spending all of your movement, you move all Duplicities at once a number of feet up to your movement speed.
Act As One. As a Bonus Action, you have each Duplicity cast Genjutsu you know of D-Rank or lower each targeting a different creature. You only spend the highest Chakra cost of the Genjutsu cast if each are different.

features.name: Beguiling Force
features.description: Also at 10th level, creatures currently under the effects of a Genjutsu you cast or are concentrating on suffers a 1d4 penalty to their attack rolls.

features.name: Betray Your Friend
features.description: Starting at 14th level, when you summon a Duplicity, as part of the same action, the Duplicity transforms its outward appearances to match that of another hostile creature you can see in initiative. While in this appearance, if there are 2 or more hostile creatures in initiative, they are always weary and no longer treat their allies who share appearances with the Duplicity as their ally until the Duplicity is dismissed. Each time the Duplicity is targeted by an attack or jutsu while it is within 5 feet of the creature whose appearance it stole, the casting or attacking creature must make a Wisdom saving throw vs your Genjutsu save DC, instead targeting their ally on a failure.

features.name: Betray Yourself
features.description: Starting at 18th level your illusory power has reached levels capable of making someone lose trust in themselves. By spending 3 Actualization die, a creature under the effect of a Genjutsu you cast or are concentrating on that you also summon a Duplicity to match using the Betray Your Friend feature begins to suffer an identity crisis as voices fill their heads telling them they were never who they thought they were. The affected creature must make a Charisma saving throw vs your Genjutsu save DC to avoid falling for the ultimate deception. On a Failed save they believe themselves to be the ultimate enemy to themselves. For the next minute they have an Identity Crisis. Creatures with an Identity Crisis can remake this save at the end of each of their turns to end this effect.

subclasses.name: Corrupt Thoughts
subclasses.description: Mind-wreckers who inject chakra directly into enemies' thoughts, breaking focus and stealing their will from range.

features.name: Rending Thoughts
features.description:
When you choose this path at 2nd Level, you learn to inject chakra directly into a creature you can see with but a gesture, look or word, causing their thoughts to rake against the walls of their mind.
As an action, select one creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You perform your chosen method of unleashing this ability, warping their mind. This is treated as a Genjutsu of the highest rank you can cast, based on your level with the Auditory (or Visual, your choice when casting) and Tactile keywords. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
Success. They take 1d6 psychic damage.
Failure. They take 2d6 psychic damage and suffer a 1d4 penalty to their next attack roll before the beginning of your next turn.
Critical Failure. Same as failure, but they instead take 3d6 psychic damage.
This Ability increases in potency as you gain levels. Beginning at 6th level, increase the damage by +1d6 and the penalty increases in die size by 1 step (1d6). This occurs again at 10th (1d8) and 14th (1d10) levels.

features.name: Collapsing Your World
features.description:
Additionally at 2nd level, when you deal damage with a Genjutsu you cast or are concentrating on you can select one of the Jutsu's damage die rolls and treat it as its highest value.
Alternatively, when an affected creature fails a saving throw as a result of a Genjutsu you cast, they become Mentally Compromised on a failed save for the next minute.

features.name: Nightmare Incarnate
features.description: Starting at 6th level, when you deal psychic damage to a creature with a Genjutsu you cast they begin to suffer slight hallucinations, echoes of a different world made entirely of their nightmares. The next creature that targets the affected creature with an attack before the beginning of your next turn, gains a +1 bonus to their attack roll and Critical threat range.

features.name: Mind Leech
features.description:
Also at 6th Level, you can use your Rending Thoughts feature as a Bonus action. You cannot use it as an Action if used in this way. If used as an Action on your turn, you cannot use it as a Bonus Action on the same turn.
When you use your Rending Thoughts feature you can spend 1 Actualization Die to also enhance its effects on a Failure and Critical Failure, draining their physical skills and experience and potentially adding them to your own.
Failure. They also suffer a -1 penalty to their Strength, Dexterity and Constitution saving throw until the end of your next turn. (This becomes -2 at 14th level, and -3 at 18th level)
Critical Failure. Same as failure, but the caster also gains a +1 bonus to their Strength, Dexterity and Constitution saving throw until the end of your next turn. (This becomes +2 at 14th level, and +3 at 18th level)

features.name: Disruptive Psyche
features.description: Beginning at 10th level, Mentally Compromised creatures suffer its enhanced effects.

features.name: Denial is a River
features.description:
Also at 10th level, you learn to utilize your Rend Thoughts in a more intimate way. By spending 1 Actualization Die, your Rend Thoughts can be used with a range of Touch. If you do, its warps the targets mind, making them question their loyalties and allegiances while not in combat. If they would enter combat while under this effect, this effect ends.
When used in this way, creatures make an Intelligence saving throw.
Success. They believe the next thing you tell them so long as it is reasonably believable by the subject. This belief persists for the next hour, after which they realize your trickery.
Failure. They believe the next thing you say, no matter how twisted or disruptive It is. This belief persists until challenged by another creature or 24 hours has passed. When challenged the affected creature remakes their saving throw breaking this charm on a success. A creature can challenge this belief once every hour.
Critical Failure. Same as failure, but the belief persists permanently until challenged and a saving throw can only be triggered by a challenging creature once, requiring a new creature to challenge this new truth.

features.name: Vindictive Thoughts
features.description: Starting at 14th Level, your Rend thoughts no longer singles out creatures. Select a point you can see within its range. All creatures of your choice in a 10-foot radius sphere centered on that point suffers its effects.

features.name: Corrupt Influence
features.description: Starting at 18th level you have learned to utilize your Genjutsu to break your foes mind and shatter their will to survive. Creatures who are Mentally Compromised can no longer spend their Actions to attempt to end hostile effects on them such as spending an Action to end the Burned, Corroded or Bruised conditions. Additionally, by spending 1 Actualization Die a creature you touch gains the Mentally Compromised status.

subclasses.name: Illusionist
subclasses.description: Phantasm summoners — their illusory companion creature casts their Genjutsu, scouts, and unleashes a Maddening Echo mind-break.

features.name: Phantasm
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level when you choose this path, you learn to manifest an illusory ally called your Phantasm. This Phantasm begins as a Small creature using the Phantasm Stat block found at the end of this subclass section and looks how you described but is always tinged black, such as a crow or snake or small version of yourself. As an Action, you can summon your Phantasm. You can command this Phantasm as a Bonus Action on your turn. You can maintain one Phantasm at a time and if you would summon another one, the previous one is dispelled. Phantasms can never be more than 120 feet away from you, immediately dispelling if they ever are. Abilities the Phantasm uses that requires a Saving throw uses your Genjutsu Save DC.
By spending 1 Actualization Die your Phantasm immediately recharges one of its Recharge abilities.

features.name: Illusionary Awareness
features.description:
Also, at 2nd Level you gain the ability to passively detect Genjutsu affecting other creatures. Your passive Genjutsu detection is equal to 10 + your illusions skill. When you encounter a creature who is under the effects of a Genjutsu who's DC is less than your Passive Genjutsu detection, then you become aware of it and the rank of the Genjutsu affecting them. You can become aware of a Genjutsu in this way once per turn if in initiative.
Additionally, you are always aware of Genjutsu affecting you with the Unaware keyword.

features.name: Phantasmic Evolution
features.description:
Starting at 6th Level, your Phantasm can shift between a Small or Medium size and adjust its visage to accommodate and gains a fly speed equal to its movement speed, such as a murder of crows or pack of snakes.
As a reaction taking damage, you and your Phantasm can swap places so long as you and it are within 20 feet of each other.
Additionally, once per turn, you can command your Phantasm to cast one of your known Genjutsu of D-Rank or lower using your statistics targeting only one creature, even if the Jutsu would affect an area or select multiple targets. Casting in this way still spends your chakra. Your Phantasm can cast C-Rank Genjutsu in this way beginning at 10th level and B-Rank Genjutsu in this way beginning at 14th level.

features.name: Illusionary Fury
features.description: Also, at 6th level when you cast a Genjutsu that inflicts a Mental Condition you can spend 1 Actualization Die to increase the ranks of the mental condition inflicted by +1.

features.name: Phantasmic Bomb
features.description:
Starting at 10th Level, when you complete a rest of any type. Select one Genjutsu you know of C-Rank or lower, that inflicts a Mental Condition. Your Phantasm gains a new ability called Thought Bomb infused with the chosen jutsu. The infused Genjutsu can be changed after the completion of any rest.
Thought Bomb (Recharge 9-10 or Recharge 1 Minute). The Phantasm opens its mind and releases a cacophony of thought meant to overwhelm the brain. All creatures of your choice within a 15-foot radius centered on a point the Phantasm occupies must make an Intelligence saving throw following the effects of the infused Genjutsu.

features.name: Phantasmic Double
features.description: Beginning at 10th level, you can summon a second Phantasm. You can summon two Phantasms as part of the same action used to Summon one using the Phantasm class feature. You can also command both individually with a single bonus action.

features.name: Distracting Phantasm
features.description: Starting at 14th Level, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of your Phantasm makes an attack. As a Reaction you can command the Phantasm to shift into the image of the target, confusing the attacking creature. The attacker must make a Wisdom save against your Genjutsu Save DC. On a Failed Save, the attacker must target the Phantasm.

features.name: Phantasmic Influence
features.description: Starting at 18th level you have learned to utilize your Phantasm to manipulate the perception of reality and invalidate creatures' ability to differentiate real from fake. Your Phantasms no longer require a Bonus Action to be commanded, instead always acting during your turn. They can exist up to 1 mile away from you, and once dispelled you gain all visual and auditory information the Phantasm experienced.

subclasses.name: Layered Reality
subclasses.description: Reality-benders who stack Genjutsu on a single target — when enemies think they've escaped one illusion, it's just another layer.

features.name: Alteration of Reality
features.description:
When you choose this path at 2nd level, you can bend the perception other creatures have of you, making it more real than it should be. You conjure illusory chakra until your body is suffused with it and you project an image to all hostile creatures of how this new state appears. Your hair and eyes may glow, you may have wings or even begin to float slightly off the ground. Regardless of the description, this state is known as your Reality Shift.
You can enter your Reality Shift state by spending 1 Actualization Die as a Bonus Action and remain in this state for 1 minute, you end the state willingly (no action requires) or until you are incapacitated. Once you use this state, you cannot do so again until 10 minutes have passed.

features.name: False Security
features.description:
Also, at 2nd level, when a creature escapes a Genjutsu you had cast upon them, they are lulled into a false sense of safety.
Genjutsu you cast of D-Rank or lower cannot be identified unless by a special sense like Chakra Sight or Chakra Sensing. If you are in your Reality Shift state, even special senses cannot help identify those Genjutsu. This identification blocking increases in potency at 6th level even blocking C-Rank Genjutsu from being identified. This potency increases again at 14th level even blocking B-Rank jutsu from being identified.

features.name: Split Focus
features.description:
Beginning at 6th level, while you are in your Reality Shift state, you can maintain concentration on up to 3 Genjutsu at a time.
Additionally, you reduce the concentration cost to maintain a Genjutsu by 1. This increases to -2 at 14th level.

features.name: The First, Second and Third
features.description: Also at 6th level, you learn that you can layer Genjutsu on top of each other, merging them into a single casting. When you cast a Genjutsu of D-Rank or lower that targets a single creature and requires a saving throw, you can spend 1 or 2 Actualization Die. You can select additional Genjutsu of the same rank that does not deal damage, equal to the number of Actualization Die spent, merging them together. When you cast the triggering jutsu, you increase its Chakra cost by the base cost of the additionally chosen Genjutsu. If the target fails the saving throw of the triggering Genjutsu, they also suffer the effects of the merged Genjutsu. The rank of Jutsu this works with increases to C-Rank at 14th level.

features.name: Concealed Unease
features.description: Beginning, at 10th level, you have become the ruler of any Genjutsu you cast, and escape has become a fleeting dream. A creature cannot have advantage on any saving throw made that is a remake of the initial save, to end a Genjutsu you are concentrating on.

features.name: Broken Reality
features.description: Also at 10th level, while you are in your Reality Shift state, you can choose to have all Genjutsu you cast have its saving throw changed to Wisdom.

features.name: Master of Reality
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level, once per turn, on your turn, while a Creature is under the effects of a Genjutsu you cast of C-Rank or lower that you are concentrating on, where they failed the initial saving throw while you are in your Reality Shift state, you can select a different Genjutsu you know of C-Rank or lower that also requires concentration and swap its effects with the one you are currently concentrating on by spending 1 Actualization Die. This does not require a saving throw and duration of the newly swapped Genjutsu becomes the remaining duration of the originally cast Jutsu.
Genjutsu you swap to apply its Failure effects. If the Genjutsu you swap too would apply ranks of a Mental Condition, any current mental conditions are replaced with the listed ones, even if those mental conditions were there prior to the switch.

features.name: Layered Influence
features.description:
Beginning at 18th level, when a creature takes an action of any kind while under the effects of at least 1 Genjutsu that you cast, while you can see them, you may spend your Reaction, 1 Actualization Die and end the Genjutsu you are concentrating on to vastly change the creature's perception of reality, interrupting their action.
If the Action you reacted to was an Elite Action they only lose the Elite Action attempted.

subclasses.name: Misty Distortionist
subclasses.description: Inhaled-toxin specialists who fuse poison and illusion into Phantom Vapors — coat weapons, drop clouds, and distort enemies through psychotoxic feedback.

features.name: Phantom Vapors
features.description:
When you choose this path at 2nd level, you learn to mix poisons with your Genjutsu—specialized, chakra-infused inhalants that manifest potent illusions and induce psychedelic fits, collectively known as Phantom Vapors.
You gain proficiency with Alchemist Kits. If you are already proficient, you instead gain a Rank of Mastery with checks and actions that use it.
When you complete a rest of any kind, you can convert a Poison or Alchemist kit into a special tool known as a Phantom Vapors Kit, a custom item only you know how to create and handle that holds a number of Phantom Vapor Charges equal to 1 + your Proficiency bonus. You can recharge this kit in the following ways;
• Spending 1 Actualization Die.
• Or spending 10 minutes recreating your Phantom Vapor.
As a Bonus Action on your turn, you can expend 1 Phantom Vapor Charge to throw a vial to a point within 30 feet, creating a thin cloud of vapor that fills a 10-foot cube called a Phantom Cloud that lasts for 1 minute or until dispersed. Genjutsu you cast targeting a creature inside the Phantom Cloud gains the Inhaled keyword. You can have 1 Phantom Cloud active as a time. Creating a different cloud will disperse any active clouds.
This Phantom cloud can be improved at higher levels. Beginning at 10th level, you can spend 1 additional charge making the Phantom Cloud fill a 20-foot cube and at 18th level, you can spend 2 additional charges to make the Phantom Cloud heavily obscure vision and even block Chakra Sight and Chakra Sensing within its area.

features.name: Venom Strike Adept
features.description:
Also at 2nd level, you learn to weaponize your illusions directly, blending poison, precision, and Genjutsu into a single strike. Simple Weapons with the Thrown property use your Genjutsu attack bonus, in place of your weapon attack bonus, using your Genjutsu Ability modifier instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for damage calculation.
By spending 1 Phantom Vapor Charge to coat a simple weapon in your special poison, changing its damage type to poison and gaining access to the Phantom Weapon and Hallucinatory Flurry abilities that each can be used as an Action. Jutsu cast though these abilities gain the Genjutsu and Inhaled keywords if they don't already. Using either ability removes the weapons poison coat.
Hallucinatory Flurry. Cast a Bukijutsu you know.
Phantom Weapon. Select a Genjutsu of C-Rank or lower that you know with a casting time of 1 Action, and make a melee or ranged Genjutsu attack using your coated weapon. On a hit, you deal your [Weapons Damage] plus apply the Genjutsu's effect as if casted targeting only them.

features.name: Neurotoxic Feedback
features.description:
Beginning, at 6th level, Your mastery or Vaporous toxins allows you to manifest Genjutsu that react violently to Chakra disturbances. Whenever a creature is within your Phantom Cloud takes Chakra Damage, has their jutsu cost increased by a hostile effect or would attempt to make a Chakra Control Check, you may spend your Reaction and 1 Actualization Die to unleash a surge of psychoactive feedback through the Phantom Cloud. The affected creature(s) must make a Constitution (or Wisdom, your choice) saving throw vs your Genjutsu saving throw. On a failed save they take 4d6 psychic damage and they suffer one of the following effects until the end of your next turn. This damage increases by 1d6 at 10th (5d6), 14th(6d6) and 18th levels (7d6).
• They are Blinded as their eyes seemingly burn.
• Their movement speed is reduced by half as their sense of space becomes distorted.
• They suffer a -2 Penalty to their Constitution saving throws as their body is weakened by the poisonous gas.

features.name: Toxic Mastery
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your knowledge of volatile chakra reactive compounds allows you to create potent toxins that serve both as weapons and Genjutsu catalysts.
By spending 1 Actualization Die you can make a vial of basic poison function as a different poison entirely. Select a poison from the list of Poison types found in the Shinobi Handbook that can be created with a Poison Kit of D-Rank or lower. This Poison lasts for 1 hour or until used. Beginning at 10th level, you can select a C-Rank poison and at 18th level you can select a B-Rank poison.

features.name: Venom Strike Expert
features.description:
Beginning, at 10th level, your skill at weaving toxins and Genjutsu into your weapon strikes has evolved into a personalized style of fighting. You gain the following abilities when you coat a weapon using your Venom Strike Adept feature; Venom Chain and Nightmare Rain that can each be used as an Action. Using either ability removes the weapons poison coat. These abilities are treated as a Genjutsu you can cast with a rank equal to the highest rank you can cast based on your level with the Inhale keyword.
Venom Chain. Make two melee or ranged Genjutsu attacks using your coated weapon. These attacks only roll your ammunition die once, if needed. Each attack deals your [Weapons Damage] and if you score a hit at least once, the affected creature suffers the effects of Psychosis.
Nightmare Rain. Select a Phantom Cloud you can see within 90 Feet. All creatures of your choice within the clouds area must make a Wisdom saving throw, believing weapons are raining down on them. On a failed save affected creatures spend their chakra haphazardly as they attempt to dodge impossible odds. They 5d6 Chakra damage and suffer the effects of Psychosis or half as much chakra damage and no further effects on a success.

features.name: Miasmic Brand
features.description:
Also at 10th level, your toxins no longer serve as your weapons alone, they can serve as tools to aid your team, a catalyst for your shared aggression towards your unified enemies. When an allied creature begins their turn within or enters your Phantom Cloud for the first time on their turn, they become intoxicated with your Miasmic Brand. A creature is branded until the end of their turn.
While a creature carries your Miasmic Brand, [Weapon Damage] and [Unarmed Damage] they deal is increased by 2d6 poison damage, up to twice per turn. Additionally, if a branded creature deals this bonus poison damage to a hostile creature with Psychosis, the hostile creature suffers the effect of your Neurotoxic Feedback feature.

features.name: Phantom Bloom
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level, your toxins bloom into a field of psycho reactive mist, spreading the chaos of combat. At the beginning of each of your turns, your Phantom Clouds grow in size by 5-feet in all directions, up to a 40-Foot Cube in size.
Additionally, hostile creatures who begin their turns within your Phantom Cloud or enter the cloud for the first time on their turns, reduce their AC by 2. Ending their turn outside of the Cloud removes this penalty.

features.name: Venom Strike Mastery
features.description:
Beginning at 18th level, your fusion of toxin and illusion reaches its peak. Every strike, a nightmare. A phantom incision into the mind as much as the body. Weapons you coat with a Phantom Vapor Charge increases its damage die by 2 steps, up to a max of a d12.
When you use the Phantom Weapon ability, you may use up to B-Rank Genjutsu.
When you use the Phantom Weapon ability, you may select a second creature within range and repeat the attack, dealing damage and applying the Genjutsu's effect on them as well on a hit.
You may use Venom Chain and Nightmare Rain as a Bonus Action on your turn.

pickable.editorDescription: Malleable Mirages — your pickable class resource. Many require a specific Inception, Pledge, or known Genjutsu. Use + Custom for homebrew.
pickable.pickerDescription: Mirages scale: 2 at level 2, +1 each at 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 (7 total). You may swap a known Mirage each time you gain a new one.

options.name: Accelerate
options.description: Action: up to 3 willing creatures within 20 ft can Dash as a bonus action for 1 min. Affected creatures can end the effect as a reaction to gain advantage on a DEX save. Per-creature 10-min cooldown.

options.name: Agnostic Mind
options.description: You can understand and speak any language or dialect, so long as the creature you communicate with speaks a language.

options.name: Armor of Psychosis
options.description: Add your Genjutsu ability mod instead of DEX when calculating AC.

options.name: Battle Ready Minds
options.description: Casting Bless can target up to 4 willing creatures within 60 ft instead of the listed range.

options.name: Beast Speech
options.description: Cast Animal Companion as a bonus action at no cost.

options.name: Beguiling Influence
options.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in both Deception and Persuasion. (Apply the ranks manually on the skill rows.)

options.name: Beneath the Boot
options.description: Psionics: Crush can target up to 2 additional creatures.

options.name: Berserk Thoughts
options.description: Shadow Monsters affects all creatures in a 15-ft radius sphere at a chosen point within range.

options.name: Bewitching Whispers
options.description: Charming Dissonance on a Charmed hostile: make yourself the source of infatuation; disposition +1 rank.

options.name: Black Fangs
options.description: Shadow Bite +1 crit threat range; on crit, target saves at disadvantage.

options.name: Blade Song
options.description: Weapons of Darkness cannot lose concentration from damage; no chakra required for concentration.

options.name: Blinding Lights
options.description: Color Spray affects a 10-ft cube at a point within 60 ft; +5 ft per rank above D.

options.name: Book of Stolen Secrets
options.description: In conversation with a scroll/book in hand, ask one question — unwilling targets WIS save vs Genjutsu DC; failure or truthful response writes the most accurate answer in the book. 1-hour cooldown per creature.

options.name: Chained Mind
options.description: Mind Burst: if target within 10 ft, attack at advantage; on save fail, you become Invisible to the Berserked creature.

options.name: Tree Binding Death
options.description: Effortless Paralysis target count up to 3. 10-min rest before reuse.

options.name: Cloak of Chains
options.description: Bonus Action + 1 Actualization Die: phantasmic chakra aura in 5 ft around you, 1 min. Intimidation Mastery vs non-hostiles in aura; hostiles starting a turn in aura take 4 psychic.

options.name: Decelerate
options.description: Action: one creature within 60 ft WIS save vs Genjutsu DC — fail = 1 rank Slow. Target must spend Reaction to resave. 10-min per-target cooldown.

options.name: Demon Sight
options.description: See normally in darkness and chakra-based darkness up to 120 ft.

options.name: Devilish Recovery
options.description: Mind-Body Recovery: spend 2 Actualization Die — temp HP gained becomes HP regained. 1-hour per-target cooldown.

options.name: Doubled Weapon
options.description: Create a second Illusory Weapon (neither can be two-handed). While dual-wielding, damage die -1 step; attacks with them +1 on Attack Action.

options.name: Dreamscape
options.description: Action: target a known creature within 100 miles. Enter trance and appear in their dream (if asleep) — converse, shape dream landscapes. They recall perfectly on waking.

options.name: Dulled Mind
options.description: Slow affects all creatures of your choice in a 20-ft cube at a point within range.

options.name: Evil Thoughts
options.description: Mind Crunch: attack result 10+ over target AC is treated as a crit.

options.name: Fighting Mirage
options.description: Pick a Fighting Style from Chapter 13. Cannot take more than once.

options.name: Gaze of Two Minds
options.description: Action: touch a willing humanoid, perceive through their senses for 1 hour (max range 1 mile; ends if either takes damage). You're Blinded/Deafened to your own senses while active but gain theirs.

options.name: Ghastly Mist
options.description: Fog of War radius becomes 40 ft.

options.name: Grasp of Courage
options.description: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a Genjutsu requiring an attack roll, force them to move up to 10 ft in a straight line closer to you.

options.name: Haunting Melody
options.description: Damaging Auditory Genjutsu through your Hallucinatory Instrument's area: save fails gain 1 rank Demoralized.

options.name: Illusionary Brawler
options.description: Psionics: Strike! on yourself: no chakra to maintain concentration. While affected, unarmed attacks can be Melee Genjutsu attacks.

options.name: Illusionary Vigor
options.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in both Athletics and Acrobatics. (Apply manually on skill rows.)

options.name: Maddening Pain
options.description: Doubled Pain's bonus damage triggers even on ally attacks (once/turn per creature).

options.name: Magnum Opus
options.description: Bringer of Darkness: choose which creatures are affected.

options.name: Misty Thoughts
options.description: Cajolery of Glamour loses HS component. Casting on a non-hostile costs no chakra. Hostiles can't be targeted this way.

options.name: Misty Visions
options.description: Innocuous Aspect can only be dispelled by physical inspection.

options.name: Muddled Movements
options.description: Hitting with a Genjutsu attack reduces target speed by 10 ft until your next turn.

options.name: Myriad Forms
options.description: Transform allows small/medium/large object forms. Large: weight unchanged, occupies original space visually. Small: occupy smaller spaces but cannot move unsupervised. Investigation/Illusion check reveals disguise.

options.name: One with Shadows
options.description: In dim light or darkness, Action to become Invisible and silent. Lost on >½ movement, attack, or jutsu cast.

options.name: Piece of Mind
options.description: Mind Sliver as a bonus action with a d8 damage die.

options.name: Prolonged Songs
options.description: Heavenly Worded Song range becomes Self (40-ft cone).

options.name: Psyche Drinker
options.description: Once/turn, on Genjutsu damage: 1 Actualization Die to gain damage dealt as temp HP until your next turn.

options.name: Relentless Pain
options.description: While within 60 ft of a Doubled Pain target that can see you and you take damage: Reaction + 1 Actualization Die to deal psychic damage equal to the triggering damage.

options.name: Repelling Illusions
options.description: On a Genjutsu attack hit, push the target up to 10 ft in a straight line away from you.

options.name: Shroud of Shadows
options.description: Darkness ninjutsu gains Genjutsu + Visual keywords.

options.name: Signature Work
options.description: Programmed Illusions: choose a known C-rank or lower Genjutsu — the illusion can cast it once per minute using your stats. Dispels if the illusion takes real damage. 1 long-rest cooldown.

options.name: Superior Thoughts
options.description: Cast 1 S-rank Genjutsu you qualify for as a Full-Turn Action, no cost, once per long rest. Gain 3 ranks of Confused + Concussed on use. Repeat costs 4 chakra die.

options.name: Tentative Escape
options.description: Reaction to failing a Genjutsu save: cast Release.

options.name: Vampiric Pain
options.description: Creatures under Doubled Pain take chakra damage = the psychic damage dealt by Doubled Pain.

options.name: Voice of an Old Friend
options.description: Telepathically communicate with 2 willing creatures up to 1 mile away (→ 3 at 7, 4 at 18). Target must be generally known; they can resist.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Illusions.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

features.name: Medical Ninjutsu
features.description:
Beginning at 1st level, as a Medical Ninja you gain the ability to learn any jutsu with the Medical Keyword. Jutsu you cast with the Medical Keyword may use Wisdom for their attack and damage rolls, as well as Save DC calculation.
Finally, Medicine checks you make can use Intelligence or Wisdom and you gain a rank of Mastery in the Medicine skill. You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Medicine at levels 8 and 12.

features.name: Rejuvenating Rest
features.description: Starting at 1st level you use your medical skills to revitalize the wounded while resting. When you and allied creatures regain Hit points at the end of a short rest, roll 1d6, adding the result to the hit points regained. This healing becomes 2d6 at 5th, 3d6 at 10th, and 4d6 at 15th level.

features.name: Channeled Healing
features.description: Finally at 1st level you learn to passively enhance your Medical Jutsu to greater lengths. Whenever you cast a Jutsu with the Medical Keyword of D-Rank or higher to restore hit points, the affected creature regains +2 additional hit points. The bonus healing this feature provides increases as you grow in level as seen in the Channeled Healing column of the class table. This healing bonus can only be applied once per turn.

features.name: Tenets of Medicine
features.description: Starting at 2nd Level, you begin to focus on a Specific Tenet of Medicine that will enhance your abilities to support your allies. These Tenets will provide you additional features at 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th and 18th Levels.

features.name: Medical Domain
features.description:
Starting at 3rd Level you have gained the experience needed to save lives, even those in mortal danger or take them, even the ones who are furthest from death's door. You learn three abilities under the Medical Domain called Preserve Life, Sustain Life and Take Life. You gain additional Domain abilities granted by your Tenets. Using these Domain abilities can be taxing. You have a 4 Domain Charges per rest and each Domain ability costing 1 charge. At the completion of a short rest, you recover all spent charges. You increase the number of Domain Charges you have by +1 at 11th and 17th levels.

PRESERVE LIFE
When healing a creature other than yourself, you infuse a pulse of medical release Chakra directly into them in attempts to sustain them. When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that restores hit points you can invoke this Domain ability. If you do, select a number of healing dice the jutsu would roll, equal to its rank and reduce the number rolled by that amount (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5). Each healing dice removed is treated as its maximum value before adding it to the jutsu's results. A creature cannot benefit from this Domain ability while not in combat due to requiring intense adrenaline to function properly. You cannot use this feature on an undead or construct.

SUSTAIN LIFE
By sharing in your reserves of chakra, you keep your allies going far beyond where they would have run out of stamina. As an Action, by touching a willing creature other than yourself, you can invoke this Domain ability. The target creature regains Chakra points equal to one of their Chakra Die + their Constitution Modifier. This does not spend their Chakra die. A creature cannot benefit from this Domain ability while not in combat due to requiring intense adrenaline to function properly. You cannot use this feature on an undead or construct.

TAKE LIFE
While harming a creature you can infuse a pulse of harmful medical release chakra directly into your target. When you would deal damage to a creature using a Jutsu with the Medical keyword, you can invoke this Domain ability, increasing the damage dealt by 5 + your Medical-Nin level.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Bladed Chakra Scalpel
features.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you have learned to manifest a medical ninja's signature combat technique, the Chakra Scalpel.
As a Bonus Action, you can coat your hands in highly condensed blades of blue Chakra, designed to slice with surgical efficiency. For the next minute, when you take the attack action, you can replace those attacks with a Melee Ninjutsu Attack. These attacks are treated as a Ninjutsu with the medical keyword of the highest rank you can cast, based on your level. This attack deals 1d8 + your Channeled Healing bonus as Slashing damage.

features.name: Mystical Palm
features.description:
Also at 5th level, you have learned to manifest a medical ninja's signature support technique, the Mystical Palm.
As a Bonus Action, you can coat your hands in a highly condensed layer of medical chakra. For the next minute, you gain the following abilities when working with a Medicine Kit. Once used you cannot renter this state for the next 10 minutes.
Sterile Channeling: When you use Light Patch Up affected creatures regains 2d6 per charge instead.
Surgical Insight: When using Full Fix Up, reduce the number of Charges you expend by 1.
Pulse Stabilization: When using Stabilization, the creature wakes up with 1 Hit point, instead of remaining at 0.

features.name: Never on the Front Lines
features.description:
You believe deeply that medical ninja do not belong on the front lines and so did your master or mentor. Beginning at 7th level, you embody this philosophy and do everything in your power to live this truth. Whenever you are within 10 feet of a hostile creature, you can take the dash or disengage actions as a bonus action.
Beginning at 15th level, you can take the Dodge action as a bonus action as well.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Medical Ninjutsu (2)
features.description: You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Medicine at levels 8 and 12.

features.name: Advanced Medical Research
features.description:
Starting at 9th Level, your medical knowledge is one of a select few. You can learn and create jutsu with the Medical keyword in half the time and without spending Ryo to support your lifestyle.
Additionally, you can reduce the final Chakra cost of a newly created jutsu with the medical keyword by an amount equal to its rank. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)

features.name: Medical Domain (2)
features.description: You increase the number of Domain Charges you have by +1 at 11th and 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Medical Ninjutsu (3)
features.description: You gain an additional rank in Mastery for Medicine at levels 8 and 12.

features.name: Until Their Heart Stops
features.description: You believe deeply that medical ninja should continue to administer healing to all members of their party, no matter how dire or how hopeless it is. Beginning at 13th level, a creature other than yourself who regains hit points as a result of a jutsu you cast, and is currently under the effects of a Mental, Physical or Elemental condition, makes their next saving throw or skill check to end said condition with a Rank of Mastery.

features.name: Never on the Front Lines (2)
features.description: Beginning at 15th level, you can take the Dodge action as a bonus action as well.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Medical Domain (3)
features.description: You increase the number of Domain Charges you have by +1 at 11th and 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Medical Perfection: Supreme Medic
features.description:
At 20th Level you have reached perfection in the medical arts. Allowing you to tap into the ultimate in healing and harming arts. Utilizing the base principles of your Medical Domain feature, you can unleash the two ultimate Domain abilities Life Pulse and Hemorrhage Vitality. Each of these abilities' costs 2 Domain Charges.

LIFE PULSE
When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that restores hit points, all affected creatures begin to pulse with life, gaining the Life Pulse condition for the next minute. While a creature has this condition, you must both maintain concentration on this effect as if concentrating on an S-Rank jutsu and make a DC 25 Chakra Control check at the beginning of each of your turns to avoid losing concentration on it. If you lose concentration of this, all affected creatures lose this condition. A creature cannot benefit from this Domain ability while not in combat due to requiring intense adrenaline to function properly.

HEMORRHAGE VITALITY
When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that deals damage, all creatures affected begins to hemorrhage their very own life force, suffering the Hemorrhage Vitality condition for the next minute. This condition is treated as if applied by an S-Rank Jutsu and cannot be removed early by a Jutsu unless it is S-Rank.

subclasses.name: Adept Medic
subclasses.description: Field healers who ascend into a state of heightened support, keeping every ally standing through overflowing HP grants, condition breaks, and vital threading.

features.name: Healing Ascension
features.description:
Starting, at 2nd Level, your technique and skill with Medical Ninjutsu has allowed you to manifest a state of ascendant skill, allowing you to be the hero everyone needs when called upon.
As a bonus action, while you are in combat, you can enter as state of Ascension for the next minute. Once you enter this state, you cannot enter it again until you complete 10 minutes of uninterrupted meditation and recovery.

features.name: Talented Support
features.description:
Also, at 2nd Level, your healing adapts to each patience physical state in the moment. When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword, choose one of the following enhancements.
• If the target has no conditions, they gain 4 Temporary hit points.
• If the target has ranks of a Physical, Elemental or Mental condition, reduce the cost of the jutsu cast by an amount equal to its rank. (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5)

features.name: Healing Overflow
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your skill with medical chakra has taught you to aid your allies long after you healed them utilizing your Healing Surges. You gain the Overflow Recovery ability while within your Ascension state.
Overflow Recovery. As a bonus action, by spending 1 Surge, you mark a willing allied creature with Overflow which lasts up to 1 minute or until used. A creature with overflow can spend it when they would make a saving throw, granting themselves a +1d4 Bonus to their save. A creature can benefit from Overflow once every other turn.

features.name: Medical Domain: Mending Spark
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your mastery with regenerative techniques allows you to spark life back into the most desperate allies, knitting their wounds over time, even after you've walked away. As a Bonus action while you are in your Ascension state, you can invoke this Domain ability by spending a Charge. When you do, you reach out and touch a willing creature coating them in regenerative energies for the next minute. This Domain ability requires concentration as if concentrating on a C-Rank jutsu.
For the duration, at the beginning of the affected creatures turns, they regain hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus and ends early if the target is reduced to 0 hit points. A creature can only benefit from one instance of Mending Spark at a time.

features.name: Amplified Ascension
features.description:
Beginning at 10th Level, your Ascension state has elevated you to being the pinnacle of Healers and support Medics in your field. While within your Ascension state, you increase the maximum number of Healing Surges to 5 and you gain 2 additional abilities that you can spend Healing Surges on; Vital Transference and Condition Break.
Vital Transference. As an Action, you can spend 2 surges, allowing you to mark one hostile creature you can see within 60 Feet. While marked, the first time each turn an allied creature deals damage to them with an attack, that allied creature saps some of their lifeforce, regaining hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.
Condition Break. As an Action, you can spend 2 surges, allowing you release a flash of medical chakra meant to cure difficult to remove conditions. All allied creatures within a 20-foot radius of you lose all ranks of a single condition of your choice of B-Rank or lower. This becomes A-Rank or lower when you reach 18th level.

features.name: Gifted Healer
features.description:
Starting at 10th Level, you have mastered the application of medicine touch a degree that your hands move with certainty, your tools never tremble, and your treatment always cuts straight to the issue.
When you use the Treat Wounds action, if you fail you still gain some benefits, recovering half of the result of Success Healing as if treating at Proficient tier. This attempt can affect up to 4 creatures at once, instead of one at a time.
Additionally, if you roll a Natural 20 while performing the Treat Wounds action with a Medicine Kit, you recover 2 spent charges of the Medicine Kit, doing more, with less resources.

features.name: Vital Conductor
features.description:
Beginning at 14th level, your mastery over your own flow of chakra has reached a level, allowing you to manipulate the flows of chakra of your allies, and even enemies. Suppressing threats and guiding teammates.
As a bonus action when you either deal damage to a hostile creature or recover hit points of an allied creature using a Jutsu with the Medical keyword for either, you can generate a thin string of chakra marking one affected creature with your Vital Thread. Only one instance of Vital Thread can be active at a time. This thread last for 1 minute or until a creature ends its turn more than 120 feet away from you. While a creature is marked with Vital Thread they are affected as follows.
Allies. Whenever they would regain hit points or gain temporary hit points as a result of a Jutsu you cast, they gain +10 feet of movement speed and can spend it immediately. This movement does not trigger attacks of opportunity. An allied creature can have a thread connected to them once per rest. If you are in your Ascension state, the Threaded creature reduces the first instance of damage they take by an amount equal to your Channeled Healing bonus each turn.
Hostiles. When the hostile creature attempts to cast a Jutsu while you can see them, they must first make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC. On a failure, they gain a rank of Slow until the end of their current turn. A creature that succeeds this saving throw breaks the Vital Thread attached to them. If you are in your Ascension state, the thread is not broken if they succeed.

features.name: Over Heal
features.description:
At 18th Level, when you restore Hit points to a creature and they reach full Hit points and there are still left-over Points of restoration, the creature gains Temporary Hit Point equal to half of the left-over Hit points that would be restored.
While in your Ascension state, the temporary hit points granted instead become equal to the full amount of left over healing.
A creature may only benefit from this feature once per 10 minutes.

subclasses.name: Black Medicine
subclasses.description: Poison-and-toxin specialists whose medical chakra has turned deep green with purples and blacks — rending defenses, inflicting Envenomed/Corroded, and detonating their chakra within enemies.

features.name: Toxic Chakra
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level you have learned to turn your Medical Chakra from being a source of healing, into a source of harm. Your medical Chakra has become a deep green with hints of purples and blacks. It has become toxic to the immediate area and to creature you use it on.
When you roll initiative, you begin radiating this hazardous chakra known as your Malignant Aura, which lasts until the end of an encounter. While radiating your Malignant Aura, you gain access to Blight. Once per turn you can gain Blight, either by spending a Bonus action to manifest a charge of it, or by inflicting ranks of Envenomed or Corroded on a creature as a result of a Class feature or Jutsu you cast.
