You can maintain up to 3 Blight and can spend them to do the following abilities. If an ability calls for a saving throw, you use your Ninjutsu save DC for the contest;
Septic Detonation. As an Action, by spending 1 Blight, you target a creature with ranks of Envenomed forcing the poison inside to violently react to your chakra. The target takes 1d12 poison damage for each rank of Envenomed they have.
Rotting Pulse. When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that deals damage as part of a saving throw, by spending 1 Blight, you rapidly make the chakra more hazardous. The jutsu's damage becomes Poison or Necrotic and on a failed save, the affected creatures all become Blighted.
Malignant Barrier. As a Bonus action, by spending 1 Blight, you manifest a shield of corrosive chakra that shields you until the beginning of your next turn. The first time a creature makes a melee attack against you, they become Corroded.

features.name: Hematoxic Touch
features.description:
Also, at 2nd level your technique with corrosive chakra makes your combative medical techniques all the more abrasive. Damage you deal with a jutsu with the Medical keyword is increased by an amount equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
Additionally, when you deal damage with a jutsu with the Medical keyword, you reroll 1's and 2's, taking the second result.

features.name: Poisonous Touch
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your skill with malignant chakra bleeds into your normal Medical-Nin techniques, such as your Chakra Scalpel. When you use your Bladed Chakra Scalpel feature, you can change the damage it deals to Acid or Poison. For your Bladed Chakra Scalpel's duration, you can make an attack with it as a as a Bonus action.
Additionally, while you have your Bladed Chakra Scalpel feature active, you can, as an Action, spend 1 Blight to use the Virulent Dance ability.
Virulent Dance. Make a melee Ninjutsu attack with your Bladed Chakra Scalpel, comparing the result vs all creatures within your range AC. On a hit, your deal your Bladed Chakra Scalpels damage and force each affected creature to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they gain 1 rank of Envenomed or Blighted (Your choice).

features.name: Medical Domain: Plague Doctor
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your mastery of rending an enemies defenses leaving them defenseless. When you cast a jutsu with the Medical keyword that deals damage, you can invoke this Domain ability. If the triggering jutsu forces a saving throw, then this domain ability is applied on a failed save. If the triggering jutsu does not have a saving throw, then the affected creatures must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC. If the target has ranks of Envenomed or Corroded they make their saving throw with a -2 penalty.
On a failed save, affected creatures has your malignant chakra seep into their pores as you decide how to rend their defenses, pick one. If the affected creature is Blighted you may instead choose two.
Strip Resistance. Affected creature loses one resistance they have until the end of your next turn.
Strip Protection. Affected creature has its DR (Damage Reduction) value reduced to 0 until the end of your next turn.
Strip Fortitude. Affected creature has one Immunity it has treated as resistance, until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Toxic Tongue
features.description: Starting, at 10th Level, poison and acid damage you deal ignores resistance and you gain immunity to the Poisoned and Corroded conditions. Additionally, when you craft a Poison using a Poison kit, you make the check at advantage.

features.name: Malignant Harmony
features.description:
Also, at 10th level, your mastery of malignant chakra has reached a terrifying pinnacle. Your presence alone saturates a battlefield with hazardous chakra. You increase the maximum number of Blight you can sustain to 5, and Your Toxic Chakra abilities evolve with new effects.
Septic Detonation. By spending 2 Blight, the target creature instead exudes a 20-foot radius sphere of your volatile chakra. All creatures, excluding the original target, within the radius must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save they take the same damage the original target took. On a successful save, they instead take half.
Rotting Pulse. By spending 2 Blight, all affected creatures with ranks of Envenomed or Corroded make their saving throw at disadvantage.
Malignant Barrier. By spending 2 Blight, the shield lasts until the beginning of your next turn, and can trigger up to twice per turn from a creature attacking with a melee attack, inflicting 1 rank of Corroded for each attack.

features.name: Nerve Shredder
features.description:
Starting at 14th level, your malignant chakra has evolved into a devastating neurotoxin. It assaults the targets nervous system making it more difficult to function and fight. When you deal damage to a creature as a result of them failing a saving throw of a jutsu you cast, the neurotoxin takes effect. The effected creature cannot take reactions and suffer disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of their next turn.
If the affected creature is Blighted they also reduce their movement speed by half until the end of their next turn

features.name: Black Lotus Bloom
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, your malignant chakra has peaked. Its toxicity, lethality and corrosive nature has been perfected, resulting in the ultimate Blight ability, Bloom.
Bloom. As an Action, by spending 3 Blight, you manifest a blooming aura of decay for 1 minute. All creatures of your choice within a 30-foot radius, centered on you that begin their turn in the aura, or enter it for the first time on their turn must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC. On a failed save they suffering the following at the beginning of each of their turns so long as they remain in your aura. A creature who failed their save and exits the aura and re-enters it is still treated as if they failed for the next minute. A creature that spends 1 round outside of your aura loses the auras affect, allowing them to remake their save if they re-enter the aura.
• Affected creatures gain 1 rank of Envenomed or Corroded.
• Affected creatures Resistances and Immunities are suppressed, treated as removed until the beginning of your next turn.
• Affected creatures cannot regain hit points.
• If an affected creature is Blighted, they make saving throws to resist this aura at disadvantage.

subclasses.name: Combat Medic
subclasses.description: Front-line medics who fight alongside the martial specialists — Yin-Seal-powered strikes, Battle Medic stance, and Taijutsu-scale weapon combos backed by medical chakra reserves.

features.name: Martial Competency
features.description: Beginning at 2nd Level, you learn how to fight with the best of them. You gain proficiency with weapons with the Unarmed property and heavy armor. You can also make one unarmed attack as a bonus action. You may use your Wisdom in place of Dexterity when calculating Armor Class. You also adopt a particular fighting stance. Choose one of the Taijutsu Stance located in Chapter 13: Customization Options.

features.name: Yin Seal: Storage
features.description:
Starting at 2nd Level, you have begun to store medical release Chakra into your muscles, prepping them for when it is time to release it in a single burst. This stored power is manifested as Yin Seals, represented as d12's, of which you have 4. You regain 1 Yin Seal when you score a critical hit with a melee unarmed or Taijutsu attack, once per turn. You regain all spent seals when you spend 10 minutes of uninterrupted meditation. You can spend Yin Seals to use battle techniques while in your Battle Medic state. As a Bonus Action, while you are in combat, you enter your Battle Medic combat state for the next minute. You cannot enter this state again until you spend 10 minutes recovering from the fatigue is brings. While in this state you can spend Yin Seals to use the following abilities.
Crimson Lotus Missile. When you cast a Taijutsu that makes a single attack, by spending 1 Yin Seal as part of the attack, you add Yin Seal to the damage dealt and the affected creature and all creatures within 5 feet of it, excluding you must make a Strength saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu) save DC. On a failure, they are knocked prone and Dazed.
Cherry Blossom Rush. When you take damage from an attack or Jutsu, you can spend your Reaction and 1 Yin Seal. When you do, roll the spent Yin seal, multiplying the result by 5. You can move a number of feet, up to the result and then you can to cast a Taijutsu with a casting time of 1 Action at the end of that movement.

BATTLE MEDIC (Fortitude Amp)
While in Battle Medic, you gain the following benefits:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes a d12.
  • Your unarmed and Taijutsu attacks gain a +1 bonus to their critical threat range. Becomes +2 at 10th level.
  • Taijutsu you cast of D-Rank or lower gain the Medical keyword. Becomes C-Rank at 6th and B-Rank at 10th level.
  • If you begin your turn with half or fewer hit points, you regain hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

actions.name: Cherry Blossom Rush
actions.description: While in Battle Medic, when you take damage, spend 1 Yin Seal: roll the seal and move that many ×5 ft, then cast a 1-Action Taijutsu at the end of the movement.

features.name: Chakra Enhanced Combat
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your skill in combat keeps up with the more martial shinobi, and you won't be left behind. You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
When you score a critical hit with an Unarmed Attack, you can choose to instead deal [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage].
If you use Bladed Chakra Scalpel your unarmed damage becomes Piercing and if your attack result is 10 or more over the targets AC, they gain a rank of Bruised.

features.name: Medical Domain: Strength of 100
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, you learn to infuse a surge of chakra into your Battle Medic state. As part of entering your Battle Medic state, you can invoke this Domain ability, spending 1 Charge. When you do, you gain the Strength of 100 enhancements for the duration of this state. If an effect doesn't appear in the enhancement, then default to the original effects of the Battle Medic state.

STRENGTH OF 100 ENHANCEMENT
Replaces / augments the default Battle Medic effects with:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes 2d12.
  • You gain a rank of Mastery with Strength based checks and saves.
  • Constructs and Structures are Vulnerable to your [Unarmed Damage].
  • Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals [Unarmed Damage], you can push the target 10 feet in any direction except upwards.

features.name: Expert Combatant
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, you've trained to battle alongside your allies on the front lines making you an expert combatant. You increase the number of Yin Seals you can maintain by 2, increasing your maximum to 6.
Your unarmed attacks gain the Deadly 2 weapon properties, your unarmed and Taijutsu attacks ignores up to 4 DR (Damage reduction) and when a creature within 5 feet of you misses you with a melee attack, you gain advantage on your next melee attack, targeting them.

features.name: Yin Seal: Burst
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, you have learned to release your Medical chakra into controlled bursts. You gain the following abilities for which you can spend Yin Seals to use.
Iron Rose Spear. In place of an attack, you make when you take the Attack action, you can spend 2 Yin Seals. When you do, make a melee Taijutsu attack that rips and tears through the surrounding atmosphere like a sharpened spear. On a hit, you deal your [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + 2d12 and the affected creature must make a Strength saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC. On a failed save, they are pushed 30 feet in a direction of your choice (excluding upwards), and are Staggered.
Magnolia Hammer. In place of an attack, you make when you take the Attack action, you can spend 2 Yin Seals to strike the ground generating large columns of whatever materials are in the terrain you are in (Stone, Tree Trunks, Steel chunks, etc.) destabilizing the area and keeping everyone off balance. All creatures within a 15-foot radius, centering on you must make a Dexterity saving throw vs your Taijutsu (Or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC. On a failed save you deal your [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + 2d6 and the affected creatures are knocked prone and Dazed. On a successful save they take half damage.

features.name: Medical Domain: Strength of 1000
features.description:
Starting at 14th level, you learn to infuse a mass of chakra into your Battle Medic state. As part of entering your Battle Medic state, you can invoke this Domain ability, spending 1 Charge and 2 Yin Seals. When you do, you gain the Strength of 1000 enhancements for the duration of this state. If an effect doesn't appear in the enhancement, then default to the original effects of the Battle Medic state.

STRENGTH OF 1000 ENHANCEMENT
Replaces / augments the previous Battle Medic / Strength of 100 effects with:
  • Your [Unarmed Damage] die becomes 2d12.
  • You gain 2 ranks of Mastery with Strength based checks and saves.
  • Constructs and Structures are Vulnerable to your [Unarmed Damage].
  • Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals [Unarmed Damage] you can push the target 10 feet in any direction except upwards.
  • You gain DR (damage reduction) vs all Damage equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.

features.name: Yin Seal: Release
features.description:
Beginning at 18th Level, you learn to unleash massive surges of chakra, augmenting your strength to monstrous levels. You increase the number of Yin Seals you can maintain by 2, increasing your maximum to 8. You gain the following abilities for which you can spend Yin Seals to use.
Tiger Lily Drive. As an Action, you can spend 3 Yin Seals to dash with the intensity of a missile, moving in a 60-foot line, passing through enemies like a living warhead. Each creature you pass through must make a Dexterity saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC. On a failed save, they take your [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + 3d6, are pushed 20 feet in a direction of your choice (excluding upwards), are knocked prone, and must spend their full movement to stand before the end of their next turn, or half as much on a success.
Camellia Rupture. In place of an attack, you make when you take the Attack action, you can spend 3 Yin Seals. When you do, make a melee Taijutsu attack that collapses the ground underneath you from the force of the strike alone. On a hit, you deal [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + [Unarmed Damage] + 3d12, ignoring resistance and treating immunity as resistance and the damaged creature must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Taijutsu (or Ninjutsu, your choice) save DC, being Stunned until the end of their next turn on a failed save or no further effect on a successful save.
Orchid Pulse. As a Bonus action, you can spend 3 Yin Seals to release a pulse of Medical chakra reinforcing your allies on the front lines. All willing creatures of your choice in a 20-foot radius, centered on you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to twice your Channeled Healing bonus, end one condition of your choice, and gain immunity to Bruised, Bleed, Daze, Slowed, Blinded or Stunned until the end of your next turn.

resources.name: Yin Seals
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L2, 6 at L10, 8 at L18

subclasses.name: Reaper
subclasses.description: Pact-bound medics who summon fragments of the Reaper's power — Grim-prefix jutsu, Curse of the Reaper, and hex-driven combat with power/polearm/scythe weapons.

features.name: Reapers Techniques
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level, you learn the secrets to summoning fragments of the Reaper through dark Fuinjutsu, allowing you to utilize its frightening power. You learn specific jutsu tailored to make use of the Reapers tools, gaining the listed jutsu when you reach the listed level. Jutsu learned from this feature does not count against your jutsu known as seen on your Medical-Nin Class Chart.
Jutsu you cast with the Grim prefix with a casting time of 1 Action, of D-Rank or lower can be cast as a Bonus Action. If cast in this way it cannot be cast as an Action on the same turn. This extends to C-Ranks at 10th level and B-Ranks at 18th level.

REAPER EXPANDED JUTSU
2nd  Medical Release: Grim Mask, Medical Release: Grim Weapon
6th  Medical Release: Grim Blade, Medical Release: Grim Legion
10th Medical Release: Grim Calling
14th Medical Release: Grim Reality

features.name: Reapers Arsenal
features.description:
Also, at 2nd level, your one-sided pact with the Reaper manifests in your martial form an unnatural grip on vitality. You gain proficiency with Martials Weapons from the Power and Polearm Groups, as well as the Kama, Kusarigama, Scythe, and Triple-Bladed Scythe. The Kama, Kusarigama, Scythe, and Triple-Bladed Scythe lose their Blade grouping and instead count as both a Power and Polearm Weapon. You also gain one of the following Bukijutsu Stances located in Chapter 13: Customization Options (Power Stance, Great Weapon Stance, Polearm Stance or Two-Weapon Stance.).
Additionally, when you damage a creature with a weapon attack or with a Jutsu with the Grim prefix, you may choose to mark them with the Curse of the Reaper for the next minute. A creatures marked with this is treated as Cursed. You can only have one creature cursed at a time, and cursing a new creature removes the curse from a previous one. A creature cursed by you suffers one of the hex effects found within the Curse itself.

features.name: Entropic Combatant
features.description:
Starting at 6th level your technique with weapons allows you to continue to pressure and punish your enemies in the heat of combat. You can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action.
You can also use your Bladed Chakra Scalpel ability, coating a weapon you wield, with its slicing power. When you do, you no longer add your Ability modifier to its damage rolls, instead adding your Channeled Healing bonus in its place and its damage type becomes Slashing.
Also, once per turn, when you cast a Bukijutsu with the Chain keyword, targeting a creature marked by your Curse of The Reaper, you can select a listed Chain 1 or 2 effect and always benefit from it, regardless of your current chain rank.

features.name: Medical Domain: Reapers Verdict
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your pact with the Reaper becomes symbiotic. Now instead of you siphoning off power for your own benefit, the Reaper has taken note of what you are doing and demands payment for its shared abilities. You can invoke this Domain ability, spending 1 Charge, marking a creature you can see within 60 feet of you as Gravetouched for the next minute or until it dies. While Gravetouched the creature is Cursed as if under the effects of your Curse of The Reaper (You still select a Hex effect). Marking another creature with Curse of the Reaper does not end this Gravetouched effect.
When you deal [Weapon Damage] or damage with jutsu with the Grim prefix to a Gravetouched creature you apply one of the following additional effects based on the Hex affecting them.
Vitality Hex. All willing creatures within 20 feet of you regain hit points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus instead of a single one.
Tributing Hex. The affected creatures increases the cost of their next jutsu by an amount equal to your Channeled healing Bonus before the beginning of your next turn.
Withering Hex. The affected creatures' penalties apply to jutsu you cast as well.

features.name: Jashin's Harvest
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, your bond with the Reaper allows you to twist chakra and vitality itself, siphoning power from those daring to use it in your presence. When a hostile creature affected by Curse of the Reaper or Gravetouched casts a Jutsu within 15 feet of you, you can use your Reaction to channel the Reaper's toll. When you do, a wave of necrotic chakra erupts around the cursed creature. All hostile creatures within 15 feet of the triggering creature must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu Save DC, or take 7d6 chakra damage on a failed save (This increases to 9d6 at 18th level).
On a success, they only take half damage, and no further effects apply. If one or more hostile creatures fail this saving throw, then all willing creatures of your choice within 20 feet of you regain chakra points equal to your Channeled Healing bonus.
After you use this ability, all instances of Curse of the Reaper and Gravetouched are immediately dispelled. You must reapply the Curse or Domain Mark again through normal means.

features.name: Hexing Expansion
features.description:
Also at 10th level, the number of Hex's you can inflict through your Curse of the Reaper increases. When you inflict Curse of the Reaper or Gravetouched on a creature, you can also choose to select one of the following Hex's.
Devouring Hex. A creature marked with Devouring Hex has chains of chakra latch onto them almost binding their soul. While hexed in this way, the first time each turn a Hexed creature would cast a jutsu that costs chakra, they increase the cost of the jutsu by your Channeled Healing bonus, and they reduce number of jutsu they can maintain concentration on by 1.
Executioner's Hex. A creature marked with the Executioner's Hex has the symbol of Jashin placed upon them. For the duration, once per turn, when you take damage, the hexed creature takes necrotic damage equal to the result. This necrotic damage ignores resistance and immunity.

features.name: Soul Draining
features.description: Starting at 14th level, your Grim jutsu has become an extension of the Reapers insatiable will. Once per turn, when you cast a jutsu with the Grim prefix that deals damage as part of its casting, you regain hit points equal to half the damage dealt. If the effect would affect multiple creatures, you regain half the damage rolled.

features.name: Avatar of the Reaper
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, your contract with the Reaper reaches its zenith, twisting your Fuin-curses into a conduit of absolute control. You can now have up to two creatures marked with Curse of the Reaper at the same time. Both marked creatures share the same Hex effect. Cursing a third creature removes the effect from the one with the oldest duration.
Also, as a Bonus Action, you may shift a hex currently applied to all creatures affected by Curse of the Reaper or Gravetouched, replacing it with a different hex of your choice. You may swap Hexes in this way once per turn.
Finally, you gain the final Hex taught directly from the Reaper and his progeny, Reapers Hex.
Reapers Hex. A creature marked with the Reapers hex is marked for death and doom. Any time they would regain hit points or chakra or gain temporary hit points or temporary chakra; you instead regain it in their place.
Additionally, when they are reduced to 0 hit points or chakra points, it erupts in a burst of necrotic chakra. All enemies within 15-feet of the erupting creature must make a Charisma saving throw, taking 6d6 necrotic and chakra damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success. Regardless of a success of failure, all allied creatures within 15-feet of the erupting creature is bathed in rejuvenating energies, regaining 4d6 hit points.

subclasses.name: Transmuter
subclasses.description: Cellular-alteration specialists — biology benders who reshape allies and enemies through Transfiguration designations (Lion, Dog, Bear, etc.) and chimeric upgrades.

features.name: Transfigured Technique
features.description:
Beginning at 2nd level, you learn to transmute the physical form of creatures through precise medical chakra control, manipulating their cellular structure, imitating other things with precise and unparalleled precision. You can utilize two different versions of the same ability against creatures you can touch called Transfiguration. As a Bonus Action, by touching a creature you can attempt to apply this status to an ally or enemy.
If selecting an allied creature, they must be willing and they gain the benefits of this feature for the next minute. Only one allied creature can be under this effect at once. Transfiguring an allied creature while another allied creature is under this effect, ends the oldest instance of an ally's Transfigured status.
If selecting an enemy, they must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC and on a failed save they suffer the effects of this feature until the end of your next turn. Applying this effect to a creature already Transfigured, extends it duration by 1 round, until the end of your next turn.
Select a biological designation when applying this affect. The chosen designation applies effects to the affected creature for the duration or until you use this feature against them again.

TRANSFIGURATION (Special Amp — Fortitude)
You are temporally Transfigured, having your cells, organs and biological makeup slightly adjusted to better match one of the following designations chosen by the allied Medical-Nin that applied this effect:
  • Avian. You gain a flying speed equal to your movement speed, but must land at the end of your turn. You gain immunity to fall damage and cannot be knocked prone.
  • Carnivoran. You gain a bonus to your [Unarmed Damage] and [Weapon Damage] equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Herbivore. You gain DR equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Insectoid. You gain Darkvision and Chakra Sight out to 60 ft and resistance to Chakra Damage.

features.name: Condition Override
features.description:
Also at 2nd level, your mastery over cellular manipulation enables you to rewrite how the body responds to pain, trauma and chakra disruption.
When you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that would inflict a Physical or Elemental condition, you can choose to replace the jutsu's applied condition, and instead applying the Weakened or Bruised condition. You must choose to replace the condition when the jutsu is cast.
If a creature is currently Transfigured they cannot make saving throws with advantage to resist conditions you impose as a result of Jutsu with the Medical keyword.

features.name: Transmutation
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your control over biological transmutation deepens, allowing you to restructure cells through the simplest of touches or the lightest of incisions.
You can change your Bladed Chakra scalpel's damage type to Necrotic and its range increases to 10-feet as you tear enemies apart at the molecular level from a distance and Once per turn, when you deal damage with your Bladed Chakra Scalpel that creature must make a Constitution saving throw vs your Ninjutsu save DC, gaining 1 rank of Weakened and becoming Transfigured as you alter their DNA with each strike.
When you have your Mystical Palm active, if you apply Transfigured to an allied creature, you can select two biological designations allowing the chosen creature to benefit from two instead of one.

features.name: Medical Domain: Transmuted
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, your ability to augment the biological makeup of your allies' blooms to new heights. As a Bonus action, you can invoke this Domain ability by spending 1 charge. When you do, you release a pulse of biotramutative chakra in a 20-foot radius centering on yourself augmenting your allies beyond the norm, until the beginning of your next turn. Select one of the following biological designations.
Lion: Affected creatures gain a rank of mastery to their attack rolls and a +1 bonus to their critical threat range.
Dog: Affected creatures gain a +2 bonus to their AC while within 10 feet of another allied creature.
Bear: Affected creatures gain advantage on Strength and Constitution Saving throws.
Deer: Affected creatures gain advantage on Intelligence and Wisdom saving throws.
Rabbits: Affected Creatures gain advantage on Dexterity and Charisma saving throws.

features.name: Chimeric Mutation
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, your mastery of altered biology reaches beyond nature and into the impossible. You learn to transfigure creatures into chimeric, otherworldly specimens, granting powerful and unnatural modifications. When you use your Transfiguration feature you can now choose from the listed additional designations for either allies or enemies.

CHIMERIC MUTATION — TRANSFIGURATION

Enhancement (applied to allies):
  • Aberration. You gain resistance to psychic damage and immunity to the Mental conditions.
  • Demonic. Once per turn, you gain a bonus to your jutsu-based damage equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.
  • Monstrosity. You have advantage on the first roll of a d20 made each turn with regards to checks and attacks.

Penalty Options (applied to hostiles):
  • Aberration. A transfigured (Aberration) creature suffers disadvantage on all checks relying on any of their 5 senses and they cannot benefit from jutsu-based bonuses.
  • Demonic. A transfigured (Demonic) creature becomes unstable; the first time each turn they take damage, they must make a Constitution saving throw vs the Medical-Nin's Ninjutsu save DC, being Dazed on a failed save.
  • Monstrosity. A transfigured (Monstrosity) creature loses control of their own bodily functions, wanting to attack everything around it. If more than 1 creature is within 10 feet of it, it suffers a -1 penalty to hit for every creature within 10 feet of it.

features.name: Metachirurgical Mastery
features.description: Also at 10th level, when you cast a Jutsu with the Medical keyword that inflicts a Physical condition, you can choose to swap the condition that would be inflicted with instead the Transfiguration condition, inflicting that in place of the condition listed by the jutsu.

features.name: Transmuted Self
features.description: Starting at 14th level, your body can better handle your twisted alterations. When you would apply Transfiguration to yourself, you may select up to two benefits. You can only use this feature once every 10 minutes.

features.name: Transmogrified Biology
features.description:
Starting at 18th level, your control over biological matter has reached its apex, no longer bound by earthly templates, you tap into the mythical genetics of Sage Beasts, being of legendary composition and mysterious history. You gain the following benefits;
When you apply Transfiguration to a hostile creature, you can choose to maintain concentration on it as if concentrating on a Jutsu, for up to 1 minute at no chakra cost, instead of it ending at the end of your next turn.
Additionally, you learn to transfigure creatures DNA to more closely match a Sage beast. You can choose from the new designation, Sage Beast.

TRANSMOGRIFIED BIOLOGY — TRANSFIGURATION

Enhancement:
  • Sage Beast. At the start of each of your turns, you regain hit points equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus. If you are at full hit points, you instead gain temporary hit points equal to the same value. You also gain a +2 bonus to your critical threat range, and once per turn you can spend half your movement to select a space you can see within 60 feet that can hold you, teleporting to the chosen space.

Penalty Options:
  • Sage Beast. A transfigured (Sage Beast) creature suffers uncontrollable chakra overflow and physical instability.
    • At the beginning of a transfigured creature's turn, they take necrotic damage equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus as their cellular structure continues to break down.
    • When the affected creature casts a jutsu, they must roll a d20. On a roll of 5 or more, their jutsu cast has its cost increased by an amount equal to the Medical-Nin's Channeled Healing bonus.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain an additional rank of Mastery in Medicine.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.

resources.name: Medical Domain Charges
resources.maxFormulaDescription: 4 at L3, 5 at L11, 6 at L17

features.name: Weapon Focus
features.description:
Starting at 1st level, select one weapon group from the following list, that you wish to specialize in: Blade, Flail, Polearm, Power or Thrown/Ammunition. Weapons that are part of the chosen weapon group are those that you specialize in. You also gain proficiency in Exotic weapons of the chosen group.
Weapons within the chosen weapon group are henceforth known as your Weapon Focus. While you are wielding your Weapon Focus:
• Bukijutsu you cast with your Weapon Focus as a component can use your Dexterity in place of Strength as your Taijutsu ability modifier.
• Checks made with the Martial Arts skill can use your Dexterity in place of your Strength.
Additionally, your skill and technique with your Weapon Focus allows you to enhance it further, with weapon properties from the following table. If your weapon already has a selected weapon property, you can apply an additional rank of that property to the weapon following that property rules presented in Chapter 5: Equipment of the Shinobi Handbook.
You can select another property when you reach 7th and again at 13th levels.

features.name: Discipline
features.description:
Also, at 1st Level, years of relentless training and unyielding resolve have forged your body and mind into weapons as sharp and durable as the steel you wield. You possess a pool of Discipline, represented as your capacity to execute advanced weapon techniques, maintain perfect form or even unleash decisive strikes in battle. You begin with 2 Discipline. You can spend Discipline to use a Flurry Technique. Flurry Techniques utilize a Flurry Die which is represented as a d12.
Some Flurry Techniques require affected creatures to make a saving throw. You always use your Taijutsu Save DC for such techniques. You can gain additional Flurry Technique options from your Weapon Form (Subclass) or your chosen Styles as you grow in level. You begin with the following Flurry Techniques.
You regain spent Discipline in one of two ways.
• When you complete a short or long rest you recover all spent Discipline.
• Once per turn, when you cast a Bukijutsu with the Chain keyword, you regain a number of Discipline equal to the Chain Rank effect you've benefited from.

DEFLECTION
As a reaction to taking damage, you can spend 1 Discipline to roll 1 Flurry Die. Reduce the triggering damage taken by the result. This increases to 2 Flurry Die at 11th and 3 Flurry Die at 20th.

CHAINED MOMENTUM
Once per turn, as part of castings a Bukijutsu that doesn't have the Chain keyword, you can spend 1 Discipline. When you do, the jutsu cast gains the Chain keyword, and you can maintain your current chain value.
Alternatively, Once per turn, if your Bukijutsu has the Chain keyword, you can spend 2 Discipline and choose to either increase or decrease your current Chain rank by 1.

CHAKRA STRIKE
Once per turn, when you deal [Weapon Damage] as part of a Bukijutsu you cast, you can spend 1 Discipline. When you do, the first time you deal damage with the triggering Bukijutsu, you add +1 Flurry Die. This increases to +2 at 5th level, +3 at 11th level, +4 at 15th level and +5 at 20th level. If the Bukijutsu affects an area, reduce the Flurry Die rolled by half (Min 1). This is treated as [Weapon Damage] for the purpose of class features.

features.name: Weapon Stance
features.description: Finally at 1st Level you adopt a particular Weapon Stance as your specialty. Choose a Bukijutsu Stance and its associated Bukijutsu Stance Expertise located in Chapter 13: Customization Options and you also gain an associated Bukijutsu Stance Expertise.

features.name: Katas
features.description: Starting at 2nd level, through discipline, creativity, and battlefield instinct, you develop unique Shinobi Katas that shape the way you fight. You learn one Katas of your choice. Your Kata options are detailed at the end of this Class. You gain more as you grow in level as detailed in the Weapon Specialist class table. If a Kata requires a saving throw, you use your Taijutsu save DC unless otherwise specified.

features.name: Augmentation
features.description:
Also at 2nd level, your training has allowed you to teach yourself different ways to augment your body passively. This comes about in a variety of ways. Select one of the following Augmentations. You can select an additional Augmentation at 9th and 15th levels.

TACTICAL AUGMENTATION
You have a mind for tactics on an off the battlefield. When you make an Intelligence or Wisdom based skill check, you can spend 1 Discipline to make the triggering check with advantage.

PSYCHE AUGMENTATION
Through training your body, you've also trained your mind. When you would make a Wisdom or Charisma saving throw against a Genjutsu or a mental effect, you can spend 2 Discipline to make the saving throw with advantage.

MOBILE AUGMENTATION
You've trained your body to move with such intent, that you can cross long distances in a much swifter time than others. On your turn, you can spend 1 Discipline to roll 1 Flurry Die. You gain additional speed equal to 5 times the result and can take the Dash action as a Bonus action until the end of the current turn.

SENSORY AUGMENTATION
You've trained your perceptive capabilities to sense other creatures presence or even intents. On your turn, you can spend 1 Discipline to expand your sensory range. While out of combat, for the next minute you gain 30 feet of a special sense of your choice between; Tremor sense and Blind Sight. While in combat, you pick between both senses, but they last until the beginning of your next turn.

RESTORATIVE AUGMENTATION
You've trained your body to restore and reset itself in a shorter burst of self-healing via intense training and focus. On your turn, by spending 1 Discipline and a Bonus action, you regain hit points equal to 1 Flurry Die. This increases to 2 Flurry Die at 9th and 3 Flurry Die at 15th. Once you use this feature you cannot use it again until the end of your next turn. Additionally, you cannot regain hit points in this way outside of combat as your body requires adrenaline for this to function.

ENDURING AUGMENTATION
You've trained your body to persist through any punishment. When you make a Strength or Constitution saving throw, you can spend 2 Discipline to make the saving throw with advantage.

features.name: Eye for Skill
features.description:
Finally at 2nd level, you can evaluate the combat potential of creatures from a passing glance while speaking, to even while in the heat of combat. As a bonus action, you can select a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, you can make a Martial Arts check vs a creatures Level DC. If you've dealt damage to this creature this turn, you gain a rank of Mastery to the check.
On a success, you learn one characteristic of your choice from below. On a failure, you cannot use this feature on the same target again until you score a hit against them on your next turn.
• Armor Class
• Condition Immunities/Resistances.
• Damage Immunities/Resistances.
• Highest or Lowest Ability Score.
• One trait they have (General, Clan, Role, etc.)
• One special Action they can take.

features.name: Combat Doctrine
features.description: When you reach 3rd Level, you begin to focus your studies on a combat doctrine. Your Doctrine grants you features at 3rd level, and again at 6th, 10th, 14th & 17th levels.

features.name: Ability Score Improvement / Feat
features.description: When you reach 4th and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th, level, you can increase one ability score by +1, gain a Feat of your choice that you qualify for and gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice. As normal you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

features.name: Extra Attack
features.description:
Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.
Additionally, if you use your action to Dash or to Disengage you can make one weapon attack as part of that action.

features.name: Critical Focus
features.description:
Also at 5th level, weapons treated as your Weapon Focus gain +1 rank of the Critical property. Additionally, the Critical property now also applied to Taijutsu attacks made with your Weapon Focus.
When you reach 11th level your Weapon Focus gains an additional +1 rank of Critical. This increases by another +1 rank of Critical at 18th level.

features.name: Chaining Adept
features.description:
Starting at 7th Level, once per turn when you would cast a Bukijutsu with the Chain keyword, you gain the select benefit based on the Chain rank the jutsu is cast at:
Chain 1: You can remake a missed attack, once per casting.
Chain 2: You can reduce its cost by an amount equal to its rank (D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5).
Chain 3: Allied creatures (other than yourself) within 10 feet of you gain Temporary Hit points equal to 1 Flurry Die. This increases to 2 Flurry Die at 11th and 3 Flurry Die at 15th level. You gain half the result as temporary hit points.

features.name: Inspiring Words
features.description: Also at 7th Level, your presence and words encourage others to fight on through their injuries. While in combat, as an Action, by spending 2 Discipline you plant your Weapon Focus into the ground as a show of comradery, all allied creatures within 30 feet of you regain hit points equal to 1 Flurry Die + one of its Hit Die, but it does not spend their hit die as part of this ability.

features.name: Weapon Focus (2)
features.description: You can select another property when you reach 7th and again at 13th levels.

features.name: Superior Discipline
features.description:
Beginning at 9th level, you gain an advanced level of proficiency with some of your most basic Flurry Techniques. Select two of the following benefits. You select an additional benefit at 13th and 18th levels.
Redirect. When you use the Deflection flurry technique the triggering creature takes damage equal to the reduced amount. The type of damage is the same type as your held weapon.
Warden. You can use Deflection when an allied creature within 10 feet of you takes damage, applying its effects to them.
Persistence. You can use Deflection without spending a reaction. But if you do, you can only use it once per turn.
Impulse. You can use Chained Momentum when casting any Jutsu without the Chain keyword, not just Bukijutsu.
Stimulus. When you use Chained Momentum you gain Temporary Chakra equal to twice your current Chain rank.
Blitz. When you use Chakra Strike, you can choose to remove the Discipline cost. If you do, reduce the number of Flurry Die added by half.
Assault. You can use Chakra Strike as part of your Attack action. When you do, you roll only 1 Flurry Die, but apply it to every attack made as part of the Attack action.
Raid. You can use Chakra strike to enhance an allied creatures Taijutsu or Bukijutsu. As a reaction to them casting a Taijutsu or a Bukijutsu, you can use Chakra Strike applying its benefits to them. This counts against your use limits for the turn.

features.name: Augmentation (2)
features.description: You can select an additional Augmentation at 9th and 15th levels.

features.name: Flurry Attack
features.description:
Starting at 11th level, when you take the attack action, you can spend 2 Discipline to make one additional attack as part of the attack action.
Additionally, you can spend 2 Discipline to use the Multiattack weapon property once on each of your turns, at no bonus action cost.

features.name: Critical Focus (2)
features.description: When you reach 11th level your Weapon Focus gains an additional +1 rank of Critical. This increases by another +1 rank of Critical at 18th level.

features.name: Chakra Sunder
features.description:
Starting at 13th level, when you see a creature casting a jutsu that will affect you within 60 feet, you can spend your reaction and 3 Discipline to cast a Bukijutsu with a Casting time of 1 Action. The Bukijutsu cast gains the Clash keyword and immediately initiates a Clash with the triggering jutsu.
If the jutsu cast is at a rank equal to or higher than triggering jutsu, you make your Clash check at advantage.

features.name: Weapon Focus (3)
features.description: You can select another property when you reach 7th and again at 13th levels.

features.name: Superior Discipline (2)
features.description: You select an additional benefit at 13th and 18th levels.

features.name: Augmentation (3)
features.description: You can select an additional Augmentation at 9th and 15th levels.

features.name: Critical Focus (3)
features.description: This increases by another +1 rank of Critical at 18th level.

features.name: Superior Discipline (3)
features.description: You select an additional benefit at 13th and 18th levels.

features.name: Dauntless
features.description:
Starting at 20th level, you rival legendary warriors and artisans of technique. You have become a weapon user of myth and prestige. You regain 1 Discipline at the beginning of each of your turns and you reduce the Discipline cost of all Katas by 1, to a minimum of 1.
Additionally, all allied creatures within 15 feet of you feel the weight of your presence on the battlefield. The first time that they would fail a saving throw, you can spend 2 Discipline to allow them to remake their saving throw. Once you use this feature on an allied creature, you cannot use it again on them until they complete a rest.

subclasses.name: Battle Dancer
subclasses.description: Ruthlessly efficient duelists who turn combat into choreography — switching between Samba and Waltz dances to overwhelm foes through mobility and flourish.

features.name: Dazzling Dances
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your every strike flows like choreography, weaving battle into a performance. To you, the clash of steel is no different than music, and combat is your dance floor. When you choose the Battle Dancer Doctrine, your utilization of weapons that you can move gracefully with is a beautiful symphony to onlookers.
While you are in combat, you can spend a Bonus action on your turn, to enter one of two different Dances; Samba or Waltz
You can dance in this way for the duration of Combat and can end early at no action on your turn. You can spend a Bonus action to switch your dance.

SAMBA (Battle Amp)
You move rhythmically, with a swift and decisive dance, making it hard to foresee your next move. While in this dance:
  • Whisk. As part of dealing damage with a Bukijutsu, you can spend 1 Discipline. You can reroll the damage dealt, taking the highest result.
  • Spin Step. As part of a weapon attack, you can spend 2 Discipline to make the attack target all hostile creatures within your weapon's range. If your weapon is a ranged weapon or has the Thrown property, you instead target all hostile creatures within 15 feet of you. Compare your attack result to the AC of all targeted creatures, scoring a hit on a success.
  • Technical Step. Once per turn, as a bonus action you can spend 2 Discipline to perform a special sequence while dancing. The next attack you make is at advantage. If this attack scores a critical hit you gain an additional action this turn.

WALTZ (Battle Amp)
You perform a smooth dance filled with elegant steps, but you can't do it alone. You require a Partner. At the beginning of your turn you select a Partner — a willing creature whom you can see who remains your Partner until the beginning of your next turn. You can only have 1 Partner at a time. When you begin your turn within 10 feet of your willing Partner, you both gain the following benefits:
  • Move in Tandem. If you or your Partner goes first in the turn order, either of you can spend 1 Discipline to allow the other to take their turn at the same time, on the same initiative count this round. When their turn comes up in the initiative count, it is skipped until the next round. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this ability, they can spend 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.
  • Battle Step. In place of your movement, you and your partner select two spaces adjacent to each other that either of you can reach with your movement and perform an aggressive maneuver, moving to the chosen spaces. This spends all of the user's movement.
  • Cascade Step. When you take the Attack action or cast a jutsu that requires an attack, you can spend 1 Discipline to force the affected creature to make a Dexterity saving throw vs the Weapon Specialist's Taijutsu Save DC. On a failure, they suffer a -2 penalty to their AC until the beginning of your next turn. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this ability, they can spend 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.
  • Defensive Dance. When you or your Partner take damage you can spend your Reaction and 2 Discipline to gain resistance to the triggering damage. If a Partner without access to Discipline uses this, they can instead spend their reaction and 1 of the Weapon Specialist's Discipline to use this ability.

actions.name: Technical Step
actions.description: While in Samba, once per turn spend 2 Discipline: your next attack is at advantage; if it's a critical hit, gain an additional action this turn.

actions.name: Defensive Dance
actions.description: While in Waltz, when you or your Partner take damage, spend 2 Discipline (or 1 if a Partner without Discipline triggers it): gain resistance to the triggering damage.

features.name: Flourish!
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your Doctrine guides you to learn special Flurry Techniques that supplement your Dances Form While you are wielding weapons with the Thrown, Finesse, Reach, Multiattack, Light or Ranged properties.
Echo Step. When you score a hit with a weapon or Taijutsu attack, you can spend 1 Discipline to push the affected creature 10 feet and move yourself up to 10 feet as part of the hit. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.
• If you are in the Waltz your Partnered creature moves with you and can make a weapon attack as part of their movement against the same creature.
• If you are in the Samba, the affected creature is also knocked prone or disarmed of any weapon or item they are holding (Pick one)
Shifting Rhythm. When you would begin your turn with a Physical or Mental condition, you can spend 2 Discipline and roll 1 Flurry Die. On a roll of 10 or higher, you can remove all ranks of the chosen condition, otherwise, you remove 1 rank.
• If you are in the Waltz you can choose to instead use this feature on your Partnered creature instead of yourself.
• If you are in the Samba, you can use this Flurry technique with Elemental conditions as well.
Inspiring Flourish. In place of an attack, you make as part of the Attack action or a Bukijutsu you cast you can perform an inspiring flourish meant to reinvigorate your allies by spending any number of Discipline. When you do, For each Discipline spent, select one allied creature, granting them a special Reaction which they can use to either move, make two weapon attacks or cast a jutsu of D-Rank or lower.
• If you are in the Waltz and target your Partner, they can gain two special reactions, but cannot use them for the same activity.
• If you are in the Samba, increase the rank of the jutsu they can cast to C-Rank, and then B-Rank at 14th level.

features.name: Twin Step
In the Spotlight
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your movements and poise command attention in ways that words alone cannot. While in a social encounter, by spending your Reaction and 1 Discipline, on an opposing creatures turn, you take the Spotlight from them until the end of their current turn.
While you have the Spotlight each time they attempt to make a Charisma or Wisdom check, you can roll 1 Flurry Die. On a roll of 7 or higher, they make their check at disadvantage as you distract them and the rest of the audience where applicable.

features.name: Spectacular Form
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, your dances evolve into spectacles of deadly artistry, blending elegance and destruction into perfect harmony. You enhance your given dances in the following ways;
Samba. You gain the following two abilities while in this dance.
• Encore. When you roll the maximum value on your [Weapon Damage] die, you can spend 1 Discipline to roll 1 additional [Weapon Damage] die, adding it to the result. You can use this ability on the additional [Weapon Damage] die rolled by this ability as well.
• Blazing Rhythm. When you score a hit with a Weapon or Taijutsu attack, you can spend 1 Discipline. For each successful attack, you can spend an additional 1 Discipline. For every Discipline spent, increase all subsequent attack roll results by +1. This stacks up to three times (+3) and lasts until the end of the current turn.
Waltz. While you are in this dance, you can have up to two different Partners.

features.name: Whirlwind Pirouette
features.description:
At 14th Level, you can use your Action to perform a whirlwind style sweep attack. Spend a number of Discipline and select a number of creatures equal to the Discipline spent within 15 feet of you and make a single melee Taijutsu attack as if casting a B-Rank Bukijutsu, comparing the result to each creatures AC.
On a successful hit, you deal your [Weapons Damage] + apply the Chakra Strike Flurry Technique's damage. Additionally, each creature you dealt damage to must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save affected creatures are knocked Prone, gain 3 ranks of Dazzled.

features.name: Dance of the Dawn
features.description:
At 17th level, your art transcends mere combat, becoming a performance that blurs the line between beauty and devastation.
As a bonus action you can spend 4 Discipline to enter both Samba and Waltz simultaneous, sustaining them both for 1 minute. While in this dancing state, you also can select two of the following benefits;
Guiding Rhythm. Your Partners when they begin their turn within 10 feet of you, gain a +1 bonus to their attack rolls and critical threat range until the end of their turns.
Rejuvenating Step. When you spend Discipline you and your Partners regain 4 hit points for each Discipline spent.
Spotlights Call. When a hostile creature targets you or a Partner, you or your Partners can spend your reaction so long as the triggering creature isn't targeting the creature using this reaction. When the reaction is spent, the targeted creature (yourself or your Partners) gain advantage on the triggering saving throw or the triggering attack is made at disadvantage targeting them.
Synchronous Steps. When yourself or a Partner casts a Jutsu of B-Rank or lower, yourself or the other Partners not casting the jutsu, can spend their reactions to cast a Jutsu, targeting the same creature of B-Rank or lower. Jutsu cast in this way must target the same creature, even if it would affect an area, the original target must be part of the selected area.

subclasses.name: Fang Breaker
subclasses.description: Savagely furious polearm/flail wielders — wide-arc Battle Trance strikers who dismantle defenses piece by piece.

features.name: Fang Breaker Fury
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your body floods with a violent fury and each swing of your weapon is driven by an instinctual need to break your enemies, weapons and all.
As a Bonus action, you can spend 1 Discipline to enter a chakra fueled Battle Trance, which lasts for 1 minute of until you fall unconscious. Once you exit this Battle Trance, you cannot enter it again until you complete 10 minutes of meditation or refocusing. If you would attempt to enter Battle Trance against prior to completing 10 minutes of meditation, increase the Discipline cost by 1, for every use beyond the first.

BATTLE TRANCE (Battle Amp)
While in Battle Trance you gain the following benefits:
  • Furious Advantage. You have advantage on checks and saving throws using your Taijutsu ability score.
  • Furious Resilience. You have resistance to one damage type of your choice (chosen when you enter Battle Trance). Increases to two choices at 10th level and three choices at 17th level.
  • Furious Focus. You cannot maintain concentration on non-Taijutsu or Bukijutsu based effects for the duration of your Battle Trance.

features.name: Savage Assault
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, your Doctrine guides you to learn special Flurry Techniques that supplement your Furious state while you are wielding a weapon from the Polearm or Flail groups.
Okuri-Inu Fang. When you would deal [Weapon Damage] to a hostile creature, you can spend 2 Discipline to force all affected creatures to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, affected creatures become Shaken.
Hachiman's Brutality. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a hostile creature, you can spend 2 Discipline to force all affected creatures to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, affected creatures become Hobbled.
Koyanes Awareness. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 Discipline gaining 60ft of special sense called Fracture Sense. You become aware of all creatures in your sensory range with ranks of a Physical, Mental or Elemental condition until the end of your next turn.

features.name: Berserkers Frenzy
features.description:
Beginning, at 6th level, your chakra fueled fury boils over into a Frenzy. While you are in a Battle Trance you gain the benefits of all abilities marked as Level 6 within the Battle Trance state.
Additionally, while in Battle Trance you gain immunity to the Charmed and Demoralized conditions.

BATTLE TRANCE — Level 6 Benefits (Battle Amp upgrade)
While in Battle Trance you also gain:
  • Frenzied Assault. When you deal damage with an attack or Bukijutsu that uses Polearm or Flail weapons, you add +2 damage. Increases to +4 at 14th level. At the start of your turn, you can forgo this damage bonus to gain advantage on the first attack you make using a Polearm or Flail weapon.
  • Frenzy Roar. When you take damage from a creature within 15 feet of you, you can spend your reaction and 2 Discipline to release a roar that shakes the battlefield. The triggering creature and all other hostile creatures within 15 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw, gaining 2 ranks of Demoralized on a failed save, or 1 rank on a successful save.
  • Undying Frenzy. When you would fall to 0 hit points, you can spend 3 Discipline to instead fall to 1 hit point. When you do, you end your Battle Trance state. You cannot use this ability again for 10 minutes.

features.name: Gut Feeling
features.description:
Also, at 6th level, while exploring or interacting in a social situation, by spending 3 Discipline you can ask the GM a single question about the situation you are in or a creature you are talking to. The GM responds not in a direct answer, but instead with a one worded impressions as if your Gut was telling you something from the following list;
• Safe
• Risky
• Trap
• Honest
• Deceptive
• Warning
• Advantageous
• Exposed

features.name: Savage Exploits
features.description:
Starting, at 10th level, your mastery of furious Flurry techniques grows into something far more devastating. The deliberate savagery of a predator who knows exactly where to break its prey. You gain the following additional Flurry Techniques.
Okuninushi Frenzy. When you score a critical hit that deals your [Weapon Damage] you can spend 2 Discipline to force one of the affected creature to make a Constitution saving throw, being Stunned on a failed save until the end of their next turn.
Raijin's Terror. When you score a critical hit that deals your [Weapon Damage] you can spend 2 Discipline to shatter an enemies focus. If they are concentrating on a jutsu, for the next minute they have disadvantage on Intelligence, Wisdom and Charisma saving throws and cannot concentrate on more than one jutsu at a time. A creature under this effect makes a Constitution saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending this effect on a success.
Fujin Cleave. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a hostile creature, you can spend 2 Discipline to deal the same [Weapon Damage] to all creatures of your choice adjacent to the original target.
Shuten-Doji Dismantling. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a hostile creature, you can spend a number of Discipline equal to the number of affected creatures. For each Discipline spent, one creature of your choice suffers a 1d4 penalty to their next saving throw or attack roll, whichever occurs first, before the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Aggressive Reprisal
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level, you have learned to focus your Battle Trance, allowing it to lash out at those reckless enough to harm you. While you are in your Battle Trance, each time you take damage you gain 1 Temporary Discipline (Max 4). You lose all temporary discipline at the beginning of your next turn. You can spend Temporary Discipline in place of normal Discipline to fuel your class features that call for it.
While you have Temporary Discipline, when you take damage from a hostile creature within range of your weapon attack, you can use your Reaction to spend 2 Discipline. When you do, you can cast a Bukijutsu of C-Rank or lower, with a casting time of 1 Action, targeting the triggering creature.

features.name: Titanic Wrath
features.description:
Starting, at 17th level, your fury is an eternal rhythm that thrums in your body, ready to erupt at the faintest spark. Your mastery of your Battle Trance reaches its peak, granting you the following benefits;
• You no longer need to spend a bonus action to enter Battle Trance. At the beginning of your turn, if you are not already in Battle Trance, you can choose to enter it automatically.
• Your Battle Trance no longer requires meditation or downtime to reenter. You may use it freely without spending 10 minutes in-between uses.
• The Discipline cost of Flurry Techniques granted by your Savage Exploits class feature is reduced by 1.
• When you fail a saving throw that uses your Taijutsu ability score, you can spend 1 Discipline to instead treat it as a success. If you use this ability again before completing a short rest, increase the Discipline cost to use this feature by +1 for each additional use beyond the first.

subclasses.name: Obsidian Guard
subclasses.description: Unbreakable defenders — heavy flail/hammer wielders with an Obsidian Ward that intercepts damage and a Provoke-Protect loop that anchors the battlefield.

features.name: Obsidian Ward
features.description:
Beginning at 3rd level, your indomitable spirit hardens into a shield formed of Obsidian chakra, forming a barrier between you and all who would harm your allies.
You summon an Obsidian Ward that has a number of hit points equal to 11 + your Level. The Obsidian Ward intercepts all damage dealt to you before your hit points. (The Ward also takes damage before your temporary hit points.) Excess damage carries over to your hit points if the wards hit points are reduced to or at 0. Your Obsidian Ward has the same resistances, immunities and DR as you.
Ward Restoration. Once per turn, on your turn, if you spend Discipline, the ward regains hit points equal to the result of one Flurry Die. The wards hit points can never exceed its maximum. This increases to the result of two dice at 10th level and three dice at 17th level. Alternatively, the ward also regains all of its lost hit points when you complete a rest of any type.

features.name: Warden Techniques
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you learn additional Flurry Techniques that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Power or Flail groups or a weapon with the Blocking property.
Provoke. In place of an attack made as part of your Attack action or by spending a bonus action, you can spend 1 Discipline to take an intimidating stance, intimidating gesture or make a cutting remark agitating the opposition to not avert their eyes. Select one hostile creature you can see that can also either see or hear you within 30 feet. They become Provoked until the beginning of your next turn. You can have one creature Provoked in this way. You can increase the number of creatures you can provoke by +1 at 6th and 14th levels.
Obsidian Projection. As a reaction, when an allied creature within 15 feet of you would take damage, by spending 1 Discipline you may redirect that damage to your ward instead. If the ward drops to 0, any excess damage carries over to you, not the ally. You cannot use this if your ward has 0 Hit points.
Punishing Guardian. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a hostile creature that has dealt damage to an allied creature of yours since the end of your last turn, you can spend 2 Discipline. When you do, you force them to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save they gain 2 ranks of Concussed or 1 rank of Concussed on a successful save.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description:
Beginning at 6th level, while your Obsidian Ward has hit points, all allied creatures of your choice (excluding yourself) within 15 feet of you gain a +1 bonus to their AC and saving throws. This increases to +2 at 14th level.
Additionally, your Obsidian Ward can regain hit points even when its not your turn, but still only once per turn.

features.name: Retributive Words
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your tendency to seek retribution and defend your allies spill over into even petty social engagements.
When a creature attempts a Charisma based check against an allied creature, you can spend your reaction and 2 Discipline to use a Retributive Argument.
Retributive Argument. Make an Intimidation check using your Taijutsu ability modifier in place of Charisma contested by the Charisma based check the opposing creature rolled. On a success, you nullify and interrupt any benefits that the check would have provided to the opposing creature.
Ex. This cancels an attempted persuasion check that would change another NPC's attitude or a deception attempt that would plant false info.

features.name: Obsidian Bulwark
features.description:
Starting at 10th level, your ward no longer feels like a fragile barrier but an extension of your body.
Your Obsidian Ward increases its maximum hit points by an amount equal to your proficiency bonus and whenever you roll your Flurry Die to restore the wards hit points, you can roll twice, taking the higher result.
You also gain the following additional Flurry Techniques that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Power or Flail groups or a weapon with the Blocking property.
Stonewall Reprisal. When your ward takes damage, you can spend 2 Discipline. When you do, you can spend your reaction and until the beginning of your next turn, each time a creature deals damage to your ward, you can make a weapon attack, adding the result of your [Weapon Damage] die to your wards hit points.
Shackle Guard. When an allied creature within 15 feet of your takes damage, you can spend your reaction and 2 Discipline, and move up to 10 feet towards the hostile creature that dealt the damage and make a weapon attack targeting them. On a hit, you deal your [Weapons Damage] plus 2 Flurry Die and force the affected creature to make a Strength saving throw being Slowed and Prone on a failed save.
Intimidating Presence. As a Bonus action, by spending 2 Discipline, you can release a frightening aura that immediately applies the Provoked condition on all hostile creatures within your Aura of Protection until the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Guardian Force
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level, your Obsidian Ward has grown and no longer requires active input from you to function. While in combat, at the beginning of each of your turns, you may roll 1 Flurry Die, adding the result to the Wards hit points.
Additionally, if an allied creature within your Aura of Protection would make a Strength, Dexterity or Constitution saving throw as a result of a jutsu a hostile creature casts, you can spend 2 Discipline to intercept the triggering effect, allowing you to become the new target of the triggering effect.

features.name: Boundless Guardian
features.description:
Starting, at 17th level, your chakra has been conjoined with your Obsidian spirit making you a force of protection and defense like none before.
First, you gain an additional Reaction, which you can only use for any feature or ability granted by this Doctrine.
Second, creatures that are Provoked remain so until they target or damage you with an attack or jutsu after which they lose the condition immediately.
Third, creatures that are Provoked take additional damage from your allies (not yourself) that begin their turns within your Aura of Protection, equal to 1 Flurry Die

subclasses.name: Primal Weapon
subclasses.description: Elemental-chakra wielders who manifest a Primal Weapon of pure nature-release energy and chain it with Ninjutsu for devastating hybrid combos.

features.name: Elemental Armaments
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you learn to bring out dormant natural energies. If you have access to one or more nature releases already, select one. It becomes your chosen nature release that this and other feature from this Doctrine references.
If you do not have access to one, you may select one (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning Release). You gain the ability to learn and cast jutsu with the corresponding keyword.
Additionally, you learn to manifest a weapon made of pure chakra. Once per turn at no action cost, you can manifest or dismiss your Primal Weapon. Your Primal Weapon takes the shape of weapon of your choice from your chosen Weapon Focus group, using that weapons statistics as its own but its damage counts as your nature releases corresponding damage type. If your Primal Weapon has the Light property, you can choose to summon two of them, with each one occupying one hand each.
Finally, you when you complete a long rest, you can infuse Weapon seals into your Primal Weapon found in Chapter 7: Adventuring & Missions, Weapon Enhancement Seals. You can select 2 D-Rank weapon seals to infuse into your Primal Weapon gaining their effects. At 6th level, you can select 1 C-Rank weapon seals. At 10th level, you can select 1 B-Rank weapon seal. At 14th level, you can select 1 A-Rank weapon seal. At 17th level you can select 1 S-Rank weapon seal.

features.name: Primal Strikes
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you learn additional Flurry Techniques that you can utilize while you are wielding your Primal Weapon.
Cross Synergy. When you cast a Ninjutsu with a nature release keyword you can spend 2 Discipline to grant the jutsu the Chain keyword. It also gains the Primal Chain effect and can count for a Chain rank 1, 2 or 3.
Elemental Augmentation. When an allied creature within 15 feet of you has ranks of an Elemental condition, you can spend 1 Discipline to target them and roll 1 Flurry Die. On a roll of 10 or more, you reduce all ranks of the chosen condition to 0. On a roll of 7 or more, you reduce the chosen condition ranks by 2, otherwise, reduce the ranks of the chosen condition by 1. Regardless of the ranks of conditions reduced, you infuse it into your Primal Weapon until the end of your next turn. For the duration, add 1 Flurry Die to your Primal Weapons damage of a type equivalent to the condition whose ranks you reduced. If the condition does not have an associated damage type, the damage type of the added Flurry Die becomes Force.
Disperse Chakra. When you deal [Weapon Damage] with your Primal Weapon, you can spend 2 Discipline in an attempt to disrupt one Ninjutsu or Genjutsu based effect currently applied to the affected creature. Make a roll using a d20 + 1 Flurry Die and compare the result to the jutsu casters save DC. On a success, the jutsu is Dispelled. If the effect doesn't have a Jutsu save DC then you instead use the Standard jutsu Ability Check DC found in Chapter 9: Jutsu Casting, Saving Throws section.

PRIMAL CHAIN (keyword)
Ninjutsu you cast that gain the Chain keyword (via Cross Synergy or otherwise) gain the associated Chain effect, regardless of the Chain rank used:
  • Earth Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Strength saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you magnify their weight as they succumb to the intense gravity. Reduce the number of attacks they can make and the number of creatures they can target with their next jutsu or attack by 1 (Min. 1). Penalty increases by 1 (2 reduced attacks/targets) at 10th, and again at 17th (3 reduced attacks/targets at 17th).
  • Wind Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a burst of blinding wind overcoming them as they're thrown 30 feet, fall prone and suffer half the result of 1d12 as a penalty to their next attack before the beginning of your next turn. Increases to their next two attacks at 10th and next three attacks at 17th level.
  • Fire Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Constitution saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a wave of flaming chakra overwhelming them as you drain them of their immediate fighting potential. They suffer a 1d12 penalty to their next damage roll. Increases to 2d12 at 10th and 3d12 at 17th level.
  • Water Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Constitution saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save you release a torrent of waves breaking your enemy and disorienting them as the next attack targeting them, until the end of your next turn, can be made at advantage. Increases to the next two attacks at 10th and the next three attacks at 17th level.
  • Lightning Release — If your jutsu requires an attack, on a hit the affected creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. If your jutsu already requires a saving throw, apply this effect on a failed save. On a failed save the affected creature is electrocuted and the next time they attempt to use their Action or Bonus action or at least 5 feet of movement they must roll a 1d12, losing the attempted action or movement if they roll 10 or more. The result needed to lose the attempted movement or action decreases to 8 or more at 10th level and 6 or more at 17th level.

features.name: Chained Cascade
features.description:
Beginning, at 6th level, your master of weaving ninjutsu into your Bukijutsu techniques allows you to unleash techniques in perfect succession.
When you cast a Ninjutsu of C-Rank or lower, as an Action, you can spend 3 Discipline to cast a Bukijutsu of the same rank as the triggering Ninjutsu at no additional action cost. Immediately after the Bukijutsu is cast you enter your Chained Cascade state until the end of your next turn. If you would enter this state again before it ends, its duration is extended by 1 round until the end of your following turn.
Beginning at 14th level, you can use this feature with Ninjutsu of B-Rank.

CHAINED CASCADE (Battle Amp)
Your chakra surges outward in a relentless cascade. While in Chained Cascade:
  • Chained Empowerment. You gain a number of temporary Chakra equal to twice the Chain rank you benefitted from as part of the Bukijutsu cast with your Chained Cascade feature. These temporary chakra points dissipate when this state ends.
  • Cascading Chakra. You gain a rank of Mastery to your Ninjutsu attacks and a +1 bonus to your Ninjutsu save DC.
  • Protective Barrier. As a reaction to an allied creature within 10 feet of you taking damage you can transfer all remaining temporary hit points granted by Chained Cascade to them, allowing the transferred temporary hit points to be applied to the triggering damage.

actions.name: Protective Barrier
actions.description: While in Chained Cascade, when an allied creature within 10 ft takes damage, transfer all remaining temporary HP from this state to them, applying it to the triggering damage.

features.name: Resonant Jaunt
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your chakra hums in rhythm with the world around you, letting you slip between the moments of each hum as if carried by invisible currents of chakra.
First, you gain a special sensory quirk, allowing you to sense elemental material or effects up to 1 mile away. You cannot sense materials or effects through chakra-based barriers or structures. Elemental material is defined as sources of an elemental effect such as;
• A Burning Torch or larger flame-based effect
• A Body of Water large enough to submerge yourself inside.
• A strong wind, capable of knocking over or moving unattended objects (20+ MPH winds)
• A collection of untreated stone or rock large enough to fill a 5-foot cube.
• A large enough collection of electricity potent enough to become visible to the naked eye such as a lightning bolt or storm cloud.
While you are not in combat, you can spend 1 minute focusing on an elemental material or effect you can see or sense within your sensory range. The GM has final say if a source qualifies.
Once 1 Minute has passed, you can teleport to a space within 10 feet of the elemental material or effect. You can spend a number of Discipline equal to 1 per additional creature you want to bring with you as part of this teleportation. Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you complete a rest of some type.

features.name: Primal Weapon Mastery
features.description:
Starting, at 10th level, you learn additional Flurry techniques that without your Primal Weapon, you otherwise would be unable to even fathom.
Elemental Mark. As a bonus action, you can spend 1 Discipline to mark a creature you can see within 60 feet of you with an Elemental Mark. A special Fuinjutsu seal designed to be perceived by you and you alone. You can sense them as if they were an elemental effect. A creature is marked until you mark another creature, it dies, or it leaves your sensory range, after which the mark dispels itself. When a marked creature attempts to a cast a jutsu, it can trigger an attack of opportunity from you. If you do, you can instead cast a Ninjutsu you know in place of an attack made as apart of your attack of opportunity.
Primal break. Once per turn, when you cast a Bukijutsu with a nature release keyword of the same type as your Primal Weapon you can spend 2 Discipline to upcast it at no additional chakra cost and ignoring rank-based limits.
