Chakra Network Overload. When you deal [Weapon Damage] with your Primal Weapon, you can spend 3 Discipline in an attempt to overwhelm the targets chakra network. One affected creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, they take chakra damage equal to your [Weapon Damage] plus the bonus damage from the Chakra Strike Flurry technique at no additional Discipline cost and any concentration check they make as part of taking this damage is made at disadvantage.

features.name: Reflective Sunder
features.description:
Starting, at 14th level, your ability to sunder an enemies chakra and jutsu with your own surpasses even your peers.
When you succeed on your clash check as part of Chakra Sunder you can spend 1 additional Discipline to redirect it back towards the original target as part of the successful clash. If it requires an attack, you make a Taijutsu attack (melee or ranged based on the triggering jutsu) applying its effects on its original caster on a successful hit. If the jutsu requires a saving throw, they make the saving throw listed in the jutsu, but against your Taijutsu save DC.

features.name: Break/Reinforce the Seal
features.description:
Starting, at 17th level, your ability to draw out ones latent primal power is a gift to you, but a curse to your foes, as you also know how to seal it away. When you use a Flurry technique that is applied as part of dealing [Weapon Damage], if that Flurry technique costs 4 or less Discipline, you can cause it to now cost 4 Discipline. If you do, you can choose to either Break the Seal or Reinforce the Seal.
Break the Seal. The affected creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save they feel their chakra network begin to rupture and release large amounts of chakra. For the next minute, they double the chakra cost of all jutsu they cast and reduce the damage they deal with Jutsu by half of a jutsu's damage dice. A creature under this effect remakes their saving throws at the end of each of their turns. On a success, they end this effect and cannot be affected by it again until you complete a rest.
Reinforce the Seal. The affected creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save they feel their chakra network working against them. Blocking off valves and paths they know they had, but can no longer access. For the next minute, they cannot cast jutsu that cost more than 12 chakra and they cannot maintain concentration on jutsu that have a concentration cost greater than 6 chakra. A creature under this effect remakes their saving throws at the end of each of their turns. On a success, they end this effect and cannot be affected by it again until you complete a rest.

natureReleaseGrants.description: Select one nature release (Earth, Wind, Fire, Water, or Lightning). If you already have multiple releases, the picker excludes already-owned ones.

subclasses.name: Seeker
subclasses.description: Ranged-combat specialists who mark targets with Vector Seals — triangulating pinpoint attacks, creating distance, and mapping the battlefield with chakra-tuned senses.

features.name: Converging Vector
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you read lanes, allies, rooftops, and wind as one geometry. With a subtle seal and a few steps of positioning, you turn the battlefield into a diagram where the marked point is always the answer.
As a bonus action, you can spend 1 Discipline to mark one creature you can see within 90 feet of you with a Vector Seal. A creature marked with a Vector Seal remains marked for up to 1 minute, until you are incapacitated, it dies, you mark a different creature, or it dispels the mark with an effect that can dispel D-Rank Genjutsu. You can maintain only one Vector Seal at a time.
While a creature is marked with a Vector Seal you gain the following bonuses against them;
Always Enough. You do not need to roll your Ammunition Die when making attacks that normally require them, when you are targeting the marked creature.
Triangulation. You gain a +1 bonus to hit with ranged weapon and Taijutsu attacks as a result of a Bukijutsu. This instead becomes a rank of Mastery if you have 1 or more allied creatures within 15 feet of the marked creature.
Course Correction. Once per turn, if you miss an attack against a marked target by 5 or less, you can spend 1 Discipline to ricochet the shot, allowing you to remake the attack against the same creature. If you do, this remade attack cannot score a critical hit.
Zeroing In. If the marked creature ends their turn without moving at least 5 feet, you gain advantage on the next attack roll targeting them before the end of your next turn.

features.name: Crossfire Aim
features.description:
Also at 3rd level, you learn additional Flurry Techniques that supplement your Seeker Doctrine that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Ammunition or Thrown weapon groups.
Critical Aperture. When you score a critical hit with a ranged weapon or Taijutsu attack as a result of a Bukijutsu, you can spend 2 Discipline to reduce the affected creatures AC or Saving throws by -1 (Pick one). This penalty lasts until the end of your next turn. This penalty increase to -2 at 10th level.
Snap Volley. When you deal [Weapon Damage], after the action concludes that enabled you to deal the triggering damage, you can spend 2 Discipline and your bonus action to make two ranged weapon attacks against the same or different creatures dealing 1 Flurry Die in damage using your weapons damage type. Increase the number of attacks you make as part of this Flurry technique by +1 at 10th and 17th levels.
Threaded Relay. Once per turn, when an allied creature deals damage to a creature marked with your Vector Seal, you can spend 1 Discipline to make 1 ranged weapon attack targeting the marked creature, dealing 1 Flurry Die in damage using your weapons damage type.

features.name: Creating / Maintaining Distance
features.description:
Starting at 6th level, you can both still your body, and immediately respond to someone closing in. Your body is capable of adapting rapidly to the changing circumstances of combat.
Creating Distance. When a hostile creature would make a melee attack against you and you are not grabbed, prone or restrained, you can spend your reaction and 2 Discipline to make a Dexterity saving throw vs the results of the attack roll. On a success, the attack misses you and you immediately move up to 20 feet away from the triggering creature without triggering attacks of opportunities and make a ranged weapon attack targeting them.
Maintaining Distance. When you begin your turn with a creature marked with your Vector Seal and haven't move more than 5 feet since the end of your last turn, you can spend 2 Discipline to gain advantage on the first ranged attack you make this turn targeting the marked creature. If either of the d20 land on a 1 or a 2, you can reroll that dice, taking the second result.

features.name: Vector Mapping
features.description:
Also at 6th level, your senses hum in tune with the spaces you occupy. You can feel the layout of any building, hallway or structure you are within imprint itself behind your eyes.
By spending 1 minute meditating while within a structure, giving off rhythmless ticks, odd sounds or even harmonistic hums, you delicately map out an area, a 200-foot radius centered on yourself. This mental map persists for 1 hour and includes the following information if it is within the given radius;
• Layout: Entrances/exits, corridors, shafts and doors.
• Hazards: You are aware that something in the affected area isn't normal. You cannot immediately discern what it is or how to avoid it.
This special sense cannot penetrate chakra-based barriers, constructs or structures and thus you cannot perceive them with it and it does not reveal creatures, illusions or secret compartments, rooms or storage devices such as chests, safes etc.
Each time you do this beyond the first, before you complete a rest, the time it takes to accomplish and reestablish focus increases by tenfold. Multiply the time it takes by 10 each time you use this feature beyond the first. The time it takes to complete resets to 1 minute, when you complete a rest of any type.

features.name: Dread Ambusher
features.description:
Beginning at 10th Level, you don't stalk prey. You redraw the map and let fear do that for you. Now two sit in your crosshairs, and neither know which is the target of the next strike.
You can have up to two creatures marked with your Vector Seal at a time, and when you spend your bonus action to mark them, you can mark both at the same time as part of the same action at no additional cost. Also, now your Vector Seal can only be dispelled with an effect that can dispel B-Rank Genjutsu.
You also learn additional Flurry techniques from the Seeker Doctrine that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Ammunition or Thrown weapon groups
Siphoning Seal. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a creature marked with your Vector Seal you can spend 3 Discipline. When you do, the triggering [Weapon Damage] and each subsequent [Weapon Damage] dealt reduces all creatures marked with the Vector Seals speed by 10 feet and they are unable to gain a bonus to their speed until the beginning of your next turn.
Point Blank Shot. If you make a ranged weapon or Taijutsu attack targeting a creature that is adjacent to you, you can spend 3 Discipline to make the attack without disadvantage, gain advantage, and if the target is marked with your Vector Seal you treat the attack as a critical hit if the result is 10 or more than the targets AC.
Tethering Shot. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to a creature marked with your Vector Seal you can spend 2 Discipline to force the other marked creature to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, they are each pulled 15 feet closer to the other as if snatched by an invisible wire. The creature who failed their saving throw is moved first and then the creature whom you dealt damage to is moved immediately afterwards.

features.name: Sharpshooting Savant
features.description:
Beginning at 14th Level, you learn to perform miracles with your ranged weapons.
On your first turn in combat, if you are not surprised, you can use your Action to attack creatures that have not acted yet. Spend any number of Discipline equal to the number of creatures that you want to target, that you can see within 90 feet of you. Make a ranged Taijutsu attack targeting each creature.
Additionally, once on each of your turns, when you make a ranged weapon or Taijutsu attack, you can spend 2 Discipline to attach a chakra rope to it. If you score a hit you can choose to flip up to 20 feet away from the target, and they must make a Strength saving throw, being pulled 20 feet towards you into a space of your choice, on a failed save.

features.name: Seeker of the End
features.description: Starting at 17th Level, your skill as a Seeker has become mythical, rivaling sages and huntsmen of legend. Whenever you score a hit against a creature marked with your Vector Seal, you can spend 3 Discipline, causing your [Weapon Damage] to be treated as the maximum possible value. If this damage reduces a creature classed as a Standard or Elite to 50 or fewer hit points, it must make a Constitution saving throw, or instantly by reduced to 0 hit points. If the creature is classed as a Solo, it makes its constitution saving throw at advantage.

subclasses.name: Reaver
subclasses.description: Blade/Power weapon specialists who trade space for inevitability — applying Reaver Echoes to Quarries for delayed-blast damage reduction and crowd control.

features.name: Reavers Quarry
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you focus your senses, honing in on your target, mentally marking them for execution. As a bonus action, you can mark a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The creature marked becomes known as your Quarry. You can only have one Quarry at a time. You can maintain a Quarry for up to 1 minute. Selecting a new Quarry removes the mark from the previous creature. If a creature marked as your Quarry is reduced to 0 hit points, you can select a new creature immediately at no additional action cost. While a creature is marked as your Quarry you gain the following benefits:
• You gain a +1 bonus to [Weapon Damage] against the marked creature. This increases to +2 at 10th and +3 at 17th level.
• Once per turn, when you deal [Weapon Damage] to your Quarry you can generate a number of Reaver Echoes on them equal to the rank of the Rank of the Bukijutsu cast (Weapon Attack/D-Rank: 1, C-Rank: 2, B-Rank: 3, A-Rank: 4, S-Rank: 5). Reaver Echoes dispel themselves if they are not detonated before the beginning of your next turn.

features.name: Kenjutsu
features.description:
Starting at 3rd level, you learn additional Flurry Techniques that supplement your Reaver Doctrine that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Blade or Power weapon groups.
Frenetic Counter. When a creature with Reaver Echoes that you can see would deal damage to you or an allied creature within 30 feet of yourself that you can see, you can spend your reaction and 1 Discipline to detonate them. A Reaver Echoes detonated in this way does not deal damage, but instead reduces damage by the result of dice rolled as part of their detonation. If the triggering creatures damaging action would detonate your Reaver Echoes anyway, you can use this Flurry Technique at no reaction cost.
New Moon. When you deal [Weapon Damage] to two or more creatures as part of the same jutsu's casting and would apply Reaver Echoes to one of them as part of the Reavers Quarry feature, you can spend 2 Discipline to instead generate 1 Reaver Echoes on each affected creature even if they are not marked as your Quarry. This increases to 2 Reaver Echoes at 10th and 3 Reaver Echoes at 17th level.
Sundering Detonation. When a hostile creature casts a Jutsu with the Barrier keyword that intercepts [Weapon Damage] you would deal, you can spend 1 Discipline to inflict the barrier with 3 Reaver Echoes. Reaver Echoes applied in this way detonate the next time the barrier takes damage.

features.name: Circle of Protection
features.description:
At 6th Level, you learn to fend off attacks directed at you and other nearby creatures. When you and at least one or more allied creatures within 20 feet of you would take damage from the same source, you can spend 3 Discipline and your reaction to create a force field of Reaver Echoes.
First, you can move up to your speed and end that movement in a space you can see that can hold you, that has one or more allied creatures adjacent to you.
Next, roll 2 flurry die recording the result. The triggering damage is reduced by the result for all willing allied creatures within 10 feet of your current position.

features.name: Reaver's Instincts
features.description:
Also at 6th Level, you learn to utilize unique social skills that other Weapon Specialist lack the Vipers Tongue and the Apex Glare abilities.
Viper's Tongue. You utilize wit, charm and a bit of social engineering to deceive even the most attentive of individuals. When you would attempt the Lie, Impersonate skill Action, you can spend 2 Discipline to add 1 Flurry die to the check. On a success, your lie is passed off as genuine based on the context of the lie itself. If you beat the DC by 5 or more, the lie is accepted as the absolute truth and contesting thoughts or opposing truths are treated as the lie.
Apex Glare. You attempt to break the will of your foe with but a glance. When you would take the Coerce or Gather Information skill action, you can spend 2 Discipline to add 1 Flurry die to the check. On a success, when gathering information, reduce the time it takes to gather said information down to 1 hour. If you beat the DC by 5 or more, you instead reduce the time it takes to 10 minutes.

features.name: Severance
features.description:
Starting at 10th Level, you fight during the pause between a heartbeats consequence. Swift and efficient. Brutal and merciless.
Your Reaver Echo expands in its usefulness. When a creature marked with your Reaver Echo spends their Bonus Action, it now triggers its detonation. Also, you can choose to extend the duration of Reaver Echoes. When a creatures Reaver Echo would detonate or dispel itself, you can spend 1 Discipline or your reaction. When you do, all currently active Reaver Echoes of your choice has their duration extended until the beginning of your next turn.
Additionally, you also learn additional Flurry techniques from the Reaver Doctrine that you can utilize while you are wielding a weapon from the Blade or Power weapon groups.
Reaver Gambit. If you make an attack against a creature with 1 or more Reaver Echoes, you can spend 2 Discipline and dispel one of their Reaver Echoes, to grant yourself advantage on the attack. If you roll advantage and both d20 land on the same number, regardless of the result, its treated as a critical hit and you gain regain all spent Discipline.
Wounding Scar. When one or more Reaver Echoes detonate, you can spend 3 Discipline to make each affected creature suffer a -1 penalty to their AC equal to the number of Reaver Echoes they had at detonation. This penalty lasts until the end of their next turn(s).
Burst Echo. In place of an attack made as part of the Attack action or a Taijutsu attack made as part of a Bukijutsu's casting, you can spend 2 Discipline and select a point that you can see within 60 feet of you. A Reaver Echo is manifested and detonates at the chosen point, causing all creatures within 5 feet of the chosen point to take 2d12 damage of a type equal to your held weapon and gain a Reaver Echo.

features.name: Delayed Displacement
features.description:
Starting at 14th Level, you learn to step into the wound you left in the world, and the world listens. With a single movement every delayed strike is moved to a single location.
As an Action, by spending 4 Discipline, or in place of an attack you would make as part of a Bukijutsu's casting and spending 4 Discipline, you displace all Reaver Echoes onto a single target.
All hostile creatures of your choice within 30 feet of you has their Reaver Echoes detonate and a single creature of your choice within the same radius gains a number of Reaver Echoes equal to the number of echoes that detonated as a result of this feature.

features.name: Reavers Judgement
features.description:
At 17th level, you've reached the pinnacle of your Reaver Doctrine training. You can split your attention cleanly now between more than one foe. You gain the following benefits:
• You maintain up to two Quarries at once instead of one. Selecting an additional Quarry beyond this limit ends the mark on one existing Quarry of your choice. When you mark a Quarry, you can mark a second one as part of the same bonus action.
• Twice per turn, when you deal [Weapon Damage] to either of your Quarries you can generate Reaver Echoes as defined by the Reaver's Quarry class feature, instead of once per turn.
• Once per turn, when one or more of your Reaver Echoes detonate on any creature, you regain 1 Discipline for each echo that detonated, up to a maximum of 3. This recovery happens after the detonation resolves and you cannot have more than your maximum number of Discipline as normal.

pickable.editorDescription: Weapon Specialist Katas — techniques and stances that shape your build. Use + Custom for homebrew or DM-approved entries.
pickable.pickerDescription: Katas are learned at levels 2 (initial) and +1 each at 5 / 8 / 11 / 14 / 17 (6 total by cap).

options.name: Disciplined Illusions
options.description: Once/turn on a Genjutsu cast, gain Discipline in place of a Bukijutsu cast: D/C = 1, B/A = 2, S = 3. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Ninshou in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Maneuvers
options.description: Once/turn on a skill action (Trip / Grapple / Shove / Disarm / Maintain Focus / Feint / Demoralize / Read the Enemy): gain 1 Discipline (2 at 11, 3 at 20). Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Strikes in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Ninshou
options.description: Once/turn on a Ninjutsu cast, gain Discipline in place of a Bukijutsu cast: D/C = 1, B/A = 2, S = 3. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Illusions in the same turn.

options.name: Disciplined Strikes
options.description: Once/turn on Attack action: gain 1 Discipline per attack. Multiattack/Light-property attacks don't count. Can't combine with Discipline-from-Chain or Disciplined Maneuvers in the same turn.

options.name: Efficient Bukijutsu
options.description: Once/turn on a Bukijutsu cast, 1 Discipline: reduce its cost by rank (D: 1, C: 2, B: 3, A: 4, S: 5).

options.name: Efficient Chain
options.description: Once/turn on a Chain Bukijutsu: skip chain effects but sustain current chain rank. Gain Discipline equal to the chain rank sustained.

options.name: Defensive Draw
options.description: On Deflection vs a ranged attack / effect from a non-adjacent hostile within 60 ft, 1 additional Discipline: redirect reduced damage to another creature within 30 ft. Ranged weapon attack vs them — on hit, [Weapon Damage] + the reduced damage.

options.name: Duelist Retribution
options.description: Once/turn on a melee hit against you, 2 Discipline: weapon attack vs triggering creature.

options.name: Enduring Fortitude
options.description: Once/turn as Reaction to being hit, 1 Discipline: rank of Mastery to AC vs triggering attack until end of current turn. Alternatively, on crit hit: 3 Discipline to downgrade crit to normal hit.

options.name: Infectious Determination
options.description: Once/turn on a passed save, 2 Discipline: allies who see/hear you gain rank of Mastery to their next save before the end of your or their next turn.

options.name: Persistent Will
options.description: On a concentration check from damage, 2 Discipline: add 1 Flurry Die. Success by 5+ = regain 1 Discipline; by 10+ = next concentration cost on one jutsu = 0.

options.name: Pressure Build
options.description: Each [Weapon Damage] inflicted: +1 DR until your next turn. AoE damage counts per-creature.

options.name: Riposte
options.description: Reaction on a damaging Taijutsu/Bukijutsu cast by a creature within 30 ft, 2 Discipline: cast a 1-Action Bukijutsu — triggers a Clash. Loser's jutsu is Interrupted (no penalties).

options.name: Battle Boast
options.description: Once/turn, in place of an attack or Bukijutsu, 2 Discipline: 20-ft cone WIS save. Fail = focus shifts to you (disadvantage on attacks not targeting you; allies gain Mastery on saves vs non-you targets).

options.name: Calming Strike
options.description: Once/turn on a hit as attack of opportunity, 2 Discipline: affected creature gains 3 ranks Slow until end of current turn.

options.name: Controlled Positioning
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], up to 2 Discipline: push damaged creature 10 ft per Discipline spent (non-vertical).

options.name: Disorienting Strike
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], 3 Discipline: WIS save. Fail = pick one for 1 min (end-of-turn resave): halved speed + can't react to Bukijutsu; -2 AC; or no bonus actions. Immunity per long rest after ending.

options.name: Executioner
options.description:
Once/turn on a Bukijutsu, 4 Discipline (can't gain Discipline from this Bukijutsu): on hit or failed save, pick one (long rest before reuse on same target):
  • Kill — Standard adversary at ≤50 HP instantly drops to 0.
  • Cripple — Standard/Elite at ≤50 HP: STR save or Staggered for 1 min.

options.name: Horrid Aggression
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Discipline: afterimage acceleration. On first Attack-action [Weapon Damage] this turn, STR save — fail = inflict one of Bruised / Weakened / Dazzled / Concussed.

options.name: Muted Strike
options.description: Once/turn on [Weapon Damage], 2 Discipline: CON save — fail = cannot cast D-rank-or-lower jutsu until end of their next turn (C at 9, B at 17).

options.name: Overwhelming Malice
options.description: Action: enter Overflowing Malice stance until next turn. Once/turn in stance, 3 Discipline: Retributive Strike (melee: AoO-on-enter/exit, [Weapon Damage] + 1 Flurry Die, DEX save or prone) OR Pensive Shot (ranged: AoO-at-15-ft, 2 Flurry Die damage, DEX save or restrained).

options.name: Unbound Ferocity
options.description: Bonus Action, 3 Discipline: pick a hostile within 60 ft. Until next turn, advantage on Taijutsu attacks vs them; they gain advantage on attacks vs you.

options.name: Critical Movement
options.description: Once/turn on a crit, 2 Discipline: gain Dash at no additional action cost. Each creature you pass: one weapon attack (once per creature per activation).

options.name: Strider
options.description: 1 Discipline at turn start: ignore natural difficult terrain. 2 Discipline instead: ignore jutsu-imposed difficult terrain. Until next turn.

options.name: Forced Separation
options.description: Once/turn on a melee attack (hit or miss), 1 Discipline: target can't make AoOs against you until end of current turn.

options.name: Elite Focus
options.description:
Bonus Action, 4 Discipline: enter heightened focus (concentration as B-rank jutsu). Pick 2 benefits:
  • +20 ft speed.
  • +2 AC.
  • Advantage on one save of your choice.
  • Extra Action each turn (skill action / Dash / Disengage / single weapon attack).

Maintain concentration at start of each turn by spending 2 Discipline.

options.name: Trapper
options.description: 10 min + 2 Discipline: build a mundane trap (Chapter 5 rules) as if with a Trapper's Kit. Rank ≤ highest jutsu rank you know. With an actual kit, +1 Flurry Die to the crafting check.

options.name: Unfettered Attention
options.description: Bonus Action, 2 Discipline: target creature within 60 ft. For 1 min, they can't benefit from hidden/unseen vs you (within 120 ft).

options.name: Battle Medic
options.description: Once/turn, Bonus Action + 2 Discipline: target a willing creature and roll 2 Flurry Die. 10+ = remove 1 rank of any Physical condition; matching dice = remove all ranks. Usable twice per rest per creature.

options.name: Hidden Hand
options.description:
Rank of Mastery in Stealth. On Hide action with a Thrown weapon / explosive tool, pick one:
  • Tricky Escape — set the tool; 2 Discipline to detonate any time before your next turn without revealing your position.
  • Parting Gift — 2 Discipline: attach tool to Thrown weapon and attack (detonates on hit). Pre-Hide so no position reveal.

masteryGrants.description: Gain a rank of Mastery in one skill or toolkit of your choice.
